Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

19 May
Valuation / positionBofA · Vivek Arya (setup call)▲ bull
PO $320. Buy-side cal-26 EPS $13–15 vs sell-side ~$8.50; ~70% of the sector's economics on ~50%+ of the units. Rubin: the large customer ramp is Q4, not Q3. On the agentic CPU:GPU ratio — "pure BS".
20 May
PRINT — earningsNVDA · Q1 FY27 earnings
Revenue $82B (+85% y/y), DC $75B (+92%). Guide Q2 $91B ±2%. $1T Blackwell+Rubin (cal25–27). Vera Rubin ships Q3. Vera CPU = $200B TAM / ~$20B this year. GM 75%. $80B buyback + dividend. China DC = $0 in the outlook.
21 May
Margin / pricingUBS · Tim Arcuri (recap call)▲ bull
The $20B of CPU is mostly systems/head-nodes + DRAM — "apples-to-apples" on the chip is ~$5B. GM 75% defensible even with HBM4E ~2x (consignment helps). Supply $119B → capacity for ~$130B of revenue exiting the year. DC has outpaced hyperscaler capex for 5 quarters.
22 May
Competition / shareCiti/Jefferies · Blaine (Asia recal)– neutral
NVDA remains a "funding short" — the thesis hasn't broken, but the buy-side sees larger inflections in other names. Rubin capacity ~2M units in Q4. Axion mix ~60% / Graviton ~60% higher than expected = headwind for x86.
27 May
Supply / capacityJPM · Gokul (CoWoS update)▲ bull
NVDA still ~50%+ of TSMC's CoWoS allocation in 2027 — the packaging bottleneck remains favorable to NVDA.
29 May
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur (Semis Spring)▲ bull
Cloud DC capex +80% '26 / +50% '27 (~$575B). Agentic = 10–100x compute per user. Enterprise is NVDA's stronghold (~95% share). ASIC $70–75B at 40–50% CAGR.
2 Jun
Valuation / positionDB · Ross Seymore– neutral
Hold, PT $255 — on valuation. Nothing wrong with the fundamentals; the question is the multiple.
3 Jun
Product / roadmapAlphaSense · 800V expert call– neutral
Kyber rack (2027/28) = the 800V DC inflection. Vera Rubin (mid-2027) still on the 480V shelf. NVDA targets ~7% of electrical efficiency gain — "so the customer buys more chips". Power $ content only accelerates in 2027+.
4 Jun
Competition / shareBofA · Tim Rohde (AVGO callback)▲ bull
If forced to choose, prefers NVDA to AVGO: NVDA's multiple has already collapsed, AVGO "still has room to fall". GPU/ASIC split ~70/30 durable; NVDA gaining share at Anthropic; more degrees of freedom.
5 Jun
Supply / capacityFubon · 2027 outlook▲ bull
NVDA ~50%+ of CoWoS in 2027; total GPU production "could reach 10mn+ in 2027". Vera CPU migrates to ASE FoCoS packaging (outside the CoWoS queue).
9 Jun
Competition / shareSemiAnalysis · DeepSeek V4 (Dylan)◆ mixed
GB300 NVL72 dominates all systems ($0.156/Mtok) — the 72-GPU NVLink domain wins. CUDA Day-0 (SGLang/vLLM), but NVDA's own TensorRT-LLM shipped with bugs. Warning: Huawei CANN now also delivers Day-0.
9 Jun
Demand / capexBriefing · dnystedt (Jensen)▲ bull
Jensen: "next year's 2H much bigger than this one"; memory scarcity should "persist for several years".
11 Jun
Demand / capexVital Knowledge · Crisafulli▼ bear
Low PE = market doubting the sustainability of demand. Oracle F27 capex/sales >100%; hyperscalers don't generate FCF; "picks-and-shovels making a fortune, but low PEs". "Toxic" price war.
13 Jun
Demand / capexJim Chanos▼ bear
Pokes at the cost economics of the GPU datacenter — questions the return on the capital deployed in the buildout.
15 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Tim Arcuri (semis/memory)– neutral
"Over on earnings, under on the multiple" — nothing bad in the fundamentals, but a persistently low multiple. The Anthropic deal helps: it grows a model-builder that reinvests in capex. WFE $250B in 2028.
15 Jun
Valuation / positionBarclays · Tom O'Malley/Rand▼ bear
NVDA the "popular funding short", behind SOX/AMD; "hard to find a high-conviction upside catalyst" near term.
16 Jun
Competition / shareJPM · AI Capex 2.0◆ mixed
NVDA captures $25–35B of revenue/GW (~2x AMD). GPU units 6.2M→8.9M→9.9M. But ASIC share of units 42%(26E)→53%(27E) — ASIC overtakes merchant GPU in 2027; Broadcom AI $60B→$150B+.
16 Jun
Valuation / positionMS · Joe Moore (bus tour)▲ bull
Top Pick, OW PT $288. "Definitively the best product in the market"; best token-per-watt and AI-factory ROI. Trades at ~mid-teens next year vs speculative AI names — a value setup.
18 Jun
Demand / capexCantor · CoreWeave/neocloud▲ bull
NVDA = 40 GW of US chip sales '25–30 (vs ~21 GW of DC built in the prior 25 years). GPU useful life ~7–8 years; revenue per GPU-hour ≈ the current token price (VR ~$10/h).
22 Jun
Supply / capacityMS · Joe Moore (memory+semicap)◆ mixed
NVDA cutting LPDDR5 ~by half in the Vera Rubin racks — not bad news, it's a tell of how acute the scarcity is ("everything getting nerfed"). DRAM = the #1 bottleneck for AI builds, worse than CPU.
22 Jun
Product / roadmapJefferies Asia · Conor O'Mara▼ bear
The "Kyber" rack (orthogonal backplane) slips from Rubin Ultra 2027 to ≥2028; Rubin Ultra 2027 stays on Oberon/NVL72. AI PCB/CCL impact ~5%/8% in 2027 (up to ~11%/16% in 2028). Benefits copper cables.
23 Jun
Margin / pricingRedburn · Conor O'Mara (Asia trip)▼ bear
NVDA's HBM $17–18 → $30–32/GB (+70–80%). GPU+HBM ~$4.3M/rack; total memory bill ~$200B in 2027. Risk: if hyperscalers force HBM consignment, it compresses revenue/ASP/EPS — "the stock that looks cheap isn't that cheap".
23 Jun
Margin / pricingStoneX/TrendForce · Avril◆ mixed
NVDA gets the LOWEST HBM price (AMD, Broadcom, AWS pay more). HBM4 needs ~3x for ~$5/Gb in 2027. LPDDR5 = the tightest-supply product of 2027 (Vera/next-gen CPU vs smartphones).

AVGOBroadcom Inc.richQ2 FY26 · Apr 30 → Jun 23 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23

events
7
bull reads
5
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

30 Apr
Margin / pricingB-side TMT · Ken/Andrew▲ bull
Recap of hyperscaler callbacks: Google books merchant TPU sales to Anthropic on a gross basis — Broadcom builds and sells the TPU to Google at ~$21k/chip; Google adds a license fee of ~10-15% (~$2-3k/chip) and resells. Read-through: AVGO ASP per TPU to Google ~$21k all-in; ~$21B of Anthropic orders ($10B+$11B) ≈ ~1 GW.
22 May
Supply / capacityCiti/Jefferies · Blaine▼ bear
Asia recal call: supply-chain partners still point to ~3M TPU units in 2025 (vs buy-side ~4M), with a gap between hope and reality. Anthropic deal: ~$10B of racks (Jul-Q) now tracking ~$2-3B, served internally on GCP — higher margin (chips not racks) but a net dollar cut; AVGO became a 'funding short'.
29 May
Product / roadmapJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
Semis Spring Series: Broadcom's TPU order book ≈ 6.5M units for next year (up from ~5.5-6M); ~$20B AI ASIC+networking in 2025 → ~$60B+ in 2026, backlog >$150B for next year. In volume on Ironwood/V7 (3nm); won V8i 'Sunfish' (3nm) and the 2nm 'Blade Runner' (cal-28). Tomahawk 6 (102T) basically sold out for next year, qualifying Samsung for more 3nm.
3 Jun
PRINT — earningsAVGO · Q2 FY26 earnings
Q2 FY26: rev $22.2B (+48% y/y), EPS $2.44 vs Street $22.1B/$2.39 — a beat vs consensus but below buyside bogeys. GM 77.1%, record op margin 67.3%, FCF $10.3B. AI semis $10.8B (+143% y/y); AI bookings >$30B vs $10.8B shipped, visibility through 2028.
4 Jun
Guidance / numbersUBS · Tim Rohde▲ bull
AVGO networking callback: 'Just because he didn't raise the number doesn't mean the number didn't go up' — AVGO told UBS FY27 AI could go to $110-120B but won't ratchet every quarter. Bookings 3x shipments in the quarter. FY28 steps up materially more than FY27-vs-FY26. GM held ~74% for 3-4 quarters; the constraint is backend/substrate (Singapore pulled Dec→Aug), not HBM.
4 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Tim Rohde◆ mixed
Revenue-per-GW message walked down: >$20 → $20 → '$10-20, varies by customer'. OpenAI compute demand ≈ Anthropic, but revenue visibility lower; OpenAI's XPU tapes out at year-end, ships 2H FY27, big ramp FY28. Anthropic 5GW is all Broadcom (no MediaTek).
5 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
Post-AVGO callback: FY28 is the story — ~20-22GW deploy in FY28 → $250-300B AI rev, ~$35-38 EPS vs cons ~$25. TPU V8i 'Sunfish' = ~70% of Google's FY27 TPU demand, ~6.5M units ≈ ~$100B on its own; +~$45B networking.
5 Jun
Supply / capacityFubon/Jefferies▼ bear
Semi 2027 outlook: cuts AVGO's TSMC CoWoS share to 24-25% in 2027 (≈459.6k of ~1.89M wafers; #2 behind NVDA 50%) on limited volume from the 2nd/3rd ASIC projects (OpenAI/Meta — MP slips to 2H27). V9 NOT cancelled but pushed (V8i MP late 3Q26, V9 MP late 3Q27); total AVGO TPU ~6.2M units in 2027. Wins AWS T4 design service.
6 Jun
Guidance / numbersBriefing Capstone▼ bear
Q3 FY26 guide: total rev $29.4B (+84% y/y, above Street $28.6B); AI semis $16.0B (buyside ~$18.5B — the clear miss); GM down ~310bps q/q to ~74% on ASIC mix. FY26 AI semis $56B (~+180%). FY27 AI reiterated '>$100B' with no raise vs Street ~$116B — that omission, not the print, drove the -12.6% reaction.
15 Jun
Competition / shareRedburn · Conor O'Mara▼ bear
Asia Trip Takeaways: the key reason Google is migrating TPU share to MediaTek is that Broadcom charges HBM in the BOM and MediaTek doesn't — Google cuts a 'gigantic part of the BOM'. Google share going 6% (2026) → 30% (2028) [MediaTek gain], with 400G switch timing (V9 vs V10) the swing factor; checks lean toward Google adopting 400G only on V10.
15 Jun
Product / roadmapRedburn▲ bull
Recent CPO reliability improvements 'tip the balance' toward broader adoption in scale-up networking by 2028/29, enabled by optical-engine hybrid-bonding (TSMC COUPE/SoIC); models ~50 cumulative BESI bonders for CPO by 2030. Read-through: AVGO's scale-up CPO push is a 2028+ ramp gated by equipment.
16 Jun
Capital / M&AJPM · AI Capex 2.0– neutral
Structure of the AI XPV JV (Broadcom/Apollo/Blackstone SPV for Google TPUs for Anthropic): $800M equity Apollo/Atlas + $6B A1 Notes (~T+100bp) + $24B A2 Notes (~5.75%) + $4.5B Series B (~8.5%). A1/A2 tranches carry an AVGO residual-value guarantee (mid-BBB); backstop with $29B max exposure. All-in pre-tax cost in the low-6% range.
16 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · AI Capex 2.0▲ bull
Models AVGO at $150B+ of AI revenue in 2027 (ASIC/XPU + AI-networking), >2x the ~$60B of 2026E, viewing the disclosed >$100B 2027 backlog as a 'conservative message'. ASICs ~42% of DC AI-chip units in 2026 → ~53% in 2027, growing ~3x faster than merchant GPUs.
17 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
FY28 bull case: ~20-22GW deploy in FY28 → $250-300B AI rev, ~$35-38 EPS vs cons ~$25. Google's 5-year LTA (through 2031, signed in April) carries growing guaranteed annual revenues and covers the next 4 TPU generations (V8-V11); V9 2nm 'no delay', ramp C28. AVGO more exposed to frontier-lab risk (OpenAI/Anthropic) than NVDA.
23 Jun
Competition / shareStoneX/TrendForce · Avril▼ bear
Memory expert call: Google previously relied entirely on Broadcom for HBM procurement; MediaTek (TPU V8/V9) is now increasingly handling Google's HBM directly and is in talks with Samsung — confirming HBM procurement leaking away from Broadcom.

Gross margin / mixsemi GM ~67%, AI dilutiveconsol GM 79.1%; AI dilutivesemi GM ~69%; XPU mix dragsGM 77.9%; systems to dilute 2H26GM 77%, 'no rack impact', stableGM 77.1%, Q3 ~74% on AI mix; op-lev holds

AMDAdvanced Micro Devices, Inc.richQ1 FY26 · May 06 → Jun 23 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
12
events
8
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

6 May
PRINT — earningsAMD · Q1 FY26 earnings
Q1 FY26: rev $10.3B (+38% y/y, beat at the top of guide), EPS $1.37 (+43% y/y), non-GAAP GM 55% (+170bps y/y), op income $2.5B (25% OM), record FCF $2.6B. Record Data Center $5.8B (+57% y/y); Server CPU 4th consecutive record, >50% y/y. Q2 guide ~$11.2B ±$300M (+46% y/y), GM ~56%, Server CPU guided >70% y/y.
6 May
Demand / capexAMD callback▲ bull
Server CPU >70% y/y in Q2, sustainable through 2H, mostly unit-driven (~2/3 units, 1/3 ASP) at 60-65%+ GM vs lower-margin GPU+HBM. Each GW of deal ≈ $15-20B; OpenAI + Meta ≈ 2GW of the ~3GW in play for 2027. AMD confirms ~$15B+ per GW and 'multiple GW+/year customers'. The final OpenAI tranche only vests at $600/share; ~26M shares per GW of dilution via warrant.
6 May
Valuation / positionBofA · Vivek Arya▲ bull
Post-Q1 recap: consensus moving to ~$7.25 EPS FY26 (from ~$6.80), ~$12+ FY27, ~$15 FY28; AMD historically trades 35-40x next-year in growth → target $450-500. AMD remains under-owned — only ~1-in-3 large-cap funds hold it at ~0.41x relative weight (~36-60% underweight) vs NVDA ~78% ownership — the positioning gap is itself the bull catalyst.
6 May
Valuation / positionGS▲ bull
GS upgraded to Buy PT $410; part of the broad sell-side reset post-Q1 on the agentic CPU thesis (BofA Buy $450, MS OW $410, UBS Buy $300).
22 May
Supply / capacityCiti · Blayne Curtis▲ bull
Asia recap: MI450 'on track' for first revenue in Q4; Curtis saw 2 of the 3 MI450 ODMs (end-customers Meta and Microsoft) and flagged Inventec building MI450 for Microsoft as a new data point.
13 Jun
Valuation / positionCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Citi upgraded to Buy, PT $575, ~+12-13% above the Street CY26-28E, SOTP-based — sees 2027 GPU revenue dispersion of $30-50bn as ~3GW come into play.
15 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Harlan Sur▼ bear
JPM Neutral PT $385 vs stock ~$511 — explicitly cautious on valuation (~45x NTM); GPU still 'show-me'.
15 Jun
Competition / shareUBS · Arcuri▲ bull
Asked AMD-vs-Intel-vs-ARM, Arcuri 'would rather be in AMD' — core count is decisive in an agentic world (agents spawning agents), and AMD is 'well ahead of Intel on core count'; much less optimistic on Intel's product roadmap. AMD server units +50%.
16 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · AI Capex 2.0▲ bull
AMD DC capex at $35-40B per GW, of which AMD revenue is $15-20B/GW ($14-18B compute, ~$2B networking); raises GPU units to 0.7M (2025) → 0.6M (2026E) → 1.0M (2027E) as MI450 ramps; OpenAI deal (6 GW MI450, multi-generation) sees first ~1 GW deploy 2H26.
16 Jun
Product / roadmapMS · Joe Moore◆ mixed
Bus tour: 'very enthused' on Venice (2nm, 2H ramp), sees agentic CPU as incremental net-new demand, not a GPU ratio, and flags x86 security advantage for enterprise agents over Arm. On GPU 'still show-me' with the stock at ~45x next year.
17 Jun
Valuation / positionBernstein · Rasgon▲ bull
Bernstein Outperform PT $600.
23 Jun
Supply / capacityRedburn · Conor O'Mara◆ mixed
Asia Trip Takeaways: AMD constrained on TSMC 3nm + two substrate bottlenecks — ABF (Kinsus ~40% share, growing only ~10%) and CCL (iTeq ~60% share at ~75% util on a T-glass shortage, recovering to 90-95% in Q4). Trade: if long AMD, trim ~20% into the print since AMD may guide light on the constraints, then buy the weakness. Server CPU pricing +15-20% again in H2.
23 Jun
Product / roadmapRedburn · Conor O'Mara◆ mixed
MI450/Helios: MI450 chip in production on track; rack forecast steady at ~30,000 units for next year (mostly Meta and OpenAI), via AVC (liquid cooling)/Delta (power) checks. ODMs split on Helios rack assembly timing — one sees a delay to Q1 2026, another only a minor slip to late Q4 2025; the swing is whether the rack (not the chip) can be built. Vs NVDA's ~70-80K racks, 30K is a 'decent number'.

TSMTaiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing CompanymediumQ1 FY26 · Apr 16 → Jun 23 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
7
events
6
bull reads
0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

16 Apr
PRINT — earningsTSMC · Q1 FY26 earnings (20-F filed)
Q1 FY26: rev US$35.9B (+6.4% q/q, above the top of guide), GM 66.2% (+390bp q/q), OpM 58.1%, EPS NT$22.08, ROE 40.5%. HPC +20% q/q to 61% of revenue. FY26 rev guided 'above 30%' in USD (from 'close to 30%'); capex revised to the top of US$52-56B. N3 = 25% of wafer revenue, N5 36%, N7 13%.
18 May
Valuation / positionBernstein · Mark Li▲ bull
Bernstein OP, PT $430 vs ~$351, calling TSM the 'most reliable compounder in AI' — 28% EPS CAGR through '28, +50% EPS this year, '27 capex $68B, trading at ~20% discount vs SOX.
21 May
Demand / capexUBS · Sunny Lin▲ bull
Quantifying the server CPU opportunity: sizes the server-CPU market at ~$170bn by 2030 (units 23m→63m, 30% CAGR); server CPUs need ~200kwpm of leading-edge by 2030, of which TSMC serves 122kwpm — an opportunity of ~$44bn of TSMC revenue (11% of sales) in 2030, from $3.7bn/3% in 2025. Raises sales 3-7%/EPS 4-9% in 2027-30. Buy, PT NT$3,000.
27 May
Supply / capacityJPM · Gokul▲ bull
CoWoS update: TSMC turning aggressive on CoWoS 2027, raising capacity to 115k/175k/220k WPM by end-26/27/28; total CoWoS consumption +86% this year / +30% next. N3 hot-run/super-hot-run pricing rising further in Q3, regular N3 hikes ~10% and N2 ~5-6% in Jan-27. EMIB vs CoWoS-L decision on Google TPU V9 expected late-2026/early-2027.
5 Jun
Margin / pricingFubon▲ bull
2027 semi outlook: supply-demand gap widens to 15-20% in 2027, N2/N3 util 110-120%, super-hot-run at a 15-20% premium; 2027 wafer-price hikes start at 10% including Apple (vs only +1-2% Apple in 2026) — lifting blended ASP ≥10% and GM into the 68-70% range. FY27 rev NT$7,057bn (+37%, Street-high). Raises TP to NT$3,500. Arizona P1 GM already ~50% after 2 years.
23 Jun
Margin / pricingRedburn · Conor O'Mara▲ bull
Asia Trip Takeaways: 3nm 'tight and nobody cutting anything'. 2026 hikes by customer: AI/HPC ~+10%, consumer ~+5%, Apple low-single-digit → blended ASP ex-2nm only +3-4%. 2027 +≥10% across ALL customers incl. Apple, with GM potentially reaching high-60s to 70%. Supply/demand gap widens to 15-20% in 2027. More positive tone on H2'26 GM (flattish vs guidance of a decline).
23 Jun
Supply / capacityRedburn · Conor O'Mara◆ mixed
TSMC already at its prior peak of ~8M wafers/month with limited new capacity, so the agentic CPU surge could push the system into a broad wafer shortage; Samsung acknowledged the same risk. EUV constrains capex upside: 40-45 EUV units already booked for 2027 (memory makers competing for DRAM EUV), capping 2027 capex at ~$70-75B vs others' $80-85B.
23 Jun
Supply / capacityMS · Charlie Chan▲ bull
Preliminary 2027 CoWoS allocation: raises 2027 CoWoS capacity assumption to 200kwpm by year-end (from 170k) = ~60% Y/Y AI-XPU growth. NVDA CoWoS-L ~910k (+40% Y/Y); Google TPU #2 via MediaTek 180k + Broadcom 365k; CPU becomes a material consumer of 2.5D — NVDA Vera 5.75mn / AMD Venice 6.75mn units in 2027 (vs ~1.25mn '26). Reiterates OW TSMC.

ASMLASML Holding N.V.mediumQ1 2026 · Apr 15 → Jun 23 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
8
events
5
bull reads
0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

15 Apr
PRINT — earningsASML · Q1 2026 earnings
Q1 2026: net sales €8.8B (in guide); system sales €6.3B of which EUV >€4.1B (incl. 2 High-NA) and non-EUV >€2.1B; mix ~50/50 Logic 49%/Memory 51%. IBM €2.5B (above guide). GM 53.0% (top end). Net income €2.8B, margin 31.4%, EPS €7.15. FY26 raised/narrowed to €36-40B, GM 51-53%, H2-weighted. Low-NA EUV to at least 80 systems in 2027 (from 44 in 2025).
15 Apr
Demand / capexBofA callback▲ bull
DRAM is the new leg — the market shifted materially vs CMD'24, EUV-layer adoption rising 1b (~3-4) → 1c (~6-7) → 1d (~10 by ~2028); BofA's base moves ASML to Top Pick Dec'25. ASP/GM ladder locked in: D→E (~55% GM)→F (high-50s/low-60s, immersion-like), wafer capacity >2x even with EUV units ~2x. FY26 guide-raise driven by immersion+IBM, 'nothing to do with EUV'.
16 Apr
Demand / capexBofA · Didier◆ mixed
AI demand structurally outpaces supply through 2027+; lithography is 'clearly the binding constraint'. Near-term risk to the reaction: the guide-raise was driven by immersion+IBM, which does not by itself signal incremental EUV strength.
21 May
Product / roadmapBofA · Sam Sivakumar (litho expert)▲ bull
High-NA primer: one High-NA exposure replaces low-NA multi-patterning, cutting steps from 'several dozen to sub-10' (~75% fewer) — a 'no-brainer' case. Mid-field stitching 'is not as serious as previously thought'. DRAM 'eminently suitable' (likely early-adopter). TSMC a deliberate slow-adopter, Intel pushing harder. Quoted throughputs (170/230/250 wph) are 'demonstration numbers'. Resist transition CAR→MOR below ~12-13nm.
3 Jun
Supply / capacityJPM · Deshpande▲ bull
'The Street is Behind the Curve': the message shifted from '≥80 units' to capacity — the 90-tool capacity 'is not the ceiling'; ASML can expand output without new clean rooms (cycle ~20→14 weeks by 2H27, free up R&D bays, use High-NA capacity for Low-NA, fast shipments). JPM thinks ASML can do 110 Low-NA EUV or more without new buildings. PT €1,900, FY27/28 rev €55.4B/€63.8B.
15 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Arcuri▲ bull
WFE to ~$250B in CY28, possibly in the $300s in CY29 (A-rated semicaps would gain ~$50/share). ASML ~110 EUV 'slots' of physical capacity ≈ ~$225B WFE before needing to add floor space. On DRAM-dollar leverage: ASML ~30% and AMAT ~25% of DRAM WFE dollars (>50% combined) — the two most levered to DRAM.
17 Jun
Valuation / positionRothschild/Redburn▲ bull
Raised to €1,900 (from €1,570) Buy, ASML 'remains our top pick among semicaps' post Silicon Valley bus tour — raised FY27-30 EPS +9-12%, sitting 14% ahead of consensus; $68bn lithography market and $54bn of ASML system sales in 2030 at a stable 91% share. At peer multiples ASML would be €2,170 vs PT €1,900.
22 Jun
Valuation / positionAlphaSense Deep Research (Bernstein within)◆ mixed
ASML primer: Bernstein Outperform (May-21), P/E FY25A/26E/27E = 51.2x/38.7x/26.9x — multiple compression brought FY27E to 26.9x, 'directly aligned with AMAT' and below KLAC/LRCX; premium over SPE median compressed from 1.7x historical to ~1.1-1.2x. PT spread: Bernstein €1,700 / Kepler €1,460 / ING €1,700 / Santander Underperform €980. China normalizing to ~20% of 2026 sales.
23 Jun
Supply / capacityRedburn · Conor O'Mara◆ mixed
Asia Trip Takeaways: TSMC already booking 40-45 EUV units for 2027 and finding it 'hard to get more' as memory makers compete for the same tools — read-through of EUV sold-out/capacity-allocated in 2027. But caution: buy-side expectations for ASML have already risen a lot ('a lot is already priced in') after the narrative turned to 'we can do at least 100 EUV machines'.

MUMicron Technologyrichintra-quarter · May 15 → Jun 23 2026 (F3Q26 reports Jun 24) · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
14
events
11
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

18 Mar
PRINT — earningsMU · Q2 FY26 earnings
Q2 FY26 (blowout): rev $23.9B (+75% q/q, +196% y/y, 4th consecutive record). DRAM $18.8B (+74% q/q), price +'mid-sixties %' seq; NAND $5.0B (+82% q/q), price +'high-seventies %'. Consolidated GM 75% (+18pts q/q), op margin 69%, non-GAAP EPS $12.20. Record FCF $6.9B. Q3 guide (Jun-24): rev $33.5B ±$750M, GM ~81%, EPS $19.15. Supply is the binding constraint — filling only 50% to 2/3 of key-customer demand.
15 May
Demand / capexUBS · Arcuri (Sathya Kumar)▲ bull
Memory repositioned 'from system component to strategic asset' for AI — Micron supplies the entire hierarchy that agentic inference consumes: HBM next to the accelerator, LPDDR/DDR5/SoCAM on the CPU, and high-capacity QLC/TLC SSDs for the growing KV-cache/context tier.
19 May
Valuation / positionCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Citi Buy, PT $840 — re-rate to 8x C27 EPS from 5x.
22 May
Guidance / numbersJPM (TMC conf)▲ bull
Conf feedback: MU emphasized tightness 'well beyond CY26', undersupply extended into CY27, secured its first 5-year DRAM SCA with multiple additional customers in active negotiation (incl NAND SCAs); HBM4 ramping ~2x faster than the HBM3E ramp; 1γ DRAM and G9 NAND on track to be the majority of bit mix by mid-2026. JPM OW.
26 May
Valuation / positionUBS · Arcuri (briefing)▲ bull
Marquee bull: UBS Arcuri Buy, PT $1,625 (raised from $535) on a 'path to >$100 EPS', LTAs locking up ~30% of the industry's DDR bits and ~$400bn of FCF C27-29E. UBS's own desk called the note 'the most controversial from UBS recently'.
28 May
Margin / pricingJPM · Gokul/Memory▲ bull
Models blended HBM ASP +~30% for 2026 (~10% like-for-like + HBM4 mix) — but with the HBM4 ramp pushed to 2H, more of the ASP effect spills into 2027; HBM price negotiations only begin in July after the June quarter. Incremental CPU upside: NVDA Vera standalone ~500k units this year, ~3M next, could lift total server DRAM demand >15-20%. EUV as the 2028 supply governor.
4 Jun
Valuation / positionMS▲ bull
MS reiterates bull case PT $1,050.
8 Jun
Valuation / positionGS · Junie Yoon▲ bull
Central argument: cycle longevity is underappreciated — Hynix/Samsung (and by read-across MU) at 5-6x earnings says the market doesn't believe in the durability; GS's 9x target is 'conservative' vs semis peers at high-teens-20x. Downside-protection math: even a bear case of DRAM -30% in 2027 and -30% in 2028 (or -50% in 2028) still leaves DRAM GM ~40%.
10 Jun
Valuation / positionEquity call · ex-VP Procurement (Micron)▼ bear
Sharpest bear voice: the market is pricing peak earnings as a sustainable level — 'a highly catastrophic assumption'. HBM differentiation is temporary (commoditizes as 2nd/3rd vendors qualify); MU's cost structure is worse than Samsung, ~equal to SK Hynix; 'your $900 stock will go back to $50' when new fabs bring disruptive capacity ~1 year out, possible normalization 'this calendar year or early next'.
12 Jun
Guidance / numbersBriefing Capstone– neutral
F3Q26 setup (print Jun-24): Street rev $34.7B vs guide $33.5B; FY27/28 EPS pegged $111.3/$115.7. Watch: GM trajectory past 81%, HBM4 ramp commentary, DDR-vs-HBM margin crossover, and any SCA disclosure.
15 Jun
Margin / pricingUBS · Arcuri (call)▲ bull
Incremental tell into the June print: the market is fixated on DDR but HBM pricing is 'what is really surprising to the upside'; LTAs (not pricing) are the re-rate catalyst. MU told him after the PT raise: 'we think the stock should trade at a higher multiple and you finally opened that debate'. DDR GM (ex-HBM) likely exceeds HBM GM for the first time in early C2026.
17 Jun
Demand / capexDB · Melissa Weathers▲ bull
DB Buy, raised PT: 'DRAM tightness extends to 2028+; agentic AI drives memory intensity'.
22 Jun
Demand / capexMS · Joe Moore▲ bull
Fundamentals '10/10 for DRAM, maybe 8/10 for NAND' and more bullish on Micron than SanDisk — DRAM is the primary AI bottleneck (NVDA halved LPDDR5 content on Vera Rubin for lack of memory; servers shipping 'significantly nerfed' on DRAM). Sees MU as 'a much cheaper stock on 2028 numbers than SanDisk'. Tim Cook (WSJ) called memory pricing a '100-year flood'.
23 Jun
Supply / capacityStoneX/TrendForce · Avril◆ mixed
Memory expert call: Micron is the 'only company with extreme difficulty adding wafer capacity in 2026', ~flat at ~365K wafers/month (vs Samsung ~740K, SK Hynix ~610K). 27/28 growth comes from Idaho 1&2 + the acquired PSMC Taiwan fab (~40K), reaching ~440K by end-2027; grows faster than Korean peers in 2028 (+21% YoY). HBM cap end-2026 ~100K, risk of dropping to ~80K if HBM4 qualification issues persist. Anthropic deal = server DRAM+eSSD (2027) + HBM (2028).
23 Jun
Margin / pricingStoneX/TrendForce · Avril▲ bull
Flags Micron as a likely price outlier in the QoQ negotiations — capacity-constrained, it pushes for the highest ASPs, 'and if Micron succeeds, the two Koreans follow' on share competition. Her DRAM base-case is the most conservative in the market: Q3 +8-13% / Q4 +3-8% (gut-feel upside Q3 >+20% / Q4 +10-15%) — vs Redburn checks of 30-40% QoQ in Q3 and Q4.

2026-06-23HBM end-2026 cap ~100K wafers, risk of drop to ~80K if HBM4 qual issues persist; HBM 2027 revised down 60→50bn Gb-eq · StoneX/TrendForce + Redburn

MRVLMarvell Technology, Inc.mediumQ1 FY27 · May 19 → Jun 23 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
9
events
4
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

19 May
Competition / shareSusquehanna▼ bear
Networking call: pushback on Marvell's 1.6T DSP performance; NVDA is not sourcing MRVL for 1.6T (Broadcom + captive only), and 'will look very different from the ~80% share Marvell had at 800G' — Broadcom gains share as the market broadens beyond Google. MRVL still holds the Google 1.6T DSP design and ~80% of the prior-gen 800G DSP market.
22 May
Demand / capexCiti/Jefferies · Blaine◆ mixed
Asia recal: Trainium server production ~flat in Q2 on the Trn2→Trn3 transition; Blaine is below the buy-side enthusiasm of ~3M units '25/~4M '26, and warns that MRVL 'doesn't get paid on all units' in the Amazon deal — may not capture all the volume upside. Sees the FQ2 beat-and-raise as 'a couple hundred million', optics the source of strength (+20%+), ASIC less so.
27 May
PRINT — earningsMRVL · Q1 FY27 earnings
Q1 FY27 (quarter ended May-2): rev $2.418B (+9% q/q, +28% y/y); data center $1.8B = 76% of total (+27% y/y). Non-GAAP EPS $0.80 (+29% y/y), ~$0.05 above the Street ~$0.75; non-GAAP GM 58.9%. New 'line of sight to >$10B of annual custom silicon revenue for FY29'. Interconnect raised to >70% y/y in FY27; Celestial AI closed in the quarter. Guide: Q2 ~$2.7B (+35% y/y); FY27 ~$11.5B (~+40%); FY28 raised to ~$16.5B (~+45%).
29 May
Product / roadmapJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
Semis Spring Series: MRVL ships ~$9B AI ASIC+networking in 2025 → ~$15B in 2026. Trainium 3 ships this quarter with a strong 2H ramp; Trainium 4 (2nm) ramps 2H next year, adding CPO content (Celestial), Elink IO chiplets and a UALink scale-up switch. Microsoft Maia 3nm on track 'with no issues' + Maia 2nm cal-28. New: a multi-billion Google SmartNIC/DPU program + a Google CXL-controller ASIC ramping 2H. Optics 'on fire', ~65-70% of the optical-DSP market.
3 Jun
Product / roadmapIrrational Analysis▲ bull
Under-appreciated IP/margin: (1) dense custom SRAM runs at 500mV (450mV pushed) vs a typical floor of ~630mV — 'better than most/all internal SRAM IP at NVDA/AMD/Broadcom'. (2) Structera CXL controller: the DRAM shortage flipped it 'from useless junk to extremely valuable' — likely Marvell's highest-GM product (~85%). (3) NVDA's $2B stake read as strategic (NVLink-Fusion lock-in, blocking AMD switching).
5 Jun
Competition / shareJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
Post-AVGO: MRVL #2 pick in semis; defends the Trainium and Maia roadmaps as not-lost; possible Google LPU win. MRVL 'holds the Trainium and Maia XPU roadmaps that Broadcom doesn't have', plus CXL and Ethernet scale-up.
12 Jun
Competition / shareBofA · Tal (Ciena call)◆ mixed
Ciena's CEO names Marvell (alongside Cisco) as the competitor that 'worries' him as coherent/co-packaged optics migrate from DCI to campus and into the datacenter — MRVL 'very strong in IMDD, recognizing the tech shift, working on co-light, very good at high-volume low-cost implementation'. Two-sided read: validates MRVL's optical reach but the merchant CPO arena is contested.
17 Jun
Competition / shareJPM · Harlan Sur◆ mixed
Call note: MRVL tied to the Trainium 3 program; flags that a US sell-side competitor downgraded MRVL on 1.6T DSP share concerns. Reaffirms MRVL #2 pick, Trainium/Maia roadmaps defended; reports an AVGO 'dig' ('won't ratchet guidance every quarter — a dig at maybe Marvell'). Of 4 T4 variants, MRVL is on the Ethernet-scale-up and Celestial/NVLink-Fusion paths.
17 Jun
Supply / capacityAlphaSense expert alert– neutral
Marvell AVP on yield optimization for advanced nodes (early challenges, improving over time).
18 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM Index Research · M. Moon▲ bull
MRVL added to the S&P 500 in the June rebalance ($88bn notional, 2nd-largest quarterly) — passive-demand tailwind.

events

5

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

15 Apr
Supply / capacityBofA · ASML callback▼ bear
Notable negative: despite the server-CPU shortage, Intel's CFO 'shot down' the idea of ordering more EUV tools, saying it has sufficient capacity; BofA models Intel taking ~1 additional High-NA tool in 2026 and ~1 in 2027, then 3 systems for the 14A ramp in 2029 (could be zero).
23 Apr
PRINT — earningsIntel · earnings Q1 FY26
Revenue $13.6B, +7% y/y, beating the guide midpoint by $1.4B (sixth straight beat); non-GAAP GM 41% (~650bps above guide); non-GAAP EPS $0.29. But GAAP showed an operating loss of $(3.1B) and EPS $(0.73) on a $4.07B restructuring charge. Q2 FY26 guidance: revenue $13.8–14.8B (+2-9% q/q).
24 Apr
Valuation / positionBofA · Vivek Arya▼ bear
Laid out the $56 PT framework: Intel Products at ~20x 2028E EPS + Intel Foundry external book at ~10x P/S on $3B (2027)/$6B (2028) of external foundry revenue. Earnings build ~$1.00 (2026)/$1.50 (2027)/$2+ (2028); $4 bull case in 2028 maps to ~$80. SOTP, not a multiple. BofA maintains Underperform.
12 May
Product / roadmapStoneX · equity call (IR/management)▲ bull
18A with yields ~2-3 months ahead of internal benchmarks; 14A with 'maturity, yield and performance outpacing 18A' at the equivalent point, with external customers in the definition phase. EMIB/EMIB-T packaging re-rated from 'hundreds of millions' to 'billions/year' per engagement, ramp visible in 2027. Capex flat y/y but equipment +25% y/y.
21 May
Product / roadmapBofA · litho expert (Sam Sivakumar, ex-Intel)▲ bull
Intel is the front-runner in High-NA EUV (vs TSMC's deliberately slow adoption); High-NA 'done right' could be a differentiator/leapfrog: ~75% fewer process steps, relaxed design rules, lower parasitic capacitance. The feared field-stitching problem proved 'not that serious'; the High-NA line validated as 'healthy and ready' before the expert's retirement in Sep/2025.
21 May
Competition / shareUBS · server-CPU TAM (Sunny Lin)▼ bear
UBS models Intel's server-CPU unit share falling from 60% (2025) to 29% (2030), with ARM rising 16%→42% and AMD 24%→29% — the same structural erosion BofA flagged on a value basis.
22 May
Product / roadmapJPM · Harlan Sur (conf feedback)▲ bull
'Extremely impressed' with Lip-Bu Tan's first event for Wall Street: 18A yields improving ~6-7 percentage points per month; 'multiple engagements on 14A, some on 18A-P', with commitments locked only in advanced packaging; possible 14A customer announcements at the 2H-2026 analyst day. Caution: the market is not pricing execution risk, 14A HVM only ~CY29.
1 Jun
Margin / pricingJefferies · Brent Thill▼ bear
To model GM use ~40-60% incremental drop-through; applied literally, exit-2026 GM lands in the low-40s; mid-40s is the bar to 'really believe' in the turnaround. On 18A, the margin drag stays neutral to the product business by year-end but remains a manufacturing headwind — pushed back on the '18A accretive by year-end' read. IFS break-even at exit-2027, pushed out with each new customer.
5 Jun
Supply / capacityFubon · 2027 Semiconductor Outlook▼ bear
EMIB production yield is only ~60-70% vs TSMC's CoWoS 95%+, and EMIB-T is 'not yet ready to enter mass production', so MediaTek/Google's priority remains TSMC CoWoS-L despite interest in EMIB-T.
11 Jun
Capital / M&ASemiAnalysis · Patel/Kundojjala◆ mixed
Frames Q1-26 as an inflection: Intel raised its FY26 capex guide to flat y/y (the first guide-up of the saga) and bought back Apollo's 49% of Fab 34 for $14.2B (closed Apr-8-2026). With ~$51.5B of debt post-bridge, SemiAnalysis argues equity is now the cheapest capital and Intel should raise ~$40B to fund the Terafab ramp and the N3 overflow opportunity; estimates a $20-35B financing hole.
15 Jun
Capital / M&AUBS · Timothy Arcuri◆ mixed
Terafab requesting ~$5B of WFE for a desired pilot line within 12 months; Terafab WFE could reach high-single-digit / up to ~$10B by 2028. In the CPU debate Arcuri prefers AMD/ARM to Intel — skeptical on the product roadmap — though 'foundry news flow remains very good' (4 potential foundry deals this fall); does not think Intel needs a large capital raise.
15 Jun
Product / roadmapMorgan Stanley · T. Wigg (bus tour)▲ bull
Bullish on the MS bus tour takeaways (INTC/MRVL/ALAB), citing the CEO's commitment to foundry and a positive view on microprocessors; INTC included among the bullish names of the West Coast Semis bus tour.
17 Jun
Valuation / positionBernstein · Stacy Rasgon (briefing)▲ bull
Raised the PT to $100 (from $65), Market-Perform, on 18A-P process milestones + the read-through on CPU TAM and IFS packaging optionality. INTC +2.6% pre-market on Jun-17 on the 18A-P update.
18 Jun
Valuation / positionBofA TMT sales / GS TMT (briefing)▲ bull
INTC +8-9% on the chip deal with Apple — a sentiment inflection.
23 Jun
Competition / shareRedburn · Conor O'Mara (Asia Trip)▲ bull
Contrarian read from an expert at one of the largest ODMs (a CPU buyer): Intel's x86 server share at 50% (2024)→55% (2025)→~60% (2026). Mechanism: Intel moved laptop/desktop designs to 18A, lifting 18A utilization/yields and freeing capacity on existing nodes for Xeon — explaining the surprise margin beat; Intel 'appears less capacity-constrained than AMD'. Both are expected to raise server-CPU prices +15-20% in H2.

Forward commentary & how it evolved · anchored on the 2 most recent references

IFXInfineon Technologies AGmediumQ2 FY26 · May 06 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
6
events
4

0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

6 May
PRINT — earningsInfineon · earnings Q2 FY26
Revenue EUR 3.812bn (+6% y/y, +4% q/q; ~+14% FX-adjusted); segment result EUR 653m, margin 17.1% (from 17.9% in Q1). Backlog ~EUR 25bn (+EUR 4bn q/q, +25% y/y) — the highlight. FY26 guidance raised to >EUR 16bn revenue (~+10%) and ~20% segment margin. AI-power business on allocation; reconfirmed EUR 1.5bn AI-power revenue FY26 → EUR 2.5bn FY27.
26 May
Competition / shareBofA · Vivek Arya (briefing)◆ mixed
BofA's 800V 'best ideas' are TXN/ADI/ON, not IFX — IFX is read more as a beneficiary by association than a top pick. IFX flagged at 12-month relative highs alongside the AI-semi complex (Redburn).
3 Jun
Margin / pricingJefferies · Janardan Menon (Analog Semis)▲ bull
Start of a powerful analog upcycle driven by tightening supply + structural AI-datacenter demand; the EPS upgrade cycle could last 'another 7-8 quarters' with 50-150% upside to 2027/28 EPS. PSS division margin reaching ~30% by Q4 FY26 → ~35%. Silicon MOSFETs reframed as 'almost a new analog business' (>30% op margin). IFX implemented a second price hike effective Jul 1, 2026; valuation ~35x two-year-forward EPS.
8 Jun
Competition / shareExpert call · Tier-1 German auto▲ bull
NXP and Infineon 'doing well' in the pipeline (vs Renesas, more vulnerable) on more stable supply chains; in safety-critical sockets — e.g. zonal controllers ASIL-D — 'most of these chipsets are from Qualcomm and Infineon', with NXP/STM 'having no chance at the moment' — a structural design-in advantage for IFX.
15 Jun
Demand / capexCiti · 800V power▲ bull
Citi names Infineon (with MPWR and Renesas) as the current incumbent in the GPU power-delivery market (800V-to-1V conversion), sized at ~$2B (2025) → ~$12B (2028, ~75% CAGR). On the industrial 800V side (grid-to-core) IFX is a key SiC beneficiary; ESS semi content ~$2,500/MW with ~31% CAGR in ESS shipments 2025-30. IFX entered the second round of analog price hikes effective Jun/Jul.
18 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · Beavington (briefing)◆ mixed
Technical tape: IFX sold off and then +5% on 'rotation from analog into WFE, not fundamentals'.
22 Jun
Demand / capexAlphaSense · Infineon Primer▲ bull
Compiles the structural AI bull case: 43% power-semi share in AI servers rising to 47% in 2026; AI-related revenue EUR 750m FY25 → 1.6bn FY26 → 3.3bn FY28 (~17% of sales) at 55-60% gross margin. Kepler Cheuvreux projects 58% CAGR in AI-DC sales FY25-28; PTs: Kepler Buy EUR 70 (from EUR 54) and Bernstein Outperform EUR 74; contrarian Santander tactical Sell. Risk flags: SiC oversupply and the IVR threat (Integrated Voltage Regulator tech post-2028, where IFX 'lacks a competitive proprietary offering').

3 JunreportJefferies "Analog Semis" — IFX/STM analog upcycle deep-dive, AI-DC demand pull, PSS ~30-35% margin, MOSFET = new analog, July price hike (Menon, 2026-06-03)

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

15 May
PRINT — earningsSMIC · earnings Q1 2026
Revenue $2,505m, +0.7% q/q / ~+11% y/y. Gross margin 20.1%, +0.9pt q/q (above consensus). Net profit attributable $197m, +14% q/q but below consensus on lower government subsidies. Utilization 93.1%, -2.6pt q/q; shipments -0.2% q/q but blended ASP +2.5% q/q. Q2 2026 guide: revenue +14% to +16% q/q, GM 20-22%.
17 May
Guidance / numbersJPM · Gokul Hariharan (briefing)▲ bull
The +14-16% Q2 guide far exceeded the low bar from the Street/JPM (+2-7%); split ~shipments +10% / ASP +5%, with price hikes of 5-10% in BCD/analog/PMIC/storage. JPM upgraded from UW → Neutral, PT HK$57 → HK$67, but kept Neutral on 2H consumer risk.

Q2 2026 revenue guideQ2'26↑ rising
now · 2026-05-17 · JPM (Gokul), briefing

2026-02-11Q1 guide flat q/q · Q4'25 call

MSFTMicrosoft CorporationrichQ3 FY26 · Apr 29 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
14
events
5
bull reads
7
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

16 Apr
Valuation / positionBofA · Fenske (spec-sales)▼ bear
MSFT called the 'most hated Mag7', 'dead for sale' in the long-only community (reflexive selling as the underweight grows), trapped in the 'software is going to die' thesis; central gripe: an Anthropic Excel plugin works better than the native Copilot despite MSFT's head start, and Azure capped in the near term because GPUs are diverted to internal R&D.
20 Apr
Guidance / numbersBofA · Fenske (earnings preview)◆ mixed
For the print, the Azure bogey was Street 37.5% / buy-side ~38.4%, needing 38-40% for the stock to work; Copilot at 15M seats = only ~3.5% Office attach.
29 Apr
PRINT — earningsMicrosoft · earnings Q3 FY26
Revenue $82.9B, +18% (+15% cc); EPS $4.27, +21% y/y (ex-OpenAI impact); op margin 46%; GM 68%. Microsoft Cloud $54.5B, +29%. Azure +40% cc, ahead of consensus, capacity delivered ahead of plan. Q4 FY26 guide: Azure +39-40% cc, with a modest acceleration in 2H CY26; supply constrained at least through 2026. AI ARR >$37B, +123% y/y. Capex $31.9B; CY26 capex ~$190B (of which ~$25B from higher memory pricing).
30 Apr
Guidance / numbersUBS · Karl Keirstead (recap Q3 FY26)▲ bull
Azure +39% (Mar) with the June guide at the top of +40% and a 'modest' 2H acceleration coming from efficiency gains, not capacity adds (40% more token throughput on the same hardware); UBS models 42-43% exiting the year. Removal of the OpenAI rev-share hits COGS, not Azure contra-revenue (the Azure acceleration is organic); AGI clause removed ('big win').
30 Apr
Valuation / positionCiti · Radke (C1Q26 recap)▲ bull
Called the print a 'clearing event' (Azure clarity + PC/Windows-OEM weakness 'ripped off'), setting up a positive revision story. MSFT says there is no 'FCF floor' — capex is driven by demand signal — while maintaining a commitment to flat operating margins in FY27.
30 Apr
Capital / M&AUBS · Keirstead (capex/FCF)▼ bear
CY26 capex guided $190B ($40B in the June quarter, ~$60B/qtr in 2H); annualizing implies FY27 ~$240B (~+65%) — UBS raised its FY27 capex estimate from ~$181B to ~$234B, pushing modeled FY27 FCF to ~-$21B. June-quarter GM guided to mid-66% (-~220bps y/y).
6 May
Valuation / positionBofA · Wigg (spec-sales)▼ bear
Investors see MSFT 'stuck in the mud' near term, used as a funding long vs SaaS, with Azure growth decelerating relative to GCP/AWS accelerating.
15 May
Capital / M&AAckman (13F via briefing)▲ bull
Ackman built a new core position in MSFT at ~21x forward, with M365+Azure representing ~70% of earnings.
18 May
Product / roadmapMSFT IR call▲ bull
Capacity allocated weekly across three buckets — Azure platform / 1P apps (M365 + GitHub Copilot) / internal R&D — adding ~1GW in each of FQ2 and FQ3, targeting ~doubling the fleet in 2 years. Usage-based pricing inflection (per-seat + consumption) as a third monetization vector; multi-model with Foundry customers using Anthropic + OpenAI up to 2x q/q (10,000+ using >1 model).
1 Jun
Competition / shareJefferies · Brent Thill (conf)▼ bear
CIO check: enthusiasm 'tempered not on Azure but on the applications' (Power Apps vs custom LLM/Snowflake/Databricks builds), with ~5 deals of ~$5M each that may not renew.
3 Jun
Valuation / positionBarclays · Rand (desk)▼ bear
Barclays desk: investors see MSFT 'stuck in the mud' near term, used as a funding long vs SaaS; positioning moved from +2 to -1 post-Q2.
13 Jun
Capital / M&A@Midnight_Captl (MS/Altimeter via briefing)▼ bear
MS/Altimeter revised their CY27 capex estimate from $178B to $276B — the largest hyperscaler revision; @ParadisLabs points to a ~-18% 1-year return with a critique of 'lowest ROI on AI capex to date'.
16 Jun
Capital / M&AJPM · AI Capex 2.0▼ bear
MSFT is the 'laggard pre-funder': alone among the five hyperscalers it funded the build-out entirely from internal cash — no IG or equity issuance in 5+ years. JPM models capex ~$88bn FY25 → ~$140bn FY26 → ≥$240bn FY27 → ~$300bn FY28, with the funding gap widening to ~$90bn FY27 / ~$130bn FY28; ~1 year behind Alphabet/Amazon on the funding curve, pointing to 'substantial new MSFT supply in C2H26'.
22 Jun
Valuation / positionBernstein · Cloud in the Quarter▲ bull
Reiterates Outperform, TP $646; MSFT well positioned in an AI-bubble scenario — 1P capacity utilization ramping, third-party contracts beyond OpenAI; Azure stable→accelerating with mid-40s GAAP margins; valuation an 'attractive entry point'. AI ARR $37B, +123% y/y; commercial RPO $627B (+26% ex-OpenAI). GitHub: commits from ~1B to ≥14B/run-rate.
23 Jun
Competition / shareStratechery · Ben Thompson◆ mixed
MSFT highly incentivized to use Chinese models: under usage-based pricing (E7 = Anthropic+OpenAI at $99/employee/month), an open-source model lets MSFT offer more usage without ceding margin to the frontier labs; MSFT confirmed to Axios it is exploring a DeepSeek V4 fine-tuned for Copilot Cowork, available 'in the coming weeks'. Thesis: US software as the biggest beneficiary of cheaper Chinese models.

Forward commentary & how it evolved · anchored on the 2 most recent references

now · 2026-06-15 · @ParadisLabs / JPM 'AI Capex 2.0'
Targeting ~50% of AI compute on own silicon by 2028; Maia 200 >30% better perf/$
2025-11-12SemiAnalysis: Maia 200 internal failure; 2nm Maia 300 not deployable near targets until late 2027 · SemiAnalysis MSFT AI Strategy
2026-06-15Targeting ~50% of AI compute on own silicon by 2028; Maia 200 >30% better perf/$ · @ParadisLabs / JPM 'AI Capex 2.0'
↳ Custom-ASIC the structural weak spot vs GOOG/AMZN; first-party silicon lowers COGS if it lands.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

15 Apr
Demand / capexJPM · Doug Anmuth (internet preview)▲ bull
Framed AMZN as an 'AI laggard→winner' transition, with the OpenAI deal adding ~$100B to backlog at the print and AI ARR >$15B / chips >$20B.
16 Apr
Competition / shareBofA · Fenske (spec-sales)▲ bull
Called AWS 'my favorite business', with Jassy's redemption arc (OpenAI $50B + Anthropic = 'two AI winners solidified', Trainium3/4 sold out); ~$50B if Trainium were an external chip program vs Broadcom's ~$100B AI-semi target.
20 Apr
Margin / pricingBofA · Post (earnings preview)◆ mixed
Preview math: ~$150B of the $200B FY26 capex is AWS-driven (~100% of AWS revenue), implying a build toward ~$400B of AWS revenue; lower AI-deal margins (~10-20%) and Q2 margin risk as Anthropic ramps.
29 Apr
PRINT — earningsAmazon · earnings Q1 FY26
Revenue $181.5B, +17% y/y; OI $23.9B; op margin 13.1% (record). AWS +28% y/y (fastest in 15 quarters), +$2B q/q, $150B run-rate. AWS backlog $364B (excludes the >$100B Anthropic deal). Custom silicon >$20B run-rate, +40% q/q; Trainium commitments >$225B. Q2'26 guide: sales $194-199B, OI $20-24B (helped by Prime Day moving into Q2).
30 Apr
Guidance / numbersBernstein · Mark Shmulik (recap)▲ bull
Called the OI guide 'very conservative' and read the revenue guide as implying continued AWS acceleration to 'low-to-mid 30s'. ~$20B of the sequential step-up is non-frontier-lab, the largest ever recorded; Trainium 30-40% better price/perf allows it to 'save billions in capex and extract margin points'.
30 Apr
Margin / pricingCiti · C1Q26 recap▲ bull
AWS grew 28% with better-than-expected 1Q margins and 'no one-timers' (mgmt reframed the historical range as effectively just 'low-30s'); Trainium saves AWS 'several hundred bps' of margin vs renting Nvidia GPUs; Bedrock model-access revenue is booked NET. AWS margins are low only because it is a 'newer-stage, more capital-intensive' business.
5 Jun
Demand / capexBernstein · Shuster (briefing)▼ bear
Cut AWS web-metric color, weakening for 4 weeks, 'Q4 risk' in non-AI cloud growth. Maintains Outperform, $315.
6 Jun
Demand / capexBernstein · Shmulik/Arbsman (briefing)▼ bear
Flagged 'unusually flat' non-AI cloud SSO growth in May, with non-AI cloud 4Q growth at risk if it persists.
8 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · Russell rebal call◆ mixed
The June-26 Russell reclassification moves ~600bps of Amazon into the Russell 1000 Value benchmark (AMZN goes to ~zero in the 1000 Growth) — a mechanical flow event (forced selling of 'winners'), part of a Mag7 growth→value rotation.
16 Jun
Capital / M&AJPM · AI Capex 2.0◆ mixed
Corroborates the AWS re-acceleration (+28%, $150bn run-rate, $364bn backlog excluding Anthropic >$100bn); >$225bn of Trainium revenue commitments from Anthropic+OpenAI. Funding: capex ~$200bn FY26 → ~$300bn FY27E → ~$350bn FY28E; TTM FCF collapsed to ~$1bn (from ~$26bn the year before). AMZN responded with ~$67bn of bonds in 1H + a $17.5bn term loan — ~$85bn of new funding capacity in <6 months without touching equity, read as pre-funding the FY27 ramp.
17 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Ben Legg (ad expert)▲ bull
Amazon ad FY ~27% (sponsored products 65% of spend, softening to ~+16% May vs +21% April), but video ads +83% y/y and DSP +77% y/y, both accelerating; Amazon 'declared war' on The Trade Desk (take rate 20% vs 1%).
17 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Karl Keirstead (tokenmaxxing)▲ bull
Hyperscaler bull case: the AWS/GCP accelerations are not explained by hourly accelerator rental alone — the hyperscalers are increasingly the distribution endpoint for the frontier labs (enterprises deploying Claude Code via AWS/Azure). Lower gross margin but powerful upsell; investor mood on AMZN/MSFT/ORCL 'more mixed than I would expect'.
18 Jun
Valuation / positionBofA · Justin Post / MS · Nowak (briefing)▲ bull
BofA Buy, PO $310. MS (Nowak) flags the SpaceX compute deal at $50/watt as ~16% of upside to AWS '27 revenue if monetization reaches $25/watt. Prime Day (96 hrs, starting Jun-23) estimated by BofA at ~$22B of GMV (≈+5% y/y).
23 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · Doug Anmuth (Prime deep-dive)▲ bull
OW, Dec-26 PT $330 (~32x 2027E GAAP EPS $10.38), AMZN Best Idea / Analyst Focus List. Prime 'unbundled' worth ~$1,440/year vs the $139 cost (~10x); 370M+ global subs at year-end 2026 (~139M US / ~231M Int'l); ~$7B-$8B of Prime Day revenue shifted into Q2'26; a $20 hike in the US price ≈ ~$3B of annualized sales, now seen slipping from 2026 to 2027.

Management commentary — how it evolved (by quarter)

GOOGAlphabet Inc.richQ1 FY26 · Apr 29 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
15
events
7
bull reads
3
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

29 Apr
PRINT — earningsAlphabet · earnings Q1 FY26
Consolidated revenue $109.9B, +22% (+19% cc); OI +30% to $39.7B, margin 36.1%; EPS $5.11 (+82%, inflated by equity gains). Search & Other +19% to $60.4B (fastest in ~5 years). Google Cloud +63% to $20B (first time >$20B), OI tripled to $6.6B, margin 32.9%; backlog ~doubled q/q to $462B, now including TPU hardware sales. Capex $35.7B; FY26 guide raised to $180-190B; C27 to 'increase significantly'.
30 Apr
Demand / capexCiti · C1Q26 recap▲ bull
Search +16% XFX / +19% reported (better than the buy-side 'high-teens' bar); Google Cloud +60%+; Gemini model revenue +800% y/y; AI Max now ~30% of campaigns; the print pulled '27 EPS estimates 'much closer to $15'.
30 Apr
Capital / M&AExpert · hyperscaler 1Q callbacks◆ mixed
Accounting clarification: Google books external TPU hardware sales on a GROSS basis, with a license-fee markup of ~10-15% (~$2-3K/chip), the full value in the Cloud backlog (no capex). ~$85B of TPU dollars entered the 1Q26 backlog (vs ~$11.5B in 4Q25), reflecting ~4GW of Anthropic orders; ex-TPU leaves ~$365B of 'pure' backlog. First quarter without a buyback since COVID; TPU revenue recognition mostly a 2027 event.
30 Apr
Valuation / positionGoldman Sachs · Eric Sheridan▲ bull
PT $450, owned/liked; Q1 pulled forward a positive revision cycle: ~30%+ cloud revenue revision in C27/28, mid-teens consolidated OI revision; ~$14 GAAP EPS '27 / ~$17 '28 (buy-side whisper $15+/$17.50-18+); sum-of-parts >$450.
8 May
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley · Brian Nowak (equity call)◆ mixed
'Best asset on Earth', won the AI Game of Thrones — the TPU bull case is now the base case, cloud guided to 80-90% growth over the next ~7 quarters by the backlog book. But bear: ranks GOOG #3 of the MAG (Meta>Amazon>Google): 'hard to re-rate Google five turns on a $5T cap', no near-term dry powder for multiple expansion.
19 May
Competition / shareBofA · Vivek Arya (NVDA setup)◆ mixed
The ~500MW-class Google/Blackstone DC vehicle for TPUs is read as a financing structure for TPU capex (not Google OEM'ing chips); ~80-90% of TPU inference is for Google's own properties (Search/YouTube/AI Mode) — Google Cloud probably runs more GPUs than TPUs; a ratio of one Axion CPU to two TPUs.
21 May
Valuation / positionCiti · Ronald Josey (briefing)▲ bull
Buy $447 based on AI Search ad formats.
29 May
Demand / capexBarclays · Ross Sandler (briefing)▲ bull
OW $405; AI Search = 'biggest change in 25 years', with higher CTR/coverage.
1 Jun
Product / roadmapJefferies · Brent Thill (conf)▲ bull
Flags 'anti-gravity' (agentic app building) as a dormant 'killer app' — an Atlassian reseller reportedly replaced its Salesforce CRM with it; framed alongside GCP's best-in-class Q1 backlog and quantum as underappreciated next catalysts.
2 Jun
Capital / M&AVital Knowledge (briefing)▼ bear
The $85B raise = 'Mag7 morphing from cash machines into capital-intensive giants — weighs on multiples regardless of the top-line'.
13 Jun
Capital / M&AMS/Altimeter (briefing)▼ bear
C27 capex revised to ~$300B (MS, +57% y/y, Altimeter data); FCF only $10.1B in Q1, buybacks shrinking, depreciation accelerating.
15 Jun
Product / roadmapCiti/Counterpoint · WWDC▲ bull
At WWDC 2026 Apple gave Gemini a dedicated keynote slide as the foundation anchoring Siri/Apple Intelligence with world knowledge (replacing the prior bolt-on ChatGPT framing); Counterpoint reads Google deeper into agentic experiences. Google One AI now includes a new $100/month plan reserving the latest Gemini features.
16 Jun
Capital / M&AJPM · AI Capex 2.0◆ mixed
Processing ~3.2 quadrillion tokens/month (~7x y/y) — evidence that agentic inference is the marginal unit of compute demand. JPM models capex FY26 $185B, ~$300B FY27E / ~$350B FY28E; FCF compresses to ~$12B FY26E → -$27B FY27E. Funding: ~$51bn of senior notes YTD + an $84.75bn equity raise; 'crossed the Rubicon' to a structural funding requirement. TPU units 1.8M (2025) → 4.5M (2026E) → 8.0M (2027E).
17 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Ben Legg (ad expert)▲ bull
Search demand re-accelerating through 2026 after the April-war wobble — Legg models Search Q2 27% / Q4 +25% (raised +3pts), FY ~26%, with AI Max made mandatory for large advertisers from September (~1pt tailwind) + the first Gemini ad; YouTube re-accelerating (FY ~21%) on B2B.
18 Jun
Competition / shareRothschild · Adley (briefing)▼ bear
Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer leaving for OpenAI (<2 years after the $2.7B Character.AI deal) — a near-term overhang at DeepMind. Maintains Buy. (Samsung foundry engagement for Axion and TPU v10 'Icefish' work also cited.)
23 Jun
Supply / capacityRedburn · Conor O'Mara (Asia Trip)◆ mixed
Rising HBM cost is the driver of Google's shift to MediaTek (Broadcom charges HBM in the BOM, MediaTek does not); MediaTek's procurement share rising from 6%→30% (2026-28). Google tried to buy HBM directly but found it too challenging, so procurement stays split between Broadcom and MediaTek for ~3 years.

now · 2026-06-22 · UBS (Scott Linzer)

METAMeta Platforms, Inc.richQ1 FY26 · Apr 29 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
13
events
7
bull reads
2
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

15 Apr
Guidance / numbersJPM– neutral
JPM internet preview for 1Q26: estimate setup and framing of the capex guide as the central theme heading into the print.
16 Apr
Valuation / positionBofA · Fenske▲ bull
BofA/Fenske (spec-sales): META screened as the cheapest Mag7 name on relative forward P/E following the social-media litigation selloff, with the best post-earnings revisions in the group and untapped consumer-AI optionality across IG/WhatsApp/FB.
20 Apr
Guidance / numbersBofA · Fenske▲ bull
BofA/Fenske preview: expected a beat (Street rev $55.4B, ~70% performance ads), with Muse Spark targeting frontier quality by year-end; a capex guide raise framed as a "non-event" vs MSFT/GOOG/AMZN, but META still lacks the enterprise/cloud re-rate thesis.
29 Apr
PRINT — earningsQ1 FY26 print (Susan Li)
Q1 FY26: revenue $56.3B +33% y/y (+29% cc); FoA ad rev $55.0B +33%; impressions +19%, price/ad +12%. Op income $22.9B (41% margin); clean EPS $7.31. Capex $19.8B; FY26 guide raised to $125-145B (memory pricing). Q2 revenue guide $58-61B.
30 Apr
Demand / capexExpert recap (hyperscaler WRC)◆ mixed
Expert recap: META was the only big-three to flag macro — an ad-spend deceleration in March (Middle East, US/W Europe), recovering in April; still printed ~33%, ~1.5pts below the top of the guide. The first full quarter of EU "less-personalized-ads" regulation exposes ~16% of ad revenue.
6 May
Valuation / positionBofA · Wigg◆ mixed
BofA/Wigg (spec-sales): META remains a "show-me story" that needs a "wow" product shipped to re-rate vs the easier capex justification at GOOG/MSFT/AMZN.
8 May
Valuation / positionMS · Brian Nowak▲ bull
Nowak (MS, OW PT $775): META is his #1 pick in the Mag7 — "a deep freaking moat" (3.5B DAU). Catalysts: a personal/shoppable agent ("Meta Claw"/Muse); ad rev could grow ~20-25% in '27 on algo improvement alone. Neocloud as "backup optionality" (~$10B/GW) = 8%+ of upside to '28 EPS.
1 Jun
Product / roadmapJefferies · Brent Thill▲ bull
Jefferies conf: a CIO said he would bring Meta into his enterprise environment IF Meta launched a commercial model wrapper/cloud (Red Hat-style) — framed as underpriced bull optionality; the absence of such a vehicle is precisely why it does not get a GOOG-style re-rate.
11 Jun
Demand / capexBofA · Justin Post (equity call)◆ mixed
BofA equity call: the bull emphasizes that the 33% growth is pre-AI-product (Muse Spark NOT yet in the ads/reco stack). Bears (buy-side PMs): ~$30B of capex to ~$150B for ~10pts of incremental growth, incremental ROIC "deeply negative," the MSL lab as a hidden loss of ~$40B/year through '27; no enterprise/cloud story to re-rate on.
16 Jun
Capital / M&AJPM ("AI Capex 2.0")▼ bear
JPM: FY26 capex midpoint raised +$10bn to $135bn (memory cost); models ~$185bn FY27E / ~$225bn FY28E. FCF turns negative, cumulative funding gap ~$97bn over 3 years; contractual commitments +$107bn in the quarter to $238bn; 20% Hyperion stake with ~$28bn of RVG. Returned to the bond market with a $25bn deal.
16 Jun
Product / roadmapJPM ("AI Capex 2.0")▲ bull
JPM: Broadcom planning multiple gigawatts of MTIA deployments starting in 2027; Meta intends to train its next-generation models on MTIA — corroborates the >1GW in-house ASIC ramp.
17 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Ben Legg▲ bull
UBS/Ben Legg: increasingly excited about Meta Business Agents as the biggest 5-year tailwind — a SaaS layer ($15 and $50/month, free today). Models a ~1-2pt tailwind already in Q4, lifting FY ad growth to ~30-31%; micro-businesses ~$1.5T of ad spend by 2030, with Meta capturing ~$200B.
18 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Vayner Media▲ bull
UBS/Vayner Media (ad-agency): digital spend is a "tale of two cities" — efficiency cleanup everywhere funding continued scale in the walled gardens (Google/Meta/TikTok/Amazon) at the expense of everyone else.
22 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Scott Linzer▼ bear
UBS/Scott Linzer (agency checks): more skeptical on the core — total Meta Q2 1.4-1.5% / Q3 3.4% / Q4 4.5-5% / FY ~4%; Meta pricing flat-to-up (it does not fall like Google's CPCs) is the key hesitation; Instagram materially underperforming; Meta Business AI "not ready for prime time," more of a 2027 opportunity.

Meta Business AI 'not ready for prime time,' more a 2027 opportunity; total Meta FY ad ~4% on agency checks

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

1 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · Brent Thill▲ bull
Jefferies (Brian) contrarian defense of ORCL: solid sequencing — secured OpenAI's RPO first, locked in the leases, gave the only multi-year guide of any public cloud ($20+ EPS FY30). MS skepticism (Weiss, cutting to ~$10) read as "a window into how skeptical you can publicly afford to be." Notes Anthropic's ~$50bn Fluidstack deal against ~$1-2bn ARR.
2 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Keirstead▲ bull
UBS (Keirstead) Buy, PT raised to $285 (from $250): "positive into the print, long-compute thesis."
10 Jun
PRINT — earningsQ4 FY26 print (Maxson/Magouyrk)
Q4 FY26: revenue $19.2B +21% USD; cloud infra +93%; cloud DB +29% with multicloud +404% y/y. Non-GAAP EPS $2.11 (+24%). Headline: RPO $638B +363% y/y; $67B of AI-infra contracts signed in the quarter (4 clients each >$8B); >1.2GW delivered in FY26; GPU utilization 97.5%. FY27 guide revenue +34% cc, EPS $8.05.
11 Jun
Supply / capacityWFC ("ORCL recap")◆ mixed
WFC callback with both co-CEOs + the new CFO: OCI sold out for the next 6 months and already selling 2027 (not 2028); a ~quarter-and-a-half lag between capacity coming online and revenue run-rate explains the optical OCI "miss" in F4Q (timing, not demand). New CFO: fiscal 28 as peak dollar capex (~$90-95bn FY27 → ~$150bn FY28). BYOH GM ~55% vs ~40%.
15 Jun
Valuation / positionDB · Brad Zelnick▲ bull
DB (Zelnick) Buy, PT $300 reaffirmed post virtual-IR: "strong cloud DB/RPO positioning," margins "under the microscope"; modeled a 5th consecutive quarter of OCI acceleration (~+89% y/y vs Street ~+92%).
16 Jun
Capital / M&AJPM ("AI Capex 2.0")▼ bear
JPM: ORCL is "the most reliant on external funding" of the hyperscaler group. A growing share of contracts is prepay/BYOH (now $75bn); new net-capex metric ~$48bn FY26 → ~$70bn FY27, leaving a funding gap ~$37bn FY27 before dividends. Raised $43bn debt + $5bn convertible pref in FY26; plans ~$40bn debt+equity FY27 (incl. ATM ~$20bn) — first hyperscaler to embed equity "more out of necessity than desire." $261bn of off-balance-sheet leases.
18 Jun
Capital / M&AUBS · Keirstead ("Karl software call")◆ mixed
UBS (Keirstead): Oracle was the first of the big clouds to define when it hits peak capex (this fiscal year or the next) — but it's a moving target (if Anthropic did a $100bn/5yr deal, the peak shifts). Frames hyperscalers as the distribution endpoint of the frontier labs — a lower-GM but revenue-accelerating dynamic.
22 Jun
Capital / M&AMS TMT Credit (Tyler/Satyam)▼ bear
MS TMT Credit "$260bn Off Balance Sheet": credit read of the FY26 10-K — non-commissioned lease commitments plateaued at ~$260bn; net lease-liability growth >$10bn in F4Q (still operating-skewed); +$19bn of new cloud-infra purchase commitments (5Y, FY27-start) outside lease treatment. Framing: "funding relief, not resolution."
22 Jun
Valuation / positionBernstein ("Cloud in the Quarter")▲ bull
Bernstein reiterates Outperform, TP $325: shift from Cloud story to "major hyperscaler and AI training/inferencing provider" — $638B RPO, $166B FY30 OCI revenue guide, ~$300B RPO from OpenAI — seen as value-accretive over 5-10 years despite margin/cash pressure. Overhangs clear as ORCL secures nearly all of its financing through the end of FY27.
23 Jun
Guidance / numbersDB · Brad Zelnick▲ bull
DB (Zelnick) FY26 10-K Review (Buy, PT $300): 10-K nuggets — RPO net adds +$85.4bn q/q (>$100bn booked), cRPO ~$79bn (+73% y/y, ~88% NTM coverage); gross compute assets $60bn (+97% y/y) and CIP ~$40bn (+142% y/y) pointing to capacity not yet in service; headcount 141k (-13% y/y), reinforcing the FY27 operating-leverage story.

now · 2026-06-11 · WFC ORCL recap
GM down ~500bps FY26, another ~900bps guided FY27 before structural improvement; BYOH GM ~55% vs ~40%

NOWServiceNow, Inc.mediumQ1 FY26 · Apr 22 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
4
events
2
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

22 Apr
PRINT — earningsQ1 FY26 print (Mastantuono/McDermott)
Q1 FY26 beat-and-raise: subscription revenue $3.671bn (+19% y/y); cRPO +21% y/y (~100bps above guide); RPO $27.7bn (+23.5%); FCF margin 44%. FY26 guide raised +$205m to $15.735-15.775bn (+20.5-21%). 2026 AI commitments target raised from $1bn to $1.5bn; 50% of net new is now non-seat-based pricing.
30 Apr
Demand / capexUBS · Keirstead▼ bear
UBS/Keirstead (MSFT print recap): Microsoft cited weaker Dynamics contract renewals as enterprises paused to evaluate "new AI scenarios, including new usage-based pricing models" — a negative read for the SaaS apps space that "may have already weighed on the recent results and guide of ServiceNow and SAP."
4 May
Guidance / numbersNOW · Financial Analyst Day▲ bull
Financial Analyst Day: $30bn subscription revenue guide for 2030 (~17.5% 4-year CAGR), with $32bn as an upside case, and a "Rule of 60" guide for 2030. Some of the growth rests on the pricing pivot: an average uplift of 20-30%.
5 May
Guidance / numbersUBS · Keirstead ("NOW investor day / PLTR call")◆ mixed
UBS/Keirstead read-through from the FAD: a likely positive catalyst, but "measured" on three concerns — McDermott's unhedged optimism (zero disruption risk acknowledged), uncertainty over whether the coding-style seat→usage uplift translates to non-coding use cases, and the non-linear trajectory (the real seat→agent ACV inflection only ~2028, leaving 2027 potentially "a little more modest"). NOW also flagged an AI-native down-market ITSM at 100% consumption.
19 May
Valuation / positionBofA▲ bull
BofA reinstated coverage at Buy, PO $130 (via morning briefing): "AI beneficiary, entrenched workflow position" thesis.

Forward commentary & how it evolved · anchored on the 2 most recent references

ANTHROPICAnthropicrichintra-quarter · Apr 16 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
14
events
11
bull reads
0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

16 Apr
Product / roadmapMap podcast · Felix Riisberg (Anthropic head of product)▲ bull
Riisberg (head of product, leads eng on Claude Co-Work): Claude Mythos Preview is "a dramatic step up" internally, "particularly good at finding security issues" — it generated Project Glasswing; Mythos to be kept "completely closed and private." Co-Work extends agentic capability to non-coding knowledge work; memory lives in the harness (markdown "skills"), roadmap ≤1 month. Jensen Huang (same source): Anthropic's "$5-6 billion" month in February as an "Oppenheimer moment."
28 Apr
Margin / pricingAllen Roush (equity call) / Big Technology◆ mixed
Allen Roush (AI-coding call): ~80% of business revenue is token-based (not subscription); enterprise API by token is profitable while consumer subs ($20/$200) lose money. Chronic compute constraints cause persistent outages ("models down all the time").
30 Apr
Supply / capacityExpert (hyperscaler WRC recap)– neutral
Hyperscaler recap: Google books the gross of the Broadcom→Google→Anthropic TPU resale (markup ~10-15% / ~$2-3k per chip). Anthropic TPU orders: ~1 GW in 2026 (initial $10bn + Broadcom order $11bn end-26 = ~$21bn) + ~3 GW incremental for 2027; Google also provides credit backstops for third-party data centers.
19 May
Supply / capacityBofA · Vivek Arya (NVDA preview)▲ bull
BofA/Arya: OpenAI + Anthropic together ~40-50% of the major hyperscalers' backlog; Anthropic "always scrambling for more capacity" — service "slows down in the middle of the day." Arya expects NVDA will want to invest directly in Anthropic (an earlier "regret"); Vera CPUs delivered to Oracle, SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic.
1 Jun
Demand / capexJefferies · Brent Thill▲ bull
Jefferies (Brian): a narrative inversion — "Sam had customers and usage and Dario didn't... Dario was projecting numbers from a ~$2bn ARR company. Now Dario is [a] $30 billion ARR company." Anthropic did a "$50 billion Fluidstack deal" still with ARR ~$1-2bn — one of the most aggressively sized compute deals vs usage.
4 Jun
Competition / shareUBS · Tim Arcuri (AVGO callback)▲ bull
UBS (Arcuri): Anthropic's XPU commitment with Broadcom is 5 GW, all Broadcom (no MediaTek); the Anthropic + OpenAI SPV points to ~20 GW. Broadcom frames OpenAI's compute demand as similar to Anthropic's, but with lower revenue visibility — Anthropic is the highest-visibility ASIC anchor.
8 Jun
Demand / capexWFC · FluidStack expert▲ bull
FluidStack expert (WFC): despite serving ~30 models (half proprietary/half open), ~95% of tokens served are Claude/Anthropic — concrete evidence of frontier capture. Anthropic's Q1-2026 inflection was the single biggest driver of the GPU supply-demand imbalance; Colossus capacity now sold for inference to Google and Anthropic.
12 Jun
Product / roadmapBG2Pod▲ bull
BG2Pod: Fable 5 (Jun 2026) is SOTA on nearly every benchmark, 1M-token context, always-on adaptive thinking; Karpathy flagged long-running task capability as the real differentiator. xAI/SpaceX monetizes a compute deal with Anthropic at ~$22-23B/GW/year. Frontier captures ~90% of the economic value even with open-source capturing the majority of tokens.
16 Jun
Supply / capacityJPM ("AI Capex 2.0")▲ bull
JPM: revenue run-rate "fast-approaching $50B" (from ~$9B at YE2025); ~10+ GW of multi-vendor commitments. Capacity: AWS up to 5 GW ($100B+ over 10 years, Trainium); Google up to 1M TPUs / >1 GW by 2026; Azure up to 1 GW ($30B); and a Broadcom deal for multiple GW of next-gen TPUs ($35B, Google backstops the lease payments). Trains/serves on >1M Trainium2 chips (Project Rainier).
18 Jun
Supply / capacityCantor (CoreWeave call)▲ bull
Cantor: Anthropic signed only a small ~$2-3bn / 70 MW deal with CoreWeave (~Apr 10-11) — read as well below true need; Cantor expects a sizable CoreWeave-Anthropic deal (~$20-30bn) alongside a big RPO beat in 2Q. Google↔SpaceX deal (~$10/GPU-hr H100) read as channeling Colossus capacity to Anthropic.
18 Jun
Demand / capexBig Technology · Ramp (Ara Kharazian)▲ bull
Big Technology w/ Ramp: Anthropic is the most-used AI vendor among US businesses (~41% of firms vs OpenAI ~39.5%), having overtaken OpenAI in May 2026. AI is the fastest-growing cost category Ramp has ever tracked. Bear: neither Anthropic nor OpenAI offers spend-management controls, the #1 enterprise complaint.
18 Jun
Product / roadmapBTG · Mythos expert◆ mixed
BTG (Mythos expert): Mythos (cyber-capable) remains unreleased — too dangerous/compute-heavy to serve broadly.
22 Jun
Supply / capacityBernstein ("Cloud in the Quarter")▲ bull
Bernstein: Anthropic's >$100B deal with AWS is excluded from AWS's reported $364B backlog (i.e., AWS backlog understates Anthropic's committed compute); Anthropic appears for the first time (alongside OpenAI) in the CY25 Gartner PaaS data — evidence that the private labs already have the scale to register as cloud/PaaS players.
23 Jun
Supply / capacityStoneX/TrendForce · Avril (Memory expert call)▲ bull
StoneX/TrendForce (Avril): an Anthropic-Micron partnership combines server DRAM + enterprise SSD in 2027 + HBM in 2028 — the HBM component implies Anthropic is developing its own in-house ASIC (you only need HBM if you buy/build accelerators). Anthropic is pursuing the same deal structure with SK Hynix and Samsung.

↳ Direct memory allocation across all three makers consistent with a multi-silicon/own-ASIC roadmap.
Neocloud / CoreWeave compute dealforward (2Q expected)↑ rising
now · 2026-06-18 · Cantor CoreWeave call
signed only small ~$2-3bn/70MW CRWV deal (Apr); sizable ~$20-30bn Anthropic CRWV deal expected alongside 2Q RPO beat

↳ 70MW read as far below true need; Colossus capacity channeled to Anthropic near-term.

1 JunreportBarclays · Sandler — "AI's S-Curve Is Steepening"

OPENAIOpenAImediumintra-quarter · Apr 23 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
11
events
4
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

23 Apr
Product / roadmapOpenAI (press)– neutral
GPT-5.5 ("Spud") shipped as a frontier release; GPT-5.5/5.5 Pro on the API the next day. Positioned as a frontier model for complex professional/agentic work, with leaderboard claims vs Claude Opus 4.7 and Gemini 3.1 Pro on Terminal-Bench / FrontierMath.
30 Apr
Capital / M&AUBS · Keirstead (MSFT recap)◆ mixed
UBS (MSFT print recap): the renegotiated deal removed the AGI clause ("a very big win for Microsoft"), made OpenAI's IP royalty-free for Microsoft through 2032, kept the rev-share % but with a cap (reported ~$38bn) guaranteed through 2030. The API is no longer Azure-exclusive. Weak commercial bookings in 1Q (+7% ex-OpenAI) attributed to OpenAI having just signed a ~$250bn Azure commit.
6 May
Supply / capacityBofA · Vivek Arya (AMD recap)▲ bull
BofA/Arya (AMD recap): on OpenAI's ~6 GW / ~$100bn / 160M-warrant deal (and the Meta parallel), AMD's CFO indicated they "don't really need to do any more of these deals" — not for lack of supply, but because the MI series is now a "well-established product, demand far out-pacing supply." Warrants vest against POs + milestones (final tranche at $600/share).
1 Jun
Supply / capacityJefferies · Brent Thill▲ bull
Jefferies (Brian): Amazon flagged with "a massive partnership and deal with OpenAI... $50 billion getting them for compute" — new, on top of Amazon's large stake in Anthropic — read as Jassy solidifying both frontier labs on AWS.
3 Jun
Demand / capexOpenAI · CFO Sarah Friar◆ mixed
Friar (CFO interview): frames the company as "constantly under compute" — compute, not demand, is the binding constraint. IPO/compute context discussed.
4 Jun
Supply / capacityUBS · Tim Arcuri (AVGO callback)◆ mixed
UBS (Arcuri): OpenAI's XPU program with Broadcom tapes out late this year and begins shipping 2H27, with a much bigger ramp in 2028; of the ~20 GW Anthropic+OpenAI SPV, ~15 GW earmarked for the two through 2028. OpenAI's compute demand "similar to Anthropic's," but Broadcom has lower revenue visibility on OpenAI.
12 Jun
Competition / shareBG2Pod◆ mixed
BG2Pod placed four labs on the coding Pareto frontier: OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, xAI. The frontier revenue-accrual thesis (value concentrates at the frontier even if open-source wins on token volume) favors OpenAI.
16 Jun
Supply / capacityJPM ("AI Capex 2.0")▲ bull
JPM: ChatGPT WAU from ~100M (Oct-2023) to >900M (Mar-2026, ~9x); API token processing from ~300M/min to >6B/min (~20x); revenue run-rate >$25bn as of Mar-2026. Nvidia+AMD+Broadcom capacity commitments aggregate "well in excess of $1T" over the next ~4-5 years: NVIDIA ≥10 GW (NVDA invests up to $100B), AMD 6 GW, Broadcom 10 GW, AWS $38bn/7yr + ~2 GW Trainium ~$100B+, Cerebras 750 MW initial.
18 Jun
Competition / share@EugeneNg / @Hesamation (briefing)▼ bear
Briefing: ChatGPT fell below 50% AI market share for the first time — 46.4% ChatGPT vs 27.7% Gemini vs 10.3% Claude. By ARR, a FinTwit read claims Anthropic ($40bn) overtook OpenAI ($28bn); the two together ~89% of a ~$80bn AI-startup ARR pool. ~$34bn of spend in 2025 vs ~$28bn of ARR. MSFT reportedly evaluating DeepSeek to replace "expensive" OpenAI/Anthropic APIs.
19 Jun
Product / roadmap@OpenAI / The Information (briefing)▲ bull
Briefing: ChatGPT reported 230M+ weekly users; GPT-5.5 Instant brought free-tier users to "frontier Thinking" parity for health questions. Noam Shazeer (Gemini co-lead, co-author of "Attention Is All You Need") left Google for OpenAI — framed as resolving a "pretraining scaling bottleneck."
22 Jun
Supply / capacityBernstein ("Cloud in the Quarter")◆ mixed
Bernstein: OpenAI is the backbone of a two-hyperscaler AI economy — ~$300B of Oracle's $638B RPO is OpenAI, and MSFT discloses a $250B+ OpenAI commit within its $627B commercial RPO. Flags the open question of whether Oracle can "rely on their OpenAI commitments."
