AMATApplied Materials, Inc.richQ2 FY26 · May 14 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
13
events
7
bull reads
3
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

14 May
PRINT — earningsQ2 FY26 print
Q2 FY26 (quarter ended Apr 26): record revenue $7.91B (+13% q/q, +11% y/y); non-GAAP GM ~50.0%, op margin 32.1%, record non-GAAP EPS $2.86 (+20% y/y). Semi Systems $5.97B (+10%), AGS $1.67B (+17%, record). Key signal: CY guide raised to equipment growth ">30% this calendar year" (from ">20%"). Q3 FY26 guide: revenue $8.95B ±$500M (+23% y/y), EPS $3.36 ±$0.20.
15 May
Demand / capexUBS · Tim Arcuri (AMAT recap)▲ bull
Arcuri (UBS): WFE to ~$200B in 2027 and ~$250B in 2028 (+$50B/year); AMAT recovers ~half of the 280 bps of share it lost in 2025, growing above the market. ">30%" likely ends near +40% — "Applied being conservative... keeping a low bar." Has never seen 8 quarters of visibility in 30 years. AMAT is the single largest position in his suggested book.
15 May
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur (semicap-AMAT)▲ bull
Harlan/JPM: "eight quarters-plus of order backlog... the highest visibility vs any prior cycle." PVD is the industry's highest-margin product (~70%+ GM at ~82-84% share); EBIT could reach 38-40% by 2028. Advanced packaging guided to >50% growth in 2026 toward ~$2B.
18 May
Competition / shareMS▼ bear
MS cut AMAT to Equal-weight (from OW) while upgrading LAM to Overweight — "memory mismatch unresolved."
29 May
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur (Semis Spring Series)▲ bull
JPM (Sur): AMAT among the OW semicap names; WFE +20-25% in 2026, +20%+ in 2027; LRCX/AMAT favored as early-cycle names of the capacity cycle.
1 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Tim Arcuri▲ bull
Arcuri (UBS): allocates the single largest position in his suggested book to AMAT (30-40¢ of every $10).
8 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Tim Arcuri / BofA conf wrap◆ mixed
Arcuri (UBS): at $250B of WFE, CY28 EPS power ~$35 (min $30+) implies the stock trades at ~16x 2028 earnings; the "right" multiple is 23-25x. BofA conf wrap: semicaps "tend to be sensitive to memory stocks"; ~0.9 correlation between AMAT and Micron.
11 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM (spec-sales)▼ bear
JPM spec-sales: flags AMAT as a "crowded long" alongside Intel/memory/foundry — in tension with Arcuri's read that the buyside is under-positioned in equipment vs memory.
15 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Tim Arcuri (semis/memory)▲ bull
UBS (Arcuri): in the DRAM dollar-leverage ranking, AMAT ~25% of DRAM WFE dollars (vs ASML ~30%) — "ASML first, Applied second, then a distant gap, then Lam, then KLA"; KLAC "fundamentally not that exposed to DRAM."
16 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · Will Beavington▲ bull
Jefferies (Beavington), Global SEMIS Daily: AMAT named "Top pick" in the semicap complex.
17 Jun
Valuation / positionRedburn◆ mixed
Redburn (relative skeptic): Neutral, PT raised $450 → $560 post Silicon-Valley bus tour; lifts estimates +4-9% FY26-30 but applies a 15% discount to the peer EV/EBIT multiple for "greater exposure to slower-growth segments and vulnerability to China competition" (Naura/Piotech). Frames WFE at $272bn by 2030.
18 Jun
Valuation / positionBofA TMT sales▲ bull
BofA TMT sales: AMAT on the constructive semis tape (alongside MU/INTC) following the pop in the AAPL/Intel deal.
22 Jun
Competition / shareMS · Joe Moore / Shane Brett (On Memory and Semicap / Tech Talk)▼ bear
MS (Shane Brett): prefers LAM + KLA over AMAT — sees AMAT's DRAM-WFE growth decelerating from ~low-60s% in 2026 to mid-to-high-20s% in 2027 "regardless of how I run my DRAM WFE numbers." MS WFE $149B (2026) / $191B (2027, +28%). Reiterates the cut to Equal-weight.
22 Jun
Valuation / positionAlphaSense Deep Research (LRCX/ASML primers)◆ mixed
AlphaSense DR: peer-comp — AMAT CY27E P/E 26.8x (Mizuho, the lowest of the big-3 US semicaps), Outperform PT $540; Bernstein FY26E/27E reported P/E 34.0x/26.6x; GM ~49.9%, op margin 31.9% (vs LRCX 35.0%, ASML 36.0%); 9% stake in BESI; share leader in CVD-deposition.

KLACKLA CorporationrichFQ3 FY26 print → intra-quarter · Apr 29 → Jun 22, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
9
events
5
bull reads
2
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

29 Apr
PRINT — earningsKLA · Q3 FY26 earnings call
FQ3 FY26 (Mar): revenue $3.415B (+4% q/q, +11% y/y), above the $3.35B midpoint; non-GAAP EPS $9.40; non-GAAP GM 62.2%; FCF $622M; $875M returned to shareholders. Growth led by memory (DRAM/HBM) plus foundry/logic. FQ4 guide: revenue $3.575B ±$200M, GM 61.75%, EPS $9.87.
30 Apr
Margin / pricingCiti · Atif Malik▼ bear
Print recap: the stock fell because the June-quarter GM guide came in slightly below the Street. Citi: KLA "once again underestimated the impact of memory prices on systems GM, because its systems use far more memory... essentially servers that analyze defects." KLA guides total sales growth in the high-teens in 2026 vs LAM/AMAT "more in the 20s"; still points to 62% full-year GM and WFE ~$140B.
15 May
Competition / shareJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
KLA is "the only company that has nailed it that WFE growth next year will be stronger than this year," with better visibility than peers and exposure to sub-2nm programs in CY27 (TSMC A14, Intel 14A, Samsung/Rapidus 2nm, first Terafab orders). Advanced packaging grew ~70% in 2025 (TSMC CoWoS) and ~60-70% again in 2026. CY26 favors depo/etch (LRCX/AMAT) but CY27 rotates back to the new-node leaders, KLA and ASML — KLA is the call for positive revisions into 2027.
22 May
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
Conference feedback: KLA tracking 100+ design starts at 3nm/2nm at the TMC conference — predominantly complex AI ASICs (XPUs), merchant GPUs, ASIC CPUs and networking. KLA raised CY26 WFE to >$140B and signaled H2 materially stronger than H1, with CY27 WFE > CY26 — "best visibility entering May in over a decade." 80% of CY26 incremental WFE concentrated in advanced foundry/logic + DRAM + advanced packaging.
8 Jun
Valuation / positionBofA · Scemama▲ bull
Conference wrap: BofA flagged KLA specifically as the semicap name with "very bullish commentary" that lagged the SOX — "catch-up potential." Semicap as a group is sensitive to moves in memory stocks, so a hiccup in DRAM/HBM pricing hits the multiple.
15 Jun
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley TMT · T. Wigg▲ bull
ONTO (Onto Innovation) initiated Overweight — read-through from the process-control/metrology peer for KLAC.
15 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Tim Arcuri▼ bear
In the ranking of leverage to the DRAM dollar, KLAC is last — "fundamentally not as exposed to DRAM and won't be"; inspection intensity per WFE dollar in memory is ~half that of foundry, though it is rising as die-stacking adds inspection.
17 Jun
Valuation / positionRedburn◆ mixed
Neutral, PT raised $187 → $260. Agrees KLA is "well positioned to help maintain yields as leading-edge capacity expands" and raises estimates +1-7% on revenue (2-8% above the EBIT consensus FY27-30), but at $332bn of market cap, having rerated to 37x EV/EBIT two years out, concludes the upside "is well reflected in investor expectations"; normalizes valuation to 30x.
22 Jun
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley · J. Moore / Shane▲ bull
Shane prefers LAM + KLA among the big-3; KLA is "the way to play Intel" in US semicap (Intel capex recovery in 2027, this year's Intel spend more concentrated in process control). MS estimates WFE $149B/$191B for 2026/27.
22 Jun
Valuation / positionAlphaSense Deep Research · ASML primer◆ mixed
Bernstein peer comp: KLAC P/E FY25A/26E/27E = 52.8x / 47.6x / 34.3x (premium vs ASML/AMAT in FY26E); Bernstein sees KLAC as the "premium vehicle" on the structural growth of process-control and lower exposure to China replacement risk. Mizuho comp (LRCX primer) lists KLAC at CY27E P/E 35.6x, EV/FCF 42.0x.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

29 Apr
PRINT — earningsTeradyne · Q1 FY26 earnings call
Q1 FY26 (quarter ended Mar 29) — a record. Revenue $1,282.5M, +87% y/y / +18% q/q ($200M above the prior 2021 peak); GAAP EPS $2.53, non-GAAP $2.56; non-GAAP GM 60.9% (+370bps q/q); record operating margin 37.5%. Semi Test $1,110.8M (+104.8% y/y; SoC $882M ~75% AI-compute, Memory $203M), Robotics $91.3M (+32% y/y), Product Test $80.4M. AI ~70% of revenue. Q2 guide: revenue $1.15-1.25B, non-GAAP EPS $1.86-2.15, GM 58-59%.
4 May
Product / roadmapUBS · Arcuri / Shannon Poehlen / Gita Atalier▲ bull
UBS-Teradyne deep-dive on CPO/silicon photonics: real optionality — four test insertions, mid-term TAM of $300-700M, first-mover via Fi-Con Tech + Quantifi/Photon 100. Ramp weighted to H2.
18 May
Valuation / positionUBS · Tim Arcuri▲ bull
In the weekly preview, TER among the top picks at a higher ref: "Teradyne at 320... that's one I like a lot," a late-cycle laggard.
12 Jun
Guidance / numbersJPM · Samik Chatterjee◆ mixed
Preview: 2H improvement "nuanced positive" — stronger memory test demand → H2 upside; 1H still leads 2H on the VIP-compute split (~85/15 H1/H2). Google ASIC (VIP compute) qual finishing ~end-3Q, modest 4Q revenue and ramp in 2027; Intel merchant test shift is an out-year option. Read-through from Lumentum OCS outsourcing.
15 Jun
Product / roadmapUBS · Tim Arcuri▲ bull
"Teradyne... talking about being qualified for Google TPU — that's pretty good stuff" — incremental SoC-test design-win color for the ASIC ramp.
18 Jun
Valuation / positionSusquehanna · corporate access– neutral
TER on the test/semicap events calendar (corporate access).
22 Jun
Valuation / positionAlphaSense Deep Research · LRCX primer (Mizuho comp)◆ mixed
TER at CY27E P/E 38.2x / EV/FCF 49.8x / P/Sales 10.9x (~$60bn mkt cap) — the richest EV/FCF in Mizuho's semicap comp table.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

5 May
PRINT — earningsVeeco · Q1 2026 earnings call
Q1 2026: revenue $158M (within guide, but down y/y); a BIS export-license change removed ~$8M of LSA revenue from a Chinese customer; non-GAAP GM ~36%, non-GAAP EPS $0.14 (GAAP near-breakeven). China collapsed to $20M / 13% of sales (-72% y/y). Q2 guide: revenue $170-190M, GM 38-40%, EPS $0.20-0.32; FY26 reaffirmed $740-800M (+11-16% y/y). Swing factor: >$250M of MOCVD+wet+IBD orders for indium-phosphide laser manufacturing (silicon photonics), shipping from Q3'26 with Spectre IBD ~10x capacity by early 2027.
6 May
Valuation / positionCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Buy reiterated — 2027E upside in silicon photonics (briefing mention).
19 May
Valuation / positionJPMorgan · Tom O'Malley– neutral
"O'Malley updates post-print" mention (briefing) — post-results update.
12 Jun
Capital / M&AJPM Special Situations · Deal Survey of the Americas– neutral
Tracks the all-stock Veeco / Axcelis merger (merger-arb setup / ACLS proxy). Each VECO share converts into 0.3575 Axcelis shares; the last pending approval is China SAMR antitrust, close expected H2 2026.
19 Jun
Demand / capexCrux Capital (optics, Substack)▲ bull
Names Veeco among the "upstream tool suppliers (Aixtron, Veeco, Riber)" levered to the compound-semi/optics build.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

30 Apr
PRINT — earningsAIXTRON · Q1 FY26 IR call
Q1 FY26: revenue EUR 59m, -47% y/y (within the EUR 65m ±10m guide) on seasonality + weak power electronics; GM 18% (-12pp); EBIT EUR -22m. Signal in orders: intake EUR 171m, +~30% y/y, >2/3 optoelectronics — "clear inflection point" driven by Datacom lasers for AI data centers (copper→optical at 800G/1600G). Management raised FY26 guidance (mid-April) to revenue EUR 560m ±30m (was 520m), GM ~42%, EBIT margin 17-20%; Q2 guided EUR 110m ±10m. EUR 450m 5-year 0%-coupon convertible placed in April.
11 May
Demand / capexCapstone Morning Briefing▲ bull
Read-across from Q1/26 tagged "positive in Optical and Power."
19 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · Will Beavington▲ bull
"AIXTRON: The Multi-Year OPTICAL Play" — Buy, PT raised to EUR 73. US optical model has transceiver units +~5x 2025-2028 against AIXA's ~100% InP MOCVD share; +8%/+16% above the FY28 consensus on revenue/EPS, with GM upside on tool volumes + a growing G10 mix; opto >2/3 of order intake.
19 Jun
Demand / capexJefferies Global SEMIS Daily▲ bull
"AIXTRON — A Multi-Year A.I. Capex Play?". Crux Capital (optics) lists Aixtron among the upstream optical-tool suppliers.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

22 Apr
PRINT — earningsLam Research · Q3 FY26 earnings call
FQ3 FY26 (Mar): revenue $5.84B (+9% q/q, +24% y/y), above the midpoint — 3rd straight quarter of record revenue; GAAP GM 49.9%, operating margin 35%, record diluted EPS $1.47. DRAM hit an all-time high of 27% of systems (+45% y/y); advanced packaging guided to exceed +50% in CY2026; CSBG $2.1B (record). China 34%, Korea and Taiwan 23% each. FQ4 guide: revenue $6.6B ±$400M, GM 50.5%, EPS $1.65 ±$0.15. CY26 WFE raised to ~$140B (from $135B); a ~$40B NAND conversion wave with the majority of spend before the end of CY27. China guided down in the June quarter.
15 May
Competition / shareJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
OW all three large caps — "overweight... KLA, Lam, and Applied Materials." CY26 favors depo/etch (LRCX/AMAT), but CY27 rotates to the new-node leaders KLAC/ASML as sub-2nm takes off — a relative forward-revisions headwind vs KLA. NAND greenfield still absent (first one only in CY28). The buy-side bar on WFE is "very, very high" — AMAT sold off on the guide-up because expectations were already there.
18 May
Demand / capexUBS · Tim Arcuri▲ bull
"I like Semi Caps, AMAT, LAM. I've been pushing those now for a while" — LRCX+AMAT "the two stocks you want to own." Reads Lam's capacity actions as confirmation of a rising WFE ceiling: a 2nd Malaysia fab + discussion of a 3rd site = signal of WFE heading toward $250B CY28. UBS Buy, PT $200.
18 May
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley · Joe Moore▲ bull
MS upgraded LRCX/LAM to Overweight ("memory mismatch unresolved, LAM up to OW, AMAT down to EW"), reversing the prior UW — the fundamental skeptic turned constructive on Lam, placing it above AMAT (cut to EW).
29 May
Supply / capacityJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
Semis Spring Series: OW LAM among the top semicap picks; flags TSMC with "a lot of urgency... to pull tool orders ahead of previously discussed schedules" — LAM a named beneficiary of the pull-ins.
10 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · G. Vella▲ bull
"WFE Update: Early Innings" — constructive read-through on the WFE cycle; LRCX the most levered to memory capex of the trio.
22 Jun
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley · J. Moore / Shane▲ bull
Shane prefers LAM + KLA over AMAT; LAM seen taking "another strong market-share gain" next year, levered to NAND + an Intel CAPEX recovery (vs AMAT's DRAM WFE deceleration to mid-to-high-20s% in 2027). MS WFE $149B/$191B/+mid-teens for 2026/27/28.
22 Jun
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley · Tech Talk podcast (Joe Moore & Shane Brett, Ep. 310)▲ bull
Post-Asia-trip checks; chapter on "preference among AMAT, LRCX & KLAC." Anchors the MS rating — LAM Overweight (upgrade 2026-05-18), above AMAT (cut to EW); LAM the preferred depo/etch name alongside KLA.
22 Jun
Valuation / positionAlphaSense Deep Research · LRCX primer (Bernstein/Mizuho/expert calls)▲ bull
FQ3 FY26 non-GAAP GM 49.9% (vs 49.0% guide); June guide rev $6.60B / GM 50.5% / EPS $1.65 well ahead of the Street ($6.05B / 49.2% / $1.45 per Bernstein). SAM economics: the GAA transition ~doubles Lam's SAM/wafer vs 5nm (~$1B incremental per 100k wfr/mo) + ~$1B from backside-power; HBM ~$450M SAM per 100k wfr/mo. China: the BIS 50% affiliates rule ≈ ~$600M revenue headwind in CY26. Mizuho valuation: LRCX CY27E P/E 36.6x / EV/FCF 40.9x — Outperform PT $380 (bull $500 / bear $305); Bernstein Outperform PT $325.

SAMSUNGSamsung Electronics Co., Ltd.richQ1-2026 print → intra-quarter · Apr 30 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
14
events
8
bull reads
3
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

30 Apr
PRINT — earningsSamsung · Q1-2026 earnings call
Q1-2026 — a record quarter in AI memory. Revenue KRW 134tn (+43% q/q); OP KRW 57tn (+185% q/q); operating margin 43% (from 21%); EPS KRW 7,123. DS KRW 81.7tn (+225% y/y), memory KRW 74.8tn (+292% y/y). DRAM ASP +low-90s% q/q, NAND ASP +high-80s% q/q. HBM4 in mass production since Feb 2026, "fully booked," HBM sales >3x y/y in 2026; HBM4E samples in Q2. Foundry Taylor TX move-in on Apr 23, mass production 2027. 2026 capex with a "substantial increase."
7 May
Competition / share@paradislabs (FinTwit memory)▼ bear
FinTwit memory books underweight Samsung vs SK Hynix on the HBM/yield advantage; SK Hynix ~5.3x vs Samsung ~5.9x fwd P/E. Hyperscalers diversifying away from TSMC engage Samsung Foundry (AMD/Google/Tesla) for availability despite lagging yields.
8 May
Guidance / numbersJPM▼ bear
JPM flagged a union strike with 7-12% downside to 2026 OP (briefing).
11 May
Guidance / numbersJefferies · Jeff Kim▲ bull
Three engines firing from 2H26 — memory + foundry + robotics. Q2-26 OP KRW 100tn vs his estimate of KRW 84tn; "the cheapest it will get" at 4.7x '27 P/E. DRAM shortage "with no way out for the next few years."
21 May
Product / roadmapBofA · Sam Sivakumar (litho expert)▲ bull
High-NA EUV done well is "an advantage" via design simplification and ~75% fewer process steps vs low-NA double patterning; the half-field/stitching constraint is "not as serious as we thought"; DRAM "eminently suited" for high-NA — a structural opening for Samsung Foundry/memory to leapfrog TSMC (a slow high-NA adopter) if it executes.
28 May
Competition / shareJPM · Jay▼ bear
JPM's call that Samsung would close the gap with Hynix post-strike "didn't work" — Hynix kept outperforming on passive/single-stock ETF flows + an ADR catalyst. Samsung catalysts: a real start to HBM4 volume production, a larger AMD chiplet/foundry order at US Foundry Day (late-June/early-July), and a shareholder-return update (~October). Near-term EPS drag: the union-settlement provision (~12% of profit) hits Q2, ~2pts above JPM's prior 10%.
8 Jun
Valuation / positionGoldman Sachs · Memory (longevity of cycle)◆ mixed
5-6x earnings disbelieves the durability of the cycle; 9x would be "conservative" (briefing).
10 Jun
Margin / pricingex-Micron VP (expert call)◆ mixed
HBM commoditizes once 2nd/3rd vendors qualify, but Samsung's cost structure is actually better than Micron's — relatively advantaged in a snap-back, but still at the cyclical top. New capacity (Samsung/SK/Micron) arriving ~CY27-28 could break pricing.
16 Jun
Margin / pricing@QQ_Timmy (FinTwit)▲ bull
Server DRAM C3Q26 +30-40% q/q — DRAM ASP trajectory (a test of Jefferies' KRW 100tn OP estimate for Q2-26).
17 Jun
Product / roadmap@jukan05 (FinTwit)▲ bull
DRAM 1d (7th generation) mass production, earlier in 1H27 (briefing). The Korean tape rose +4.8% overnight on memory pass-through from Apple.
18 Jun
Competition / share@techfund1 (FinTwit)▲ bull
DRAM shortage "with no way out for the next few years"; foundry optionality is incremental upside as hyperscalers diversify away from TSMC — TSMC advanced-node capacity pre-booked into 2028+ pushes AMD, Google (Axion ~2028 / TPU), Tesla to engage Samsung Foundry.
19 Jun
Product / roadmapDigitimes▲ bull
Samsung Foundry signed a strategic deal to make 'Claros' power-management chips for AI data centers — AI-DC foundry traction.
19 Jun
Demand / capexCapstone Morning Briefing▲ bull
Memory super-cycle tape — a melt-up in conventional DRAM ASP + the HBM5 roadmap (2028-29); Samsung framed as the HBM4 catch-up story vs SK Hynix.
23 Jun
Supply / capacityStoneX/TrendForce · Memory expert call (Avril)◆ mixed
Samsung exits 2026 with the largest DRAM wafer capacity at ~740K wafers/month (vs SK Hynix ~610K, Micron ~365K), adding only ~45K in 2027 (clean-room is the constraint until end-2028); P5L phase-1 only ready mid-2028, phase-2 announced with output ~2030. NAND→DRAM conversion drives global NAND cap -3% in 2026 and Samsung loses NAND share. A real possibility Samsung overtakes SK Hynix as #1 in HBM in 2027. Anthropic's HBM deal (2028) with Samsung implies an in-house ASIC.
23 Jun
Margin / pricingRedburn · Conor O'Mara (Asia Trip Takeaways)▲ bull
HBM4 price still not finalized two weeks ago "despite Samsung already shipping," expected +90-100% vs last year's LTA. First disclosure that Samsung has a hybrid-bonding project and is "willing to offer it at a premium" (for taller stacks / HBM4E) — read bullishly by Besi holders. Samsung said that if wafers are short it would optimize the mix — pushing conventional DRAM at the expense of HBM (a TSV backend penalty), further limiting HBM capacity.

SKHYNIXSK hynix Inc.richQ1 FY26 print + intra-quarter · Apr 23 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
9
events
6
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

7 Apr
Margin / pricingJefferies · Conor▲ bull
Jefferies (Jeff Kim/KBI) argues Korea could out-earn Nvidia in 2027: HBM4 ~35% more expensive than HBM3E and Hynix HBM margins flat (not -20% as feared), with NVIDIA/hyperscalers absorbing the increase. The rally so far has been ASP-led and now needs to turn into unit growth in 2027.
9 Apr
Supply / capacityBofA · expert TSMC (Best ideas)▲ bull
The TSMC ecosystem supplies the logic base die beneath SK Hynix's HBM (Samsung joining the alliance); the expert described Jensen Huang as the supply chain's 'firefighter,' personally chasing Hynix capacity, corroborating Hynix as the primary HBM source on Rubin.
14 Apr
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley · Sean Kim◆ mixed
MS: news that Hynix is cutting HBM4 volume/share 'doesn't matter for EPS' — it is better not to have HBM because commodity DRAM returns are higher; but HBM matters for the multiple (DRAM ~4-5x peak earnings vs HBM ~10x). Lower HBM share is EPS-neutral-to-positive but multiple-negative. NAND seen as a more attractive industry than DRAM.
23 Apr
PRINT — earningsSK hynix IR / CNBC
Q1 2026: revenue KRW 52.6tn (+60% QoQ, +198% YoY), operating profit KRW 37.6tn (≈doubled QoQ), OP margin 72% (+13pp QoQ), net income KRW 40.3tn. Net cash KRW 35tn. AI demand drove 'significant price increases in DRAM and NAND'; HBM4 'far exceeding supply capacity.' NAND -10% QoQ off a high base.
28 May
Valuation / positionJPM · Jay▲ bull
JPM: the thesis that Samsung should 'catch up' to Hynix post-strike 'did not work'; Hynix's outperformance is tied to single-stock ETFs + Hynix-centric passive indexing, plus the confirmed US listing/ADR catalyst and HBM4 progress; retail is positioned in Hynix.
8 Jun
Valuation / positionGoldman Sachs · Junie Yoon (Memory)▲ bull
GS: cycle longevity is underestimated — Hynix at 5-6x earnings means the market disbelieves the durability; GS's 9x target is 'conservative.' Even a bear case of DRAM -30%/yr in 2027-28 leaves DRAM GM ~40%, supporting the earnings multiple.
10 Jun
Valuation / positionExpert call · ex-VP Procurement Micron▼ bear
Ex-VP of procurement at Micron: HBM becomes a commodity once 2-3 suppliers are qualified; 'your $900 stock goes back to $50' once capacity normalizes — a risk within CY26/early CY27.
19 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · Titu/Wong (briefing)▲ bull
JEF calls SK Hynix's ~20% pullback a buying opportunity; the KBI note argues Korean memory can out-earn the hyperscalers. DRAM ASP +50-60% QoQ in 2Q26, +20% QoQ in 3Q26; NAND +75-100% QoQ in 2Q. @rwang07: 'SK Hynix is the next $3 trillion company.'
23 Jun
Supply / capacityStoneX/TrendForce · Avril (Memory expert call)▲ bull
SK Hynix exits 2026 as #2 in DRAM at ~610K wafers/month (vs Samsung ~740K) but reaches 710K by end-2027, narrowing the gap. It remains dominant in HBM with the largest capacity (~200K end-2026 vs Samsung 180K, Micron 100K). Yongin FAB1 ready end-2027 but output only in 2028; FAB2 just announced. Risk: Samsung could overtake as #1 in HBM in 2027. Anthropic pursuing a deal (DRAM/eSSD 2027, HBM 2028).
23 Jun
Supply / capacityRedburn · Conor O'Mara (Asia Trip)◆ mixed
HBM supply for 2027 revised down (60→50bn Gb-eq) as conventional DRAM becomes more profitable; LTAs viewed as call options; wafer shortage.

Forward commentary & how it evolved · anchored on the 2 most recent references

Management commentary — how it evolved (by quarter)

ThemeQ2 2025 (2025-07-24)Q3 2025 (2025-10-29)Q4 2025 / FY25 (2026-01-29)Q1 2026 (2026-04-23)
Revenue / OP growthRecord rev 22.2tn (+26% QoQ); OP 9.2tnRev 24.4tn (+10% QoQ); OP 11.4tn, >10tn first timeRev 32.8tn (+34% QoQ); FY25 OP 47.2tn (+101%)Rev 52.6tn (+60% QoQ, +198% YoY); OP 37.6tn
OP margin41% (-1pp QoQ)47%58% (+11pp QoQ); FY25 49%72% (+13pp QoQ)
HBM cycle12-high HBM3E ramp; plan to double HBM YoY; HBM4 prepHBM4 mass production in Sept; ships Q4; 'sold out since 2023'HBM rev >2x YoY; HBM3E + HBM4 supplied simultaneouslyHBM4 ramp prep; demand 'far exceeding supply capacity'
DRAMExpanded sales; LPDDR server modules, GDDR7 24Gb128GB+ DDR5 shipments >2x QoQ; 'sold out 2026'1cnm full mass production; 256GB DDR5 RDIMM'Significant price increases'; customers prioritize volume
NAND / eSSDGrowth all apps; QLC eSSD, 321-high portfolioAI server eSSD at price premiums; 321-layer ramp321-layer QLC done; highest annual NAND rev on recordShipments ~-10% QoQ on high base; group eSSD ~$4.64bn
Capex / footprintPreemptive 2025 spend for smooth 2026 HBM supply2026 investment to increase vs 2025Capex up 'significantly YoY'; M15X, Yongin, Indiana—

KIOXIAKioxia Holdings CorporationmediumQ4 FY25 print + intra-quarter · Apr 16 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
7
events
6
bull reads
0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

16 Apr
Valuation / positionBofA · Mikio Hirokawa (Japan SPE)▲ bull
BofA 'too cheap to ignore': even after a ~50% rally, sees 'very little downside' — at ~¥30,000 the stock was at ~6x EPS FY3/27; with NAND prices +70-75% QoQ (TrendForce), EPS could exceed ¥7,000 → sub-5x P/E. Re-rate catalyst: Kioxia negotiating floor-price contracts (not fixed) to preserve the upside, taking the multiple from ~5x to ~10x.
15 May
PRINT — earningsKioxia IR / briefing
Q4 FY2025 (quarter ended Mar-2026): revenue ¥1,002.9bn (+84.5% QoQ); net income ¥407.7bn; non-GAAP OP ¥599bn vs consensus ¥519bn (+15% beat). The move was ASP-driven: ASP +100%+ QoQ (highest among NAND peers), bits -10% QoQ on maintenance. SSD & Storage ¥600.3bn (+99.8%). FY25: revenue ¥2.337tn (+37% YoY). CEO Oota: 'riding the wave of AI demand.'
17 May
Valuation / positionJPM · Mio Shikanai / Jay Kwon (briefing)▲ bull
JPM (OW) raised Street-high PT ¥38,000 → ¥80,000; EPS FY26-28E +63% to +80%, with ASP/bit +250%+ CY26E and OP 65-90% above consensus FY26E/27E; SSD mix ~60%.
18 May
Valuation / positionBofA/JPM (briefing)▲ bull
BofA/JPM team Buy, OP/EPS FY3/27 +52%/+54%; CY2026 with capacity sold out, LTAs (multi-year, prepay, penalties) being the 'this time is different' catalyst across the memory complex (Samsung/Hynix/Kioxia/MU).
28 May
Capital / M&AJPM · Mio (Memory)◆ mixed
Kioxia runs everything on 1-year LTAs today (~70% of the June quarter already fixed at +60-65% QoQ); multi-year begins next year. LTA priorities: cancel-fee/binding over prepayment, and floor price with no cap. Valuation debate migrating from ~3-5x to 7-8x (bulls at 10-11x). Bain overhang (~20%) limits buyback; dividend over buyback, minimum payout (10-20%).
2 Jun
Product / roadmapKioxia Investor Day▲ bull
Datacenter + enterprise targeted at >60% of revenue by FY2028; NAND market CAGR 20%+ (up to 46%) 2025→2028; BiCS Gen.10 (332 layers) sampling summer-2026, mass production within a year; capex averaging ¥470bn/yr FY26-28; ROIC >60%; capital return up to 50% of net cash flow with progressive dividends from FY2027; OP margin guided ~74%.
23 Jun
Product / roadmapRedburn · Conor O'Mara (Asia Trip)▲ bull
Conor's biggest reason to prefer Kioxia over SanDisk: Super IO (XL Flash) — ultra-low-latency SLC NAND for agentic AI — shipping 10M units this year vs 3M in the past, targeting 100M next year, with NVIDIA as a partner. SanDisk's competing HPF only arrives in 2028. Caveat: trade-ratio penalty for being SLC.
23 Jun
Product / roadmapStoneX/TrendForce · Avril (Memory expert call)▲ bull
QLC is the most critical product for high-density enterprise SSD (the tightest-supply segment); Kioxia and Solidigm 'doing very well' in QLC mix, while YMTC has only 13% QLC (confined to consumer) — a structural quality moat for Kioxia at the high end.

SNDKSanDisk CorporationmediumQ3 FY26 print + intra-quarter · Apr 30 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
6
events
4
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

30 Apr
PRINT — earningsSanDisk · Goeckeler/Visoso (Q3 FY26 call)
Q3 FY26 (quarter ended Apr 3, 2026): revenue $5.95B (+97% QoQ, +251% YoY), well above the $4.4-4.8B guide; non-GAAP GM 78.4% (vs 51.1%; guide 65-67%); non-GAAP EPS $23.41. Bits flat YoY and -high-teens QoQ — the move is all price/mix. Data center $1.467B (+233% QoQ). Five NBMs signed, $42B of minimum contractual revenue, >$11B of guarantees, covering >1/3 of FY27 bits. Q4 guide: rev $7.75-8.25B, GM 79-81%, EPS $30-33; $6B buyback authorized.
4 May
Capital / M&ABernstein · expert ex-WDC NAND VP Rob (HDD/NAND)▲ bull
Mechanics of NBM enforceability: ~$11B of guarantees on ~$42B (~25%) — in a severe downturn, a customer that tears up the deal still leaves SanDisk capturing ~25%, so a -50% market price translates to an effective ASP of only ~-25%. In practice 'tearing up' = renegotiating for more years at a lower price. All high-capacity drives (128/256TB) are QLC, where SanDisk + Kioxia 'have done very well.'
22 May
Supply / capacityJPM (Conf feedback)▲ bull
JPM corroborates the LTA layer at TMC: SanDisk extended the undersupply call from end-CY26 to end-CY27; the three signed LTAs carry floor-and-ceiling pricing and pre-pay-style walkaways with financial guarantees; BiCS 8 (200L+, TLC+QLC) executing well.
1 Jun
Valuation / positionGoldman Sachs (briefing)▲ bull
GS went into 'full bullish mode' on memory stocks, citing MU + SNDK.
3 Jun
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Joseph Moore (briefing)▲ bull
MS (OW) raised PT to $1,750 (from $1,100), up +59%; SNDK repeatedly cited in GOOG's $80B AI supply-chain map for NAND (ParadisLabs).
22 Jun
Margin / pricingMorgan Stanley · Joe Moore (Memory & semicap)◆ mixed
MS rates NAND fundamentals '8 of 10' vs DRAM '10/10'; SanDisk has become 'everyone's preferred investment' and is the price-setter ('being the bad guy,' Koreans pricing just below), but MS still prefers Micron because SanDisk looks more expensive on 2028 numbers. Some 2028 LTA lock-in prices are down vs 2027 ('materially' in some cases, esp. enterprise TLC). Q3 NAND pricing seen +20-30% QoQ.
23 Jun
Competition / shareRedburn · Conor O'Mara (Asia Trip)▼ bear
Conor prefers Kioxia over SanDisk on the latency angle for agentic AI: SanDisk's HPF (its answer to Kioxia's Super IO/XL Flash, in design with Global Unichip) only ships in 2028, by which point Super IO SLC will have been shipping for ~2 years. Direct read: 'if Super IO works, HPF is dead on arrival.'

WDCWestern DigitalmediumQ3 FY26 print + intra-quarter · Apr 30 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
3
events

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

30 Apr
PRINT — earningsWDC (Q3 FY26 call)
Q3 FY26: revenue $3.3B (+45% YoY), above the top of guide; non-GAAP EPS $2.72 (~2x YoY); non-GAAP GM 50.5% (+1,040bps YoY). Cloud $3.0B (89% of rev, +48% YoY). Volume 222 exabytes (+34% YoY); price per TB +9% YoY. HAMR in qualification with four customers (44TB). LTAs extend into CY2028-2029. Q4 guide: rev $3.65B ±$100M, GM 51-52%, EPS $3.25; dividend +20% to $0.15.
26 May
Margin / pricingJPM (Networking)▲ bull
JPM (read from the TMC conference): pricing is the biggest incremental upside — neither WDC nor Seagate tried to temper price-increase expectations; from the current ~+9% QoQ level, 'mid-teens increases in 12 months could be.' WDC is more price-driven (cost declines limited to ~-10%/exabyte) vs Seagate more cost-driven; both reiterated no unit additions (growth only via density).
15 Jun
Margin / pricingMorgan Stanley · Erik Woodring ('HDDs: The Next Leg Higher')▲ bull
Pricing upside idea in nearline (Industry View Cautious, but the long thesis is pricing). HDD vendors targeting ~$25/TB blended in CY27 and ~$30 in CY28 vs ~$14.50/TB today; non-LTA customers already paying ~+30% QoQ and NeoCloud spot at $30-35/TB.
16 Jun
Margin / pricingMorgan Stanley · Erik Woodring (equity call)◆ mixed
Woodring raised numbers 'materially again' on Asia checks: demand broadening from cloud to AI training/inference, humanoids and robots; supply limited to ~30-35% annual exabyte growth, elongating the cycle. WDC CY27/CY28 EPS 'a little over $45 and a little over $70' if the checks hold — sub-10x in CY28. Keeps Seagate as top pick (WDC 1B) since WDC has not yet qualified HAMR and only ramps 44TB in H1 CY27.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

28 Apr
PRINT — earningsSeagate (Q3 FY26 call)
Q3 FY26 (March quarter, beat-and-raise): revenue $3.1B (+44% YoY, +10% QoQ); non-GAAP GM 47% (+480bps YoY); non-GAAP EPS $4.10 (+115% YoY); FCF $953M, the highest in more than a decade; net leverage 0.7x; Fitch upgraded to investment grade. Exabytes 199 EB (+39% YoY) on flat units. Mozaic 4 began shipping; 75% of large cloud customers on Mozaic. Nearline almost fully allocated through CY2027; top-3 CSP RPO ~$1.1T. Annual revenue growth target raised to 'at least 20%.' Q4 guide: rev $3.45B, EPS $5.00.
12 Jun
Margin / pricingJPM · Samik Chatterjee (equity call)▲ bull
JPM/Samik: ~90% sequential incremental gross margin, STX guiding opex ~$300M/qtr (operating leverage) and 'no need to give back price on generation changes' in a constrained market.
15 Jun
Margin / pricingMorgan Stanley · Erik Woodring ('HDDs: The Next Leg Higher')▲ bull
Nearline pricing escalators + HAMR ramp; Seagate the front-runner in HAMR (MS top pick). Vendors targeting ~$25/TB in CY27 and ~$30 in CY28 vs ~$14.50/TB today.
16 Jun
Margin / pricingMorgan Stanley · Erik Woodring (equity call)▲ bull
Seagate top pick in HDDs; 'raised numbers materially, again' on Asia checks. STX CY27 EPS = $52 (base), $66 (bull); 'if we took our checks as fact... more than $70 in CY27, more than $100 in CY28' → sub-10x in CY28 bull. Idiosyncratic edge: Google's 'sweetheart' HAMR contract should reprice higher from June to Sept-26, and STX ramps 40TB HAMR aggressively in 2H CY26 vs WDC only in H1 CY27.
18 Jun
Competition / shareGoldman Sachs · Peter Callahan (briefing)▼ bear
GS desk 'firmly WDC > STX,' with WDC +48% in June vs STX 'only' +27%.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

20 May
PRINT — earningsADI (FQ2 FY26 call) / Barclays desk
FQ2 FY26 (quarter ended 2026-05-02): revenue $3.62bn (+15% QoQ, +37% YoY), above the top of guide; non-GAAP EPS $3.09 (record, +67% YoY); non-GAAP GM 73.0% (+360bps YoY); op margin 49%. Industrial $1.80bn (+56% YoY); Automotive $872m (+2% YoY, BMS back to growth); Comms $555m (+79% YoY, DC ~75% of comms, +>90% YoY). FQ3 guide ~$3.9bn / EPS ~$3.30 vs Street ~$3.62bn/$3.01. Announced Empower Semiconductor for $1.5bn all-cash. Desk: 'good enough but not a strong catalyst for a very well-owned name; a knife fight at elevated expectations.'
21 May
Valuation / positionJefferies · Blayne Curtis / Citi · Atif Malik / MS · Joseph Moore / DB · Ross Seymore▲ bull
PT walk post-print: Jefferies raised to $475 Buy ('Cooking with Gas,' record bookings, comms +90% YoY in DC); Citi to $460 Buy (28x C27 ~$16, Empower adds DC-power share in '27); MS to $428 OW (9th consecutive quarter above seasonal, 'more room to run'). DB maintains Hold $365 — 'firing on all cylinders but the premium valuation already reflects it.'
23 May
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur (briefing)▲ bull
JPM calls ADI its 'top pick in the broad-based diversified semis segment,' PT $450 (from $400), OW — industrial recovery broadening beyond ATE/A&D, auto on track for double-digit YoY in FY26, Empower closing the vertical-power gap for AI accelerators.
29 May
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur (Semis spring update)▲ bull
JPM names ADI among its top OW picks in broad-based analog/MCU (with TXN/MCHP), reading the April earnings season + post-earnings checks as confirmation of a synchronized cyclical recovery; CY27 EPS for ADI/TXN/MCHP already revised up ~23% over two earnings seasons, with positive pricing actions possibly 'firing' in 2H26.
3 Jun
Demand / capexJefferies · Menon (Analog Semis) / Barclays · Rand▲ bull
Jefferies: the AI-datacenter demand pull is tightening analog/MOSFET/MCU capacity (creating shortage), naming TI/ADI as participants with 'lots of datacenter revenue.' Barclays: the long-TXN / short-ADI pair-trade gaining traction — TXN earlier in the recovery with more GM torque and a chance to 'double' DC-power exposure vs ADI's already among the highest.
8 Jun
Competition / shareExpert call · Tier-1 automotivo▼ bear
Automotive Tier-1 expert judges TI gaining vs ADI in channel support / FAE / lead times — 'TI in general capturing the market much more and better than ADI, especially through its involvement in the automotive sector.'
9 Jun
Demand / capexBofA · Vivek Arya (TMT Analog Semis Call) / briefing▲ bull
BofA: analog up-cycle dynamics; ADI the premium franchise and the long-TXN/short-ADI pair-trade is the live debate. BofA pegs the analog AI-DC TAM at ~$8bn (2025) → ~$27bn by 2030 (~28% CAGR), ADI among the beneficiaries.
15 Jun
Product / roadmapCiti · Atif Malik (800V power)▲ bull
Citi frames Empower as the key pure-play in integrated voltage regulators (IVR) — the 2028+ co-packaged power-delivery architecture — with ADI guiding initial IVR design wins in 2H26; the deal 'could strengthen its position in the power-delivery market for next-generation platforms.'

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

22 Apr
PRINT — earningsTXN earnings call
Q1 FY26 (qtr. ending Mar-31): revenue $4.825bn (+19% YoY/+9% QoQ), above the guide; GM 58% (+210bps QoQ); diluted EPS $1.68 vs $1.36 midpoint. Industrial +30% YoY, data center ~+90% YoY. Capex cut to $2-3bn in 2026 (Lizardi); TTM FCF $4.4bn. Q2 guide: revenue $5.0-5.4bn, EPS $1.77-2.05.
12 May
Valuation / positionR&Co Redburn · Adley▼ bear
TXN on the 12m relative-high list, with semis ~63% above the 200-day average, "the widest margin since the dot-com bubble" — a technical overbought signal (briefing).
12 May
Margin / pricingR&Co Redburn · Adley▲ bull
Redburn Buy sees 5-15% price increases on mature nodes broadening out, plus AI-DC supply tightness as support for GM (briefing).
20 May
Valuation / positionGS · Schneider▼ bear
GS reiterates Sell on TXN (PT at ~32x normalized EPS of $13); reads ADI's strong print as a risk that pushes TXN estimates higher against the Sell thesis — valuation is the bear (briefing).
20 May
Demand / capexBarclays/Rand desk▲ bull
TXN is the favorite long in analog — earlier in the recovery than ADI, more positive revisions ahead, with strong GM torque if the cycle is real (briefing).
26 May
Demand / capexBofA · Vivek Arya▲ bull
BofA Buy: AI sales potentially >3x to $4.5bn long-term; best-positioned analog share gainer in the 800V data-center era (briefing).
29 May
Guidance / numbersJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
JPM keeps TXN overweight top pick in the broad-based segment (with ADI/MCHP); CY27 EPS already revised +23% across ADI/TXN/MCHP over the last two seasons, with 2H26 price actions possibly kicking in ("Semis spring update").
3 Jun
Margin / pricingJefferies · Menon▲ bull
TI will raise prices again in July, its 2nd (possibly 3rd) increase this cycle — far faster than in 2021; names TI/ADI/Infineon well positioned in China since analog/MCUs/MOSFETs see "very little" Chinese investment, leaving the catalog structurally insulated from indigenization ("Analog Semis").
3 Jun
Valuation / positionBarclays/Rand desk▲ bull
Buy-side consensus trade is long TXN / short ADI; first call with new CFO Julie Knecht on the Q2 print (~Jul-22); DB neutral on the CFO transition (briefing).
8 Jun
Competition / shareExpert call · auto Tier-1▲ bull
Tier-1 auto expert assessed TI gaining vs ADI — "TI capturing the market much better than ADI, especially through its involvement in the automotive sector"; named TI (with NXP) customizing to OEM requirements vs Qualcomm's higher-margin standardized parts.
15 Jun
Competition / shareCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Citi reiterates TXN top pick in the 800V transition, expecting DC-power share gains in 2H26 vs Infineon/MPWR/Renesas; internal high-voltage GaN capacity has quadrupled since Oct'24. Citi raised PT $280→$345 (30x 2028E) and EPS C26/27/28 +1%/+1%/+6% to $8.03/$10.10/$11.42; largest GM-expansion opportunity (>10pp) with ~85% of wafers internal ("800V power").
15 Jun
Demand / capex@bubbleboi▼ bear
Pushback on the 800V/DC tailwind: checks with DC-construction PMs show "no plans to do this anytime soon," TXN "especially vocal," a 2-3 year lag before it reaches earnings, and China may flood GaN/SiC as it did with InP (briefing).
17 Jun
Valuation / positionBofA-type investor survey▲ bull
Investor survey shows analog preference shifting toward IFX 25% / TXN 25%, displacing ADI 13%; reinforces the crowded long TXN / short ADI (briefing; Barclays/Rand).
22 Jun
Margin / pricingAlphaSense · IFX Primer▲ bull
Peer benchmark quantifies TXN as the margin leader: FY25 GM 57.0%, EBIT 34.1%, ROIC 17.4% — the highest in the analog/power set (vs IFX 39%/10%, STM 34%/1.5%, ON 33%/1.4%, NXP 55%/24.8%); US analog giants (TI/NXP/ADI) return 80-100% of net income ("IFX Primer").

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

29 Apr
PRINT — earningsQCOM earnings call
Q2 FY26 (qtr. ending Mar-29): revenue $10.6bn (-3% YoY); non-GAAP EPS $2.65 (top of guide). QCT $9.1bn (Handsets $6.02bn -13% YoY; Auto $1.33bn +38%; IoT $1.73bn +9%); QCT EBT margin compressed to 27%. GAAP net income $7.4bn inflated by a $5.7bn tax benefit (valuation allowance release), not operating strength. Q3 guide: revenue $9.2-10.0bn, EPS $2.10-2.30; China handsets expected to bottom in Q3.
30 Apr
Competition / shareCiti · Malik◆ mixed
Citi reads the first custom-silicon win as likely an LPU inference chip "very likely with someone like Google," consistent with Marvell also having an LPU program — hyperscalers engaging ASIC providers beyond Broadcom/MediaTek ("MSFT C1Q26 recap").
30 Apr
Valuation / positionCiti · Malik▼ bear
Citi: estimates are coming down (70% of sales still smartphone; QCOM missed the September quarter on smartphone weakness), but the stock re-rated up on multiple expansion (from ~12x) purely on the custom-silicon teaser — with short interest rising and data-center detail held back until the Jun-24 investor day ("MSFT C1Q26 recap").
6 May
Competition / share@SKundojjala▼ bear
AMD overtook QCOM in semi revenue in 1QC26 with the gap "widening from here" (briefing).
13 May
Valuation / positionTae Kim newsletter▼ bear
The rally was called the "problem child" of the chip complex — +80% off the lows seen as overdone given the DC ambition; QCOM -11% on May-13 (briefing).
15 May
Demand / capex@fundaai▲ bull
A server-CPU thesis circulated on the buy-side (CPU:GPU ratio heading toward 1:1 in agentic AI; lead times stretching) as optionality for QCOM (briefing).
22 May
Product / roadmapJPM · Samik Chatterjee▲ bull
At JPM's TMC conference, QCOM struck a "very bullish tone" on the DC compute TAM, outlining four sockets: (1) custom ASIC (the announced hyperscaler win, ramping late-2026 into 2027, "like MediaTek on Google TPU"), (2) CPUs (ARM IP), (3) accelerators, (4) Alphawave connectivity; wins at US and China hyperscalers, multi-billions in 2027 ("Conf feedback").
8 Jun
Competition / shareExpert call · auto Tier-1▲ bull
German Tier-1 expert described QCOM as having "a monopoly in certain product lines," with OEMs "forced to work with Qualcomm"; named QCOM (with Infineon) as one of the only ones compatible with ASIL-D zonal controllers (NXP/STM "have no chance"). Standardized higher-margin model, 15-20% price premium vs NXP/Chinese players.
23 Jun
Demand / capexRedburn · Conor O'Mara▲ bull
Amid the broad CPU/compute shortage, checks in Asia see no signs of QCOM cutting orders — "any compute is good" — with QCOM also marketing its own ARM servers ("Conor O'Mara Asia Trip Takeaways").

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

6 May
PRINT — earningsARM earnings call
Q4 FY26: revenue $1.49bn (+20%); License & other $819M (+29%); Royalty $671M (+11%, light vs Street $691M); non-GAAP EPS $0.60 (record); FY26 $4.92bn (+23%). Data-center royalty more than doubled YoY again; AGI CPU launched with Meta as lead, ~$2bn of demand across FY27+FY28. Q1 FY27 guide: revenue $1.26bn ±$50M (~+20%), EPS $0.40 ±$0.04. Stock +13% on the session (Capstone briefing).
7 May
Guidance / numbersExpert call · SiTime/ARM▲ bull
Post-Q4, ARM guides royalty and licensing both +20% YoY in fiscal Q1 (Jun) and across FY27; cloud/AI royalty ~2x YoY; cloud (data-center) share reached ~50% on ARM's metric (from ~zero in late-2020), with NVDA's Grace/Vera the big driver. AGI backlog "basically doubled in six weeks" but is memory-constrained; hyperscaler roadmap for a 700-core chip; server-CPU TAM internally pegged at $200-300bn by 2030.
7 May
Demand / capexPatrick Moorhead▲ bull
ARM holds ~50% CPU share among top hyperscalers (Graviton/Axion/Cobalt/Vera all ARM); Meta as AGI lead (briefing).
11 May
Competition / shareArm CEO @ GCP Next▲ bull
Axion actively displacing x86 host CPUs on Google TPU 8t/8i — "50% less power, 80% improvement over x86" (briefing).
18 May
Demand / capexBernstein · David Dai▲ bull
Bernstein initiates at Outperform PT $300: agentic AI = ~120M CPU cores/GW (~4x a traditional data center); ARM at ~50% CPU share among top hyperscalers; CPU:GPU ratio from ~8:1 (training) to ~1:1 (agentic/inference).
21 May
Demand / capexUBS · Sunny Lin▲ bull
UBS "Quantifying the server CPU opportunity": ARM's server-CPU unit share rises from 16% (2025) to 42% (2030) as agentic AI lifts CPU-bound orchestration work; total server-CPU market reaches ~$170bn by 2030 (units 23m→63m, 30% CAGR); AMD 24%→29%, Intel 60%→29%.
22 May
Competition / shareCiti · Blayne Curtis▲ bull
Asia checks: ARM's storyline is more levered to companion CPUs (fixed CPU:GPU ratio in AI servers, ~99% ARM in GB-class racks). CPU mix at Google up to ~60% ARM (Axion, above expected) and Graviton ~60% of AWS's general-purpose mix; NVDA's ~$20bn standalone Arm-CPU figure (Vera) is "chips, not memory" ("Asia recap").
1 Jun
Product / roadmapCapstone briefing▲ bull
PC optionality: NVDA RTX Spark / N1X Arm PC with Microsoft; ARM +12% pre-market (briefing).
15 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Arcuri▼ bear
Arcuri skeptical on the bull case's royalty math: investors model 50c-$1 per core, but the big winners pay far less — NVIDIA pays "substantially below 50 cents per core, well below," as do the large hyperscalers; the core-count × royalty math is "too high." Also discounts AGI-CPU demand because most customers (Meta + OpenAI) "don't do what they say they're going to do" ("Arcuri call").
17 Jun
Valuation / positionBernstein · Rasgon▲ bull
PT raised $300→$500, Outperform: server-CPU TAM revised to $223bn; ARM targeting $22bn of revenue by 2030, FY31E EPS raised from $9.83 to $11.79. Bernstein also raised SoftBank to OP ¥11,200 (from ¥8,200) on the same revision (briefing).
17 Jun
Valuation / positionRedburn▼ bear
Redburn Neutral PT $130, the most bearish house: raises revenue only on better-than-feared smartphone, but bakes in just $1bn of AGI CPU over the next 18 months and explicitly does NOT include the guided $15bn of 2030 AGI without confirmed contracts; sits 9-19% below consensus on FY29-31 revenue; ARM trades at ~48x EV/sales two years out, a steep premium vs peers (briefing).

NXPINXP Semiconductors N.V.mediumQ1 FY26 print + intra-quarter · Apr 28 → Jun 22, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

28 Apr
PRINT — earningsNXPI earnings call
Q1 FY26 (qtr. ending Mar-29): revenue $3.18bn (+12% YoY, -5% QoQ), $31m above the midpoint; non-GAAP GM 57.1%, op margin 33%, EPS $3.05. Auto ~$1.78bn +6% YoY (content/SDV, not units); I&IoT ~$628m +24% YoY; Mobile ~$391m +16%; Comm Infra ~$380m +21%. Data center >$500m guided for 2026 (vs ~$200m in 2025). Q2 guide: revenue $3.45bn (+18% YoY), GM 58%, EPS $3.50.
20 May
Demand / capexGS · Schneider▲ bull
GS framed strong analog results as positive for the group including NXPI, reading NXP positively in the analog recovery alongside TXN/ADI/ON (briefing).
20 May
Valuation / positionJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
JPM groups NXPI into its OW semi baskets (briefing).
22 May
Guidance / numbersJPM · Conf feedback▲ bull
At JPM's TMC conference, NXP guided to double-digit-% growth in CY26 and CY27 as the order book builds (auto/industrial/data-center drivers), and explicitly said there has been no major restocking activity yet — a tailwind still to come ("Conf feedback").
26 May
Valuation / positionR&Co Redburn▼ bear
NXP appeared on Redburn's 12m relative-high list alongside the broader semi/analog complex — stretched positioning (briefing).
3 Jun
Margin / pricingJefferies · Menon▲ bull
NXP announced July price increases alongside TI and Infineon; Jefferies groups NXP (auto MCUs) among the analog franchises structurally insulated from Chinese indigenization, which targets only high-growth categories (SiC/GaN/GPUs), not MCUs/MOSFETs/analog ("Analog Semis").
8 Jun
Competition / shareExpert call · auto Tier-1▲ bull
German Tier-1 procurement expert flagged NXP as a clear share gainer: NXP and Infineon "doing well across the whole pipeline" (vs Renesas more susceptible), NXP with "the most cost-competitive products" and the most aggressive pricing; in auto analog ICs NXP has "much better traction than the others." Sees analog IC demand +10-15% YoY in Q2'26, book-to-bill 1.1-1.2, CY2026 demand +15-20%.
8 Jun
Supply / capacityExpert call · auto Tier-1◆ mixed
Expert judged NXP's gains to be more pricing/supply-driven (less durable) than design-in lock, and flagged ICs and MOSFETs as the families most constrained on material availability; lead times back to ~8 weeks standard, channel inventory now lean/just-in-time (Expert call).
15 Jun
Margin / pricingCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Citi notes NXPI joining IFX/TXN in announcing a second round of price increases effective Jun/Jul, and that NXPI's fab utilization recovered toward the optimal mid-80s% ("800V power").
22 Jun
Margin / pricingAlphaSense · IFX Primer◆ mixed
Primer frames NXP as the margin-resilience standout of the set: FY25 EBIT 24.8% (down only ~275bps from the FY22 peak) and ROIC 11.6%, on a higher-value auto-MCU mix. However it quantifies auto-MCU share loss to IFX (IFX share rose from 10% in 2019 to 36% in 2025, "mainly at the expense of Renesas and NXP"); NXP is a 10% partner in the EUR 10bn ESMC Dresden JV ("IFX Primer").

MCHPMicrochip Technology IncorporatedmediumQ4 FY26 print + intra-quarter · May 07 → Jun 22, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
6
events
4
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

7 May
PRINT — earningsMCHP earnings call
Q4 FY26 (ended Mar-31-26): sales $1.311B (+10.6% QoQ, +35.1% YoY), above the top of the guide; non-GAAP EPS $0.57 ($0.07 above the midpoint); non-GAAP GM 61.6% (incl. $46.6M of underutilization), op margin 30.6%. Inventory: DOI 185 days (-16 QoQ), distributor 26 days (all-time low); April was the largest bookings month in ~4 years, book-to-bill well above 1.0. "70-80% of process nodes constrained" (Sanghi). FQ1 FY27 guide: sales +11% QoQ (±1%), EPS $0.67-0.71 (~19% above Street).
8 May
Valuation / positionMS · Moore▼ bear
MS reiterates Equal-Weight PT $94 — frames the DC disclosure as in-line and the price hikes as "defensive, not offensive," and flags MCHP's DC sub-segment as "not dissimilar from peers in size/growth but behind TXN and ADI" (briefing).
8 May
Valuation / positionCapstone briefing▲ bull
Sell-side stance on the print: GS reit Buy (Schneider); UBS reit Buy $85 (Arcuri); JPM "Recovery Inflection"; Citi Buy (Malik); Barclays constructive; MS EW $94 (Moore). FQ4 guide +11% with EPS ~19% above consensus "validates the re-rating call" (briefing).
29 May
Guidance / numbersJPM · Harlan Sur▲ bull
At JPM's TMC, MCHP reiterated that April was the largest bookings month in nearly 4 years, with momentum continuing into May; distribution inventory at just 26 days, restocking underway, many direct customers returning after 12-18 months. MCHP is holding price to preserve design momentum but did not rule out a broader round of increases for 2H/next year; JPM keeps OW top pick; CY27 EPS for the broad-based trio +23% over two seasons ("Semis Spring Series").
2 Jun
Demand / capexCapstone briefing▲ bull
Data-center breakout and price hikes; a read-through anchor for the analog complex (ADI/ON/NXPI/STM/IFX/POWI) — peers raising DC guides on the same inflection (briefing).
8 Jun
Competition / shareExpert call · auto Tier-1– neutral
Tier-1 auto expert described Microchip demand as "consistent," with no clear share loss, affecting the product lines / new products it launches (Expert call).
15 Jun
Margin / pricingCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Citi confirms MCHP announced a broad-based price increase starting in June (alongside IFX/TXN/NXPI/ON). Citi's cycle work frames the upside still ahead for MCU-heavy names: the microcontroller cycle has lasted only ~4 quarters (vs ~7 for analog), industry MCU revenue still ~20% below the prior peak and units ~23% below — MCU is the laggard category yet to inflect ("800V power").

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

4 May
PRINT — earningsON earnings call
Q1 FY26 (qtr. ending Apr-03): revenue $1.51bn (-1% QoQ/+5% YoY), above the midpoint; non-GAAP GM 38.5% (3rd consecutive quarter of expansion); non-GAAP EPS $0.64. GAAP net loss of $33.4m (-$0.08) on $329m of restructuring/impairment. PSG $737m (+14% YoY); AMG $540m (-5%); ISG $236m (+1%). Auto $797m (flat QoQ, +5% YoY — ends a seven-quarter decline). AI data center +30%+ QoQ, doubled YoY; guided to double again in 2026. Utilization 77% (up from 68%). Q2 guide: revenue $1.535-1.635bn, GM 38-40%, EPS $0.65-0.77.
11 May
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley– neutral
MS marks ON Equal-weight (ref $50.78); onsemi is in coverage but not a featured name in the scale-up/800V thesis (MS scale-up primer).
3 Jun
Margin / pricingJefferies · Menon▼ bear
Jefferies' analog analyst is structurally negative on the discrete-power exposure that defines ON's PSG — SiC "is a pretty bad industry... while you're playing in silicon carbide, it's going to be negative for your margin," and silicon IGBTs a "terrible and horrible industry" being killed by China — vs silicon MOSFETs/analog/MCUs that he likes ("Analog Semis").
8 Jun
Competition / shareExpert call · auto Tier-1▼ bear
German Tier-1 procurement expert: onsemi demand "definitely hasn't increased as much" vs peers, held back by a lack of new innovations / new product lines and an inability to meet OEM requirements (Expert call).
8 Jun
Valuation / positionFinTwit/crowd basket– neutral
onsemi is in the 800V DC crowd basket at ~$46.2bn mkt cap, #2 behind Infineon (briefing).
15 Jun
Competition / shareCiti · Atif Malik◆ mixed
Citi keeps onsemi Neutral with a 90-day upside Catalyst Watch into the Sep-16 Analyst Day (expects a raised target model + renewed SiC momentum). Citi raised PT $100→$120 (20x 2028E) and EPS C26/27/28 +1%/+12%/+9% to $3.07/$4.64/$6.00; sees ON (with TXN) with the largest GM-expansion opportunity (>10pp) given ~65% of wafers internal; the only name in coverage developing vertical GaN (sampling now, shipment 2H27). ESS has grown ~40% CAGR over 5 years with ~60% share. Citi's 800V top pick, however, is TXN, not ON ("800V power").
15 Jun
Demand / capex@bubbleboi▼ bear
Channel checks push the 800V earnings impact out 2-3 years — "every DC-construction PM... no plans to do this anytime soon," and China may flood SiC/GaN as it did with InP (briefing).
17 Jun
Supply / capacity@trendforce▼ bear
Chinese SiC/GaN vendors and broader indigenization challenging IDM incumbents; potential oversupply/price pressure (briefing).
22 Jun
Margin / pricingAlphaSense · IFX Primer▼ bear
Primer quantifies the operating-leverage downside: ON's FY25 EBIT margin collapsed to 1.4% from the FY23 peak of 30.8% — the most severe compression in the analog/power set — "illustrating the intense operating leverage inherent to discrete power-semiconductor manufacturing" ("IFX Primer").

ANETArista NetworksrichQ1 FY26 · May 05 → Jun 15, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23

events
9
bull reads
2
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

5 May
PRINT — earningsANET · Q1 FY26 earnings call
Q1 FY26 print: revenue US$2.709B (+35.1% YoY, above the US$2.6B guide); non-GAAP GM 62.4%; op margin 47.8%; diluted EPS US$0.87 (+31.8% YoY, vs ~US$0.808 consensus); record OCF US$1.69B.
5 May
Demand / capexANET · Q1 FY26 earnings call▲ bull
Ullal: "Our demand is actually the best I have ever seen in my Arista tenure"; demand exceeds supply across wafers, silicon, CPUs, optics and memory with lead times of ~52 weeks. Purchase commitments raised to US$8.9B (from US$6.8B).
5 May
Guidance / numbersANET · Q1 FY26 earnings call▲ bull
Guidance raised: FY26 revenue to US$11.5B (+27.7%) from US$11.25B/25%; AI fabrics target US$3.5B (from US$3.25B, >2x prior year); campus US$1.25B; GM 62–64%, op margin ~46%. Q2 FY26: revenue ~US$2.8B, EPS ~US$0.88.
5 May
Supply / capacityANET · Q1 FY26 earnings call◆ mixed
Deferred-revenue acceptance cycles stretched to 6–8 quarters vs 2–4 historically; component lead times 38–52 weeks, but management says supply is "addressed" via purchase commitments with no revenue constraint.
6 May
Valuation / positionCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Citi (Atif Malik) Buy, PT cut to US$176 on the supply constraints.
8 May
Margin / pricingJPM · Networking recap (Samik/Joe)▲ bull
On the post-print callback ANET sounded better on GM than the confusion of the public call implied: expects resilient GM ~62–63%, second-half benefit from the Q1 price increases, and is comfortable passing through the higher Tomahawk 6 cost next year — which Celestica is NOT.
8 May
Supply / capacityJPM · Networking recap (Samik/Joe)◆ mixed
What ANET called Broadcom "de-commits" is actually shipment delay ("committed for the quarter but didn't get it"), not a technical de-commit — a difference in language.
8 May
Guidance / numbersJPM · Networking recap (Samik/Joe)▲ bull
With the guide at 28%, JPM sees 30%+ for FY26 as readily achievable given the track record of ~10-pt upgrades through the year; 35% is where the bull case moderates; deferred revenue is the call option JPM "gave up trying to predict."
12 May
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Meta Marshall▲ bull
MS (Meta Marshall) raised FY25–28 revenue CAGR to ~20%+ from mid-teens and called the ~20% post-print pullback "overdone"; OW.
22 May
Valuation / positionBarclays · desk read◆ mixed
Barclays desk: "well debated but… investors are positive, just some disagreement on how quickly deferred will turn into revenue."
4 Jun
Demand / capex650 Group · Alan Weckel▲ bull
650 Group (Alan Weckel): agentic/MoE inference pushes the front-end from "one port per 80 GPUs to one to four"; data-center switching is a market with "40%+ growth this year… well north of US$100B by end of decade", "not a winner take all". Scale-across goes from ~US$500M to a US$10B market.
4 Jun
Competition / share650 Group · Alan Weckel▼ bear
650 Group: Cisco Silicon One closed the gap to ~1–2 quarters in 800G and supply resilience may be a durable advantage; NVIDIA stronger in neo-clouds; HPE/Nokia gaining share.
8 Jun
Valuation / positionBofA · Tal Liani (conference wrap)▼ bear
Tal Liani (BofA): across the three big networking names, backlog/deferred "tripled, quadrupled" over 4 years; as Oracle/Microsoft builds finish in 2H26 "these backlogs will decline… unless the orders are extremely strong" — explicitly flags "because of the valuation."
8 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Samik Chatterjee◆ mixed
JPM ran a note "ANET: The Lost Charm" and an AMA "How to Get Its Groove Back" — relative underperformance vs AI-infra peers; 2027E EPS ~US$5 referenced.
12 Jun
Supply / capacityBofA · optical supply-chain call▲ bull
BofA reads ANET's constraint as temporary: ex-deferred, underlying 1Q shipments were >50% higher YoY. Implied high-rate switching wins at Google (Virgo) and Anthropic are behind the commentary even though ANET did not name them.
12 Jun
Product / roadmapCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Citi flagged the launch of the 1.6T rack-scale portfolio with Meta/Microsoft/Oracle + AMD/AVGO silicon; 1 platform launches in Q4'26, 2 in Q1'27.

CIENCiena CorporationmediumQ2 FY26 · Jun 04 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
9
events
7
bull reads
2
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

19 May
Valuation / positionRothschild/Redburn · Mike (optical call)▼ bear
Redburn's Mike loves the story but "can't get my numbers high enough to justify the share price" — even assuming a US$7B scale-across TAM and ~50% Ciena share by 2030 he barely reaches the price; stock at ~2.5x PEG, range-bound.
4 Jun
PRINT — earningsCIEN · Q2 FY26 earnings call
Record Q2 FY26 print: revenue US$1.57B (+40% YoY), US$71M above the top of the guide; optical +42%; Routing & Switching +88% on the DCOM ramp; direct cloud +70% YoY; adjusted GM 44.9% (+90bps vs guide); adjusted EPS US$1.64 (~4x YoY); FCF US$219M.
4 Jun
Guidance / numbersCIEN · Q2 FY26 earnings call (Marc Graff)▲ bull
Backlog US$7.7B (+US$600M QoQ), ~US$6.4B hardware, ~80% deliverable within 12 months — "visibility well beyond historical norms". Guidance raised for the 3rd time this FY: FY26 revenue US$6.3B ±US$100M (~+32% YoY), GM 44.5–45%; Q3 ~US$1.625B.
4 Jun
Product / roadmapCIEN · Q2 FY26 earnings call (Gary Smith)▲ bull
Industry-first multi-rail (RLS Hyper-Rail) order from a leading hyperscaler — "hundreds of millions over multiple years", linear revenue in '27; second DCOM order + a third in qualification; competitive coherent-module takeaway at a large hyperscaler. TAM framed to ~double to ~US$50B by 2029.
5 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Samik Chatterjee▲ bull
JPM (Chatterjee) OW, PT US$635, ~90% EPS CAGR FY25-28E.
5 Jun
Valuation / positionCiti · Atif Malik▲ bull
Citi (Atif Malik) PT raised to US$658 on the print.
5 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Vogt▼ bear
UBS (Vogt) Neutral — "85x NTM = misaligned expectations", limited upside on supply/margin.
12 Jun
Supply / capacityBofA · optical supply-chain call▲ bull
BofA frames optical-sector fundamentals as stronger than HDD: optical companies sign LTAs of up to 5 years and add unit/InP capacity, while the HDD duopoly adds no capacity; customers are now locking up supply rather than chasing demand visibility.
12 Jun
Margin / pricingCiena · optical equity call▲ bull
Margins compounding: vertical integration (WaveLogic 6 + Nano "everything in there is Ciena"), 250+ cost-reduction programs, pricing/value-exchange and a richer Hyper-Rail/DCOM mix — 45% GM is a "waypoint, not the destination". Targets >2x pluggable revenue vs 2025 and #1 or near-#1 in 800G share exiting the year.
23 Jun
Margin / pricingCiti · Atif Malik (Asia Investor Meeting)▲ bull
Citi hosted CFO Marc Graff + IR Patti Trautwein in Tokyo and Taipei. Buy, PT US$658. Early-stage scaling story: revenue ~+32% and EPS nearly triples; TAM doubling to ~US$50B by 2029; GM heading toward upper-40s with a path to 50%+ (product re-engineering + mix); operating leverage from R&D reuse + a concentrated hyperscaler base lowering S&M.

COHRCoherent Corp.mediumQ3 FY26 · May 06 → Jun 17, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
11
events
6
bull reads
3

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

6 May
PRINT — earningsCOHR · Q3 FY26 earnings call
Record Q3 FY26 print: revenue US$1.8B (+7% q/q, +21% y/y reported; +9% q/q, +27% y/y pro forma); non-GAAP GM 39.6% (+57bps q/q); op margin 20.3%; non-GAAP EPS US$1.41 (+55% y/y). DC&Comms +>40% y/y; communications +60% y/y on DCI/scale-across. Record backlog, orders out to CY2028 and LTAs to the end of the decade.
6 May
Demand / capexCOHR · Q3 FY26 earnings call (Anderson)▲ bull
Anderson called the June quarter "a new inflection point" and reiterated FY27 growth > FY26. Demand "exceptionally strong with no signs of attenuation", visibility into CY2028, multi-year LTAs that combine upfront customer capex + demand commitments.
6 May
Guidance / numbersCOHR · Q3 FY26 earnings call (Luther)▲ bull
Q4 FY26 guide: revenue US$1.91–2.05B, GM 39–41%, non-GAAP EPS US$1.52–1.72 — implied acceleration. 6-inch InP pulled forward: internal capacity doubles by year-end (one quarter early), then >2x again by CY27; OCS TAM raised to >US$4B; CPO TAM >US$15B.
6 May
Margin / pricingCOHR · Q3 FY26 earnings call (O'Malley/Barclays Q&A)▼ bear
Gross-margin fall-through seen as disappointing given the 6-inch ramp and exits — incremental GM only ~44% y/y; "margins leave more to be desired" (O'Malley/Barclays question).
7 May
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley · Meta Marshall (optical)▼ bear
MS sees Coherent as less in control of its own capacity than Lumentum — "it's just taken a little bit longer to ramp". MS preference order: Lumentum > Corning > Coherent > Ciena. EW PT raised to US$330. Space trades ~25x CY28 EPS post-NVDA.
8 May
Margin / pricingJPM · Networking recap▲ bull
JPM: Coherent guiding a quarter ahead on the 2x InP ramp for cal-26, then another 2x in cal-27; high-teens data-center revenue growth message in the June quarter, no pushback on acceleration to ~30%+ in FY27; 6-inch becomes >50% of InP volume from the September quarter, when it contributes materially to GM.
9 Jun
Product / roadmapJPM · "Watts Up! Shift to 800V"– neutral
Coherent cited among Wolfspeed's SiC power-semi peers (Infineon, Mitsubishi, Navitas, onsemi, PI, ROHM, STMicro) in the 800V wide-bandgap opportunity (WBG US$1bn→US$5bn+ by 2030) — a peripheral optionality mention; the equity story remains optics.
10 Jun
Valuation / positionbriefing · FinTwit/JPM▼ bear
Stock fell ~11% on 2026-06-10 on CPO-delay concerns (JPM framed it as overdone).
11 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Samik Chatterjee▲ bull
JPM (Samik Chatterjee) OW, "buy the dip".
12 Jun
Margin / pricingBofA · optical supply-chain call▲ bull
Coherent affirms a path to >42% GM via 1.6T mix, 6-inch wafers, more internal sourcing and industrial pricing. Every product on 6-inch (EMLs, CW lasers, photodiodes) yields better than the prior generation; demand "quite strong" with limited double-ordering; likely NVDA ELS outcome = Coherent + Fabrinet as the two suppliers.
15 Jun
Supply / capacitybriefing · @crux_capital_ / FinTwit◆ mixed
InP substrate is a structural China supply risk — the CEO reportedly traveled to China with a Trump delegation seeking InP. FinTwit treats COHR as a "hold + accumulate" durable winner.
17 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · investor meetings (NYC)▲ bull
JPM investor meetings in NYC (Anderson + IR Silverstein) — management access post-selloff, read-through to sentiment.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

6 May
PRINT — earningsLITE · Q3 FY26 earnings call (Hurlston/Ali)
Record Q3 FY26 print: revenue US$808.4M (+90% YoY, above the guide midpoint); non-GAAP GM 47.9% (+540bps QoQ, +1,270bps YoY); op margin 32.2%; non-GAAP EPS US$2.37 (GAAP US$1.50). Cash US$3.17B post-NVIDIA.
6 May
Supply / capacityLITE · Q3 FY26 earnings call (Hurlston)▲ bull
EML supply-demand imbalance ">30%" (undershipping ~30% of customer demand and widening even as capacity is added); targeting >50% EML unit growth by Dec-Q 2026; record EML shipments +100% YoY. Pump-laser constraint cited as the "biggest… somewhat unanticipated" bottleneck.
6 May
Guidance / numbersLITE · Q3 FY26 earnings call (Ali)▲ bull
Q4 FY26 guide: revenue US$960M–1.01B, non-GAAP op margin 35–36%, non-GAAP EPS US$2.85–3.05 (~102M shares). OCS: multi-year, multibillion-dollar purchase agreement; backlog well past US$400M, the majority shipping in 2027, three customers.
6 May
Valuation / positionBofA · Vivek Arya▼ bear
BofA (Vivek Arya) Neutral, PT US$1,100 — best growth in the sector but "upside well priced in" (CY26/27E +18%/+9%).
7 May
Supply / capacityJPM · LITE mgmt call (Hurlston/Yuen/Liu)▲ bull
"Scale-across" pump-laser components today at mid-US$10s (~US$40–60M/qtr), guided to 3–4x output/revenue over the next 4–6 quarters; LITE holds 70–80% of the pump-laser market and similar in ITLAs, "essentially the kingmaker in terms of allocation" — pump supply behind demand by ~50–60%, more than EMLs, with mid-double-digit % price increases.
7 May
Competition / shareJPM · LITE mgmt call (Hurlston/Yuen)▲ bull
LITE reached the "front of the line" at the primary transceiver customer (Google), with 100% share on some key 1.6T SKUs near-term (more temporal than the OCS moat — Innolight/Coherent catch up in 1–2 quarters). Google's new Virgo back-end network raises optics attach per TPU.
7 May
Margin / pricingJPM · LITE mgmt call▼ bear
LITE feeding ~20% of the transceiver mix with its own CW lasers (in response to a supplier's CW-laser decommit), borrowing from InP capacity and modestly moderating EML revenue growth; transceiver manufacturing/margin is still the weak spot.
8 May
Guidance / numbersJPM · Networking recap▲ bull
OCS inflection in the Sep-Q quantified: OCS ~US$70M in the June quarter steps up to ~US$170M/qtr in 1H-FY27 (≈US$100M of sequential growth from OCS alone), making September the primary revenue driver; Dec-Q OCS ≥US$200M, ideally ~US$250M.
8 May
Valuation / positionJPM · Meyers (briefing)▼ bear
JPM (Meyers): "Lots of irons in the fire; execution the concern" — execution is the central worry.
11 May
Demand / capexLITE · IR call▲ bull
Scale-across demand is not just Ciena/Meta — Nokia, Cisco, Molex and Ciena are all placing orders, splitting the network back-end across a DCI framework. The charge-pump line (~US$30M/qtr run-rate) doubles next quarter, at margins better than even EMLs. First scale-up CPO demand (NVL576/Feynman) starts around early cal-28.
13 May
Valuation / positionbriefing · @ResearchQf▼ bear
@ResearchQf: stock "most extended vs 4yr avg", 20–40% correction plausible.
15 May
Supply / capacityMorgan Stanley · morning briefing▲ bull
MS: "Sold out through 2028" possible; LITE expects to fill its entire order book through 2028 within two quarters.
18 May
Demand / capexJPM · Samik fireside w/ Hurlston▲ bull
Hurlston: "there's no demand problem, which is an enviable situation… our issue is execution". At ~US$805M in Q3 they are ~halfway to the OFC target of US$2B/qtr revenue / 40% OM in 18–24 months. Substrate "mostly under control" via Sumitomo but "tighter than I'd like"; equipment (reactors, etch) now the inhibitor.
20 May
Valuation / positionBarclays · Rand (briefing)▼ bear
Barclays (Rand): sentiment "a lot more mixed than a few months ago… pushback on huge EPS numbers".
26 May
Product / roadmapJPM · Networking notes▲ bull
NVDA scale-out CPO adoption now tracking >50% vs ~40% a few quarters ago. Non-NVIDIA customers seeking to lock up the remaining Greensboro fab capacity, pressuring NVDA to make further fab investment for visibility.
11 Jun
Product / roadmapbriefing · JEF (Beavington) / MS / JPM◆ mixed
JEF (Beavington) flagged a CPO OIO pushout to 2030-31 on interposer overheating; MS (Marshall) and JPM (Chatterjee) call CPO concerns "overblown" and a buy-the-dip. FundaAI framed Hurlston's comments at Mizuho as "a supply problem, not demand".

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

7 May
PRINT — earningsAAOI · Q1 FY26 earnings call
Q1 FY26 print: revenue US$151.1M (+51% Y/Y, +13% Q/Q) — 4th consecutive record quarter, in line with the US$150–165M guide; non-GAAP GM 29.2%; non-GAAP loss/share US$(0.07). Data center US$81.4M (+154% Y/Y); 800G only US$4.6M (ramp still ahead); CATV US$66.8M (+24% Q/Q); cash US$449.4M; capex US$68.7M.
7 May
Demand / capexAAOI · Q1 FY26 earnings call (Thompson Lin)▲ bull
FY26 raised to >US$1.1B revenue with >US$140M non-GAAP op income. Management explicit that FY26 is capacity-gated, not demand-gated — Thompson Lin put true demand at US$1.4–1.5B, internal target US$1.2B. By mid-2027, ~US$471M/month of DC transceiver revenue (~780k transceivers/month).
7 May
Supply / capacityAAOI · Q1 FY26 earnings call▲ bull
The 2H'26 ramp is a step function, not linear — Q3 and Q4 each +60–80% Q/Q as Texas capacity comes online. Capacity: ~150k/mo 800G+1.6T this quarter, >650k/mo by YE26 (~30% Texas), >930k/mo by YE27 (>50% Texas). 1.6T contributes late-2026 (Lin: "more than US$2B for 1.6T transceivers" next year).
8 May
Valuation / positionbriefing · Crux Capital / Gaetano▼ bear
Crux Capital/Gaetano: "$30B or $3B?" — "plenty of execution risk: capacity must expand dramatically + capex ramp + dilution on table + margins improve + new orders qualified + qualified orders ship on time".
8 May
Demand / capexbriefing · @BenBajarin◆ mixed
@BenBajarin: the market needs to believe the 2027 ramp extends into a much larger base in 2028, not a one-year hyperscaler pull-forward.
12 May
Valuation / positionbriefing · Jim Cramer (CNBC)▼ bear
Jim Cramer (CNBC): the optical names are "parabolic… not going to be good for the market".
14 May
Valuation / positionbriefing · ParadisLabs / Rothschild◆ mixed
ParadisLabs ranks AAOI behind LITE/COHR on order-book scale + commercial readiness. AAOI appeared on Rothschild's "12m relative highs" list.
19 May
Demand / capexbriefing · Crux Capital / Gaetano◆ mixed
Crux Capital/Gaetano: "the story is there, but we need the execution follow-through".
26 May
Competition / share650 Group · networking research◆ mixed
650 Group: AAOI named among the new entrants in the merchant laser market (alongside MACOM, Semtech) as the CPO laser opportunity scales from the low hundreds of millions to ~US$1B; the flip side is explicit oversupply/glut risk as everyone adds laser capacity.
1 Jun
Capital / M&Abriefing · FinTwit▼ bear
Insider selling noted.
17 Jun
Demand / capexbriefing · Rosenblatt checks / FinTwit▲ bull
Game-theory leg: AMD actively chasing CW laser supply (flagged in Rosenblatt checks) → once NVDA/AMD lock LTAs, AMZN/MSFT "must follow".
18 Jun
Valuation / positionbriefing · @aleabitoreddit (Serenity)▲ bull
@aleabitoreddit (Serenity): ran from "$30 → $170/$175" and frames US$471M/month = ~US$5.6B ARR against a ~US$13.5B market cap in H1'27, "largest US transceiver supply chain for mass production of 800G/1.6T", with the bigger inflection in 2028.

26 Mayreport650 Group — networking research
GLWCorning IncorporatedmediumQ1 FY26 · Apr 28 → Jun 12, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
8
events
5
bull reads
0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

28 Apr
PRINT — earningsGLW · Q1 FY26 earnings call (Schlesinger)
Q1 FY26 print: sales +18% YoY to US$4.35B, EPS +30% to US$0.70 (both at the top of the guide); op margin +220bp to 20.2%; GM 39.1%; ROIC 13.5%; FCF US$188M. Optical US$1.8B (+36% YoY), net income +93% YoY. 8th consecutive quarter of YoY sales growth.
28 Apr
Demand / capexGLW · Q1 FY26 earnings call (Weeks)▲ bull
On the up-to-US$6B agreement with Meta, Corning concluded two more hyperscaler LTAs of similar size/duration and is working on others. Carrier had its best quarter in years in FTTH + DCI; the Lumen agreement was expanded/extended.
28 Apr
Guidance / numbersGLW · Q1 FY26 earnings call (Schlesinger)▲ bull
Q2 2026 guide: sales ~US$4.6B (+14% YoY), EPS US$0.73–0.77 (+25%), absorbing ~US$0.07 of solar-wafer drag. FY CapEx ~US$1.7B. Springboard: vs the Q4 2023 base, +33% sales / +79% EPS / +390bp op margin; the plan was raised to +US$11B of annualized sales by 2028, to be extended to 2030 at the May 6 event.
28 Apr
Margin / pricingGLW · Q1 FY26 Q&A (Weeks, w/ Wamsi Mohan/BofA)▲ bull
Weeks: would let optical ROIC exceed the glass businesses, and gross/profit margins could "eclipse" Display. Hyperscaler/Nvidia LTAs use prepayments + take-or-pay to de-risk capex, so Corning grows in AI without a cash-flow dip.
6 May
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Meta Marshall◆ mixed
MS (Meta Marshall) ranks GLW #2 in the optical basket behind Lumentum on "control of its own destiny"; the May 6 day "certainly exceeded" reduced expectations. Post-Nvidia GLW trades ~30x CY2028 vs ~25x Lumentum/Coherent — a premium to the optical pack. Would be "more aggressive buyers on down days". Order: Lumentum > Corning > Coherent > Ciena.
7 May
Competition / shareJPM · LITE mgmt call (Hurlston read)▲ bull
Lumentum's CEO characterized the NVDA-Corning deal as a large purchase agreement similar in structure to LITE's laser deal but without the ~US$2B equity backend — reads Jensen "buying fibers" as a sign NVDA is preparing to "own the wiring of scale-up clusters", more fiber in the racks and a shift from copper/Amphenol connectors to glass inside the rack.
12 May
Product / roadmapCorning · IR optical/Nvidia call▲ bull
The Nvidia partnership (announced before May 6) adds a multibillion-dollar upfront prepayment, a ~US$3.2B warrant for Nvidia to take equity, 10x US connectivity capacity, +50% fiber, and anchors the US$10B Photonics build. The DCI target of "US$1B medium term" looked conservative to some vs the US$1B Arista already has.
19 May
Valuation / positionJPM · TMT conf– neutral
GLW present at JPM's TMT conference (2026-05-19); buyback cadence ongoing.
9 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Samik Chatterjee◆ mixed
JPM moved to Neutral, PT US$185, framed around the Photonics TAM and ~US$8 of 2030E EPS, noting an Amazon deal is already "baked in Springboard" — the shift in stance reflects valuation/expectations catching up to the post-May 6 high, not a demand call.
