Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

13 May
PRINT — earningsCisco · Q3 FY26 earnings
Q3 FY26: record revenue of $15.8B (+12% YoY), above the top of guide; product +17%, services -1%. Non-GAAP EPS $1.06 (+10%); non-GAAP operating margin 34.2%. Total product orders +35% YoY (SP/cloud +105%).
13 May
Demand / capexCisco · Robbins/Patterson (Q3 FY26)▲ bull
Hyperscaler AI orders of $1.9B in Q3 (vs $600M prior year); YTD $5.3B already exceeds the prior $5B FY26 target. Management raised FY26 AI orders to ~$9B (4.5x FY25) and ~$4B of AI revenue. Acacia >$1B of orders in Q3.
13 May
Margin / pricingCisco · Patterson (Q3 FY26)◆ mixed
Non-GAAP GM 66.0% (-260bps YoY); product GM 64.3% (-330bps) on mix + memory cost. Patterson: GM 'stabilized' (Q4 guide midpoint also ~66%); hardware mix ~30% vs software ~1% is the biggest drag, more than memory.
13 May
Guidance / numbersCisco · Q3 FY26 guidance▲ bull
Q4 FY26 guide: revenue $16.7-16.9B, EPS $1.16-1.18, op margin 34-35%. FY26: revenue $62.8-63.0B, EPS $4.27-4.29. Patterson previews a FY27 frame with >=$6B of hyperscaler AI revenue.
13 May
Capital / M&ACisco · Patterson (Q3 FY26)◆ mixed
Restructuring announced (~4,000 roles / ~5% of headcount) — charge of up to $1B pre-tax ($450M in Q4), explicitly a reallocation toward silicon/optics/security/AI, not a cost-savings plan.
14 May
Guidance / numbersGS · Mike Ng (briefing)▲ bull
GS sees a F2027 framework with revenue >$68B.
14 May
Valuation / positionCiti · Atif Malik (briefing)▲ bull
Citi Buy PT $112 at 22x; calls the campus refresh 'early innings'; EPS FY26-28 +3/7/8%.
14 May
Valuation / positionMS · Meta Marshall (briefing)▲ bull
MS Overweight PT $120 — 'AI revenue at 4x FY26, >=50% YoY FY27.'
15 May
Demand / capexGS · Armbrust/Ng (briefing)▲ bull
GS frames the print as 'Validation of Hyperscaler Capex' + 'Networking as the New AI Bottleneck,' reducing the AI air-pocket fear.
15 May
Product / roadmapBofA · Sammy (IR call)▲ bull
Cat 4k/6k EOL is the structural driver of the campus refresh — Cat 4k EOL/EOS ends late-CY26, Cat 6k late-CY27 (6k installed base much larger, 2027 is the bigger wave); ~$40B of aged campus gear in scope, upsell with software/security.
15 May
Product / roadmapBofA · Sammy (IR call)◆ mixed
Half of Q3 AI orders was optics (incl. Acacia), half systems; P200 design wins are NOT in the updated $9B number; team now selling G300 (102.4T) with no design win/order yet. CPO view turned more constructive at OFC on improving yield.
15 May
Valuation / positionBarclays · Tim Long (briefing)▼ bear
Barclays maintains Equal-Weight (PT raised $76→$121) — 'AI Orders Drive Beat and Raise, But Sustainability More of a Question'; stock ~25x CY27 EPS vs ~15x historically; prefers ANET (OW PT $195) in the campus/AI pair.
16 May
Valuation / positionfirstadopter/Cramer · Robbins (briefing)▲ bull
Pushback on the dot-com bubble analogies; buyers are profitable incumbents (Robbins).
3 Jun
Valuation / positionRedburn (briefing)– neutral
Redburn at Neutral.
4 Jun
Competition / share650 Group · networking▲ bull
650 Group: CSCO is one of three volume-scale DC switch players alongside NVDA and Arista/Celestica; has parallel supply vectors vs the Broadcom ecosystem and is 'one of the best-managed supply chains.' 'Very bullish' on the 1.6T PAM4 receiver — first organic optic inside the DC, a multibillion-dollar opportunity.
12 Jun
Product / roadmapBofA · CIEN optical supply chain▲ bull
BofA: Cisco optical/systems inflection and Silicon One traction across the optical supply chain.

Management commentary — how it evolved (by quarter)

CRDOCredo Technology Group Holding LtdrichQ4 FY26 · May 22 → Jun 19, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
10
events
8
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

22 May
Demand / capexCiti · Blaine Curtis (Asia checks)◆ mixed
Citi makes CRDO a top pick ('controversial,' which he likes). The bear point that sank the stock: BizLink (AEC cable partner) guided a soft first half with XA (large customer) down; offsets — (1) Credo ramping a 2nd AEC source and (2) DSP package-and-test volumes +~50-60% in Q1/Q2, still implying beat-and-raise.
26 May
Demand / capex650 Group · networking▲ bull
650 Group constructive on the AEC market — 'a lot of robust growth ahead.' CPO 'both overhyped and much further out in time'; copper lasts much longer than expected; 400G-per-lane could still be copper. ZeroFlap optics and ALCs (micro-LED) open a new market.
1 Jun
PRINT — earningsCredo · Q4 FY26 earnings
Q4 FY26: revenue $437M, +7% q/q, +157% YoY — only ~$5M above the Street (narrow beat at the top). Non-GAAP GM 68.3% vs ~64.9% cons (large margin beat); op margin 49.6%; FCF $177.5M. Non-GAAP EPS $1.16 vs ~$1.02 cons (+14%, all from margin). FY26: revenue $1.3B (+206% YoY).
1 Jun
Guidance / numbersCredo · Q4 FY26 guidance▲ bull
Q1 FY27 guide: $465-475M, GM 67-69%. FY27: revenue >80% YoY (raised from >50% on the Q3 call), GM ~flat, net margin ~50%. Optical raised to >$600M FY27 (from >$500M), 3 legs each >$100M and +80%+ YoY; FY28 optical floor ~$1.2B. Dust Photonics funded with ~$750M of cash.
1 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Joshua Meyers (desk)▼ bear
JPM desk: stock -11.5% — revenue and guide both came in below buyside bars; 'frontier model operator' slowing deployments; 'show-me territory for a while.' ~$200 = 22.2x F28E $9. The market repriced the quality of the beat, not the size.
1 Jun
Product / roadmapJefferies · Blayne Curtis▲ bull
Jefferies Buy, PT $225→$270: 'AEC far from over, but optical is the real driver'; FY27 optical >$600M, ~doubles in F28; upside case $13 EPS C28, PT $390.
1 Jun
Product / roadmapBarclays · O'Malley▲ bull
Barclays OW $260: optical DSPs the biggest upside surprise; FY28 optical ~$1.6B.
1 Jun
Margin / pricingGS · Schneider▲ bull
GS Buy: 'GM beating expectations' — non-GAAP GM ~68% with op margin ~50% and net margin guided ~50% FY27, rare for a connectivity-IC name.
2 Jun
Valuation / positionSell-side PT table (briefing)▲ bull
Post-print PT table uniformly long: Jefferies $270 · JPM (Cardoso) $250 · Barclays $260 · GS Buy · SIG Positive; stock sold off ~15% post-print on high buyside bars.
19 Jun
Competition / share@crux_capital_ (briefing)▲ bull
@crux_capital_ puts CRDO in the 'strongest' connectivity bucket (copper+optical) alongside SMTC/ALAB/MTSI/MXL/MRVL, vs pure-play optical (LITE/AAOI/FN/CIEN) exposed to China oversupply.
19 Jun
Valuation / positionRothschild (briefing)▲ bull
CRDO at 12-month relative highs per Rothschild.

ALABAstera Labs, Inc.mediumQ1 2026 · May 5 → May 20, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
8
events
7
bull reads
0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

5 May
PRINT — earningsAstera Labs · Q1 2026 earnings
Q1 2026: revenue $308.4M, +14% q/q, +93% YoY; above the $286-297M guide. Non-GAAP EPS $0.61, non-GAAP GM 76.4% (+70bps q/q), non-GAAP op margin 36.2%. PCIe Gen6 >1/3 of revenue; Scorpio X-Series in early volume production. Content per accelerator >$1,000 and rising.
5 May
Guidance / numbersAstera Labs · Q1 2026 guidance▲ bull
Q2 2026 guide: revenue $355-365M (+15-18% q/q); non-GAAP GM ~73% (incl. ~200bps drag from the Amazon warrant/agreement); non-GAAP EPS $0.68-0.70. Management guides Scorpio to be the largest product line by Q4 2026.
5 May
Product / roadmapAstera Labs · Gajendra (Q1 2026)▲ bull
Scorpio X scale-up fabric switches now in production; UALink switches (higher ASP than PCIe) arrive in 2027 alongside Amazon + AMD ASIC/GPU launches — the 'key inflection point.'
6 May
Valuation / positionMS · Joseph Moore▲ bull
MS raised PT $210→$240, Overweight — 'buy & ramp before the product cycle kicks in'; scale-up (Scorpio) as the growth leg.
6 May
Demand / capexBofA · Vivek Arya (briefing)▲ bull
BofA positive PO change with 'AI fabric ramp strengthening.'
6 May
Demand / capexJPM · Harlan Sur (briefing)▲ bull
JPM maintained rating, 'strong R/G driven by AI demand.'
19 May
Demand / capexJPM · Sur (TMC feedback / briefing)▲ bull
At JPM's TMC conference, Astera signaled continued strong demand for fabric switching (within-rack) and copper AEC (scale-out), plus a new driver — memory tightness + higher memory/storage intensity in inferencing creating opportunities for ALAB's CXL/PCIe-switching. Stock +16% on the week. JPM (Sur) keeps ALAB as overweight top pick in small/mid-cap semis.
20 May
Competition / shareRothschild · Adley (TMT Daily)◆ mixed
ALAB/CRDO in focus; Redburn (Harrison) Neutral ('Tripping the Light Fantastic') as a cautious counterpoint — less conviction in optics than pure-plays.
22 May
Demand / capexCiti/Jefferies · Blaine (Asia recal)▲ bull
'The way to play the Trainium trade' — with Trainium server production ~flat in Q2 (Trn2→Trn3 transition) and Blaine cautious that MRVL 'isn't paid on every unit,' he points to Astera as the cleanest Trainium expression; 'there's a big underappreciated trend in scale-up content-per-accelerator,' but notes the stock 'went crazy.'

CEGConstellation Energy CorporationthinQ1 FY26 · May 11 → Jun 16, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
5
events
2
bull reads
2
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

4 May
Demand / capexJPM · DC broker call (Chris)◆ mixed
DC broker: the 'big four' hyperscalers get what they want, Oracle is the IG credit swing and neoclouds need backstops — counterparty credit, not just power, gates the contracting; ~30% of the pipeline is genuinely grid-islanded BTM. Read-through: sharpens the 'deals slip / BYOG threatens visibility' bear.
11 May
PRINT — earningsConstellation · Q1 FY26 earnings
Q1 FY26: adjusted operating EPS $2.74, a beat vs consensus $2.54 and +$0.60 YoY; GAAP EPS $4.49. FY26 guidance reaffirmed at $11.00-12.00/share (midpoint $11.50), with base-earnings growth 'above 20% through 2029.' Repurchased ~1.2M shares at ~$285 ($335M) under the $5B authorization.
11 May
Demand / capexConstellation · Dominguez (Q1 FY26)▲ bull
Hyperscaler capex in 2026 cited as 'nearly 75% higher than last year.' ~5,000 MW of new resources (nuclear uprates, gas, battery) submitted to PJM's interconnection queue. FCF guided at $8.4B for 2026-2027 and $11.5-13B for 2028-2029.
27 May
Demand / capexWells Fargo · Aman Chakri (BTM expert)▼ bear
BTM expert (ex-GE Vernova): behind-the-meter is a permanent solution, not a bridge (grid reconnection raises public tariffs; ISOs 'pick their battles') — read-through to the BYOG threat to the visibility of CEG's contracted fleet.
5 Jun
Demand / capexWells Fargo · midstream Friday wrap▼ bear
Midstream DC expert puts BTM at ~40% of power deals — reinforces the bear on IPPs/generators ceding share of DC contracting.
16 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · AI Capex 2.0▲ bull
JPM models 138 GW of US DC capacity growth through 2030 (up from 122 GW in Nov-2025) within $5.5tn of AI capex; power remains the most critical constraint. Deal table corroborates the Constellation-Meta Clinton contract, 1.1 GW, 20 years starting June 2027. EPRI: DCs could reach 9-17% of US electricity demand by 2030.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

7 May
PRINT — earningsVistra · Q1 FY26 earnings
Q1 FY26: ~$1.494B of adjusted EBITDA, a record for a calendar Q1, +~20% YoY and +~85% vs Q1 2024. Generation $1.426B; Retail $68M (deliberately down YoY against a mild ERCOT winter). Fitch upgraded VST to investment grade in Q1, the 2nd agency at IG after S&P.
7 May
Guidance / numbersVistra · Burke (Q1 FY26)– neutral
Management reaffirmed FY26 guidance for adjusted EBITDA and FCF-before-growth and held the FY27 EBITDA midpoint opportunity range — both EXCLUDE the pending Cogentrix deal and the Meta-PJM nuclear PPAs, to be updated post-close.
7 May
Capital / M&AVistra · Moldovan (Q1 FY26)▲ bull
~$525M of buybacks in the first 4 months of FY26 + ~$75M dividend = ~$600M returned YTD; the 10b5-1 program accelerates on weakness. Line of sight to >$10B of cash generation 2026-2027: ~$3B to shareholders, ~$4B to accretive growth, ~$3B unallocated, at ~2.3x net debt/EBITDA by YE27.
7 May
Demand / capexVistra · Burke (Q1 FY26)◆ mixed
Load growth 'real, but not at the extremely elevated levels on the fast timeline forecast by many third parties'; DC tightening only begins in 'late 2027 or early 2028.' ERCOT 5-6% and PJM 2-3% through 2030, but ERCOT forwards softened on mild weather and don't even reflect management's own 5-6% view.
27 May
Competition / shareWells Fargo · Aman Chakri (BTM expert)▼ bear
BTM expert (ex-GE Vernova): behind-the-meter is a permanent solution, not a bridge — read-through to the grid-vs-BTM debate that frames the 'IPPs ceding DC share' bear.
5 Jun
Competition / shareWells Fargo · midstream Friday wrap▼ bear
Midstream DC expert: BTM at ~40% of power deals, with post-grid-connection BTM assets running only ~15-30% of capacity for peak-shaving.
12 Jun
Capital / M&AMS · David Arcaro (briefing)▲ bull
MS constructive, highlighting the Helix Digital Infrastructure initiative ($10B, with KKR/NVDA/KIA) as a new theme, worth ~$23/share starting from an initial $1B.
16 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · AI Capex 2.0▲ bull
JPM corroborates VST's marquee nuclear PPA — Vistra/Meta at Beaver Valley/Davis-Besse/Perry, 2.6 GW, 20 years (announced 1/9/26). Models 138 GW of US DC growth through 2030 with power the binding constraint; interconnection queues grow in ERCOT and Texas is a key BTM market given the lack of FERC oversight (a double-edged read).

TLNTalen Energy CorporationthinQ1 FY26 · May 5 → Jun 16, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
7
events
2
bull reads
0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

4 May
Demand / capexJPM · DC broker call (Chris)◆ mixed
DC broker: ~30% of the pipeline is genuinely grid-islanded BTM and counterparty credit (big-four vs Oracle/neocloud) gates the contracting as much as power does. Read-through: supports TLN's 'value of steel in the ground,' but a reminder that the FOTM/co-location regulatory path remains the swing factor.
5 May
PRINT — earningsTalen · Q1 FY26 earnings
Q1 FY26: $473M of adjusted EBITDA, $350M of adjusted FCF — EBITDA more than doubled and FCF ~quadrupled YoY on the Freedom & Guernsey acquisitions (~2.8 GW CCGT), higher spark spreads, capacity/RMR revenues since June 2025 and the AWS ramp. The fleet generated ~16 TWh at a 55% capacity factor (Cole Muller).
5 May
Guidance / numbersTalen · Q1 FY26 guidance▲ bull
2026 guidance reaffirmed: adjusted EBITDA $1.75-2.05bn, adjusted FCF $0.98-1.18bn — excludes the pending Cornerstone acquisition (~$500M run-rate EBITDA, >$4/sh incremental FCF), close expected 'as soon as this summer.' Preliminary outlook: ~$34/sh FCF in 2027, ~$36 in 2028 base; ~$41 in 2028 with 70% of FCF to buybacks.
5 May
Capital / M&ATalen · Cole Muller (Q1 FY26)▲ bull
Pre-funded Cornerstone with $4bn of senior unsecured at ~6.25% and retired $1.2bn of 8.625% secured notes — ~$40M/yr of interest savings (~$1/sh FCF), cutting secured debt from ~60% to ~30%. Net leverage 3.1x at 3/31, target <3.5x. $2bn buyback authorization through 2028; $100M repurchased in Q1.
5 May
Demand / capexTalen · Chris Morice (Q1 FY26)◆ mixed
Single nuclear asset concentration (Susquehanna) and merchant exposure — spark spreads and a widening West Hub-PPL zonal basis (possibly non-fundamental) move FCF/sh materially; management calls the basis 'temporal.' PPL zone peak load projected +70% over five years, AEP +30%.
27 May
Demand / capexWells Fargo · Aman Chakri (BTM expert)◆ mixed
BTM expert (ex-GE Vernova): behind-the-meter is a permanent solution, not a bridge — read-through to the co-location/FOTM debate central to the TLN thesis.
5 Jun
Demand / capexWells Fargo · midstream Friday wrap◆ mixed
Midstream DC expert: BTM at ~40% of power deals, with ~15-30% peak-shaving post grid-reconnection.
16 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · AI Capex 2.0◆ mixed
JPM cites Talen's Susquehanna as the cautionary case of BTM co-location regulatory risk — FERC's 2024 rejection of the Talen/AWS BTM agreement, after which Talen pivoted to a front-of-meter arrangement (the ~1.9 GW AWS PPA structure). Deal table: Talen-Amazon Susquehanna PPA expanded 1.92 GW 20 years to 2042 (6/11/25) + original 960 MW (3/4/24). Backdrop: 138 GW of DC growth, $5.5tn of capex, power the binding constraint.

Forward commentary & how it evolved · anchored on the 2 most recent references

GEVGE VernovarichQ1 FY26 · Apr 22 → Jun 22 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
12
events
9
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

17 Apr
Guidance / numbersGEVernova preview◆ mixed
Pre-print buy-side survey skews cautious: most expected Q1 orders only 'slightly above consensus, not a blow-out', and just ~12% saw Section 232 as 'impactful' — the bull case is largely embedded. 2030 EBITDA expectations re-rated: 44% see $18-20B, ~20% see >$20B vs the August pitch of only $14B.
17 Apr
Supply / capacityGEVernova preview▲ bull
The Gas Power castings/forgings bottleneck (Howmet/PCP/Doncasters/CPP) appears to have eased — GEV 'wrote a check', is securing capacity first vs Siemens/MHI, commands a ~20-30% price premium in the US vs ENR, and may raise the 24 GW production target.
22 Apr
PRINT — earningsGEVernova callback
Q1 FY26: clean beat-and-raise, stock +14% on the day. Orders $18.3B (+71% y/y), book-to-bill ~2x; total backlog $163B. Revenue +7% y/y, adj. EBITDA $896M (+87% y/y, +390bps margin). FCF $4.8B in Q1 alone vs $3.7B for all of FY25. Guidance raised: FY26 revenue $44.5-45.5B; EBITDA margin 12-14%; FCF $6.5-7.5B.
22 Apr
Demand / capexGEVernova callback▲ bull
Gas Power: signed 21 GW of new agreements, GW under contract 83→100 sequentially (44 GW firm + 56 GW SRAs). ~80% traditional customers / ~20% data center. The US installed only 13 GW of gas in all of 2025 vs GEV signing ~18 GW in two quarters — demand 'rather dramatic'. Now expects $200B of total backlog by '27 (pulled forward from '28).
22 Apr
Demand / capexGEVernova callback▲ bull
Electrification flipping from 'peaking' to accelerating: orders +86% y/y at ~$7.1B, data-center orders $2.4B in Q1 alone (more than all of FY25), EBITDA margin +590bps to 17.8%. Management doubled the TAM from $150B (mid-March) to $300B by the end of the decade.
22 Apr
Margin / pricingGEVernova callback▲ bull
Pricing power confirmed: H1'26 orders priced 10-20 pts higher $/kW vs Q4'25, with another 10-20 pts of price upside sitting in the SRAs. The stock is an 'earnings-revisions machine' and PMs buy valuation-insensitive on the pace of EBITDA growth.
22 Apr
Valuation / positionGEVernova callback▼ bear
Order-peak risk: the Q4'25 of 24 GW / $22.2B is the comp to beat; post-analyst-day, hedge funds called the desk arguing 'this is the peak, there was pull-forward'. Ambiguity in the pricing metric — the 10-20 pts $/kW figure ignores mix (peakers/aeros distort it), desk 'not quite sure what's going on'.
14 May
Demand / capexUBS · 800V power▲ bull
Equity call on 800V power links GEV to the power-density theme in data centers.
16 May
Valuation / positionbriefing · positioning▲ bull
Hard-asset/AI-grid positioning: Tiger top-10 holding in GEV, ~$1.03B.
19 May
Valuation / positionJPM · Rishabh Gupta▲ bull
JPM OW on the India T&D arm, PT raised to Rs5,050 — a read-through for parent-level grid demand, not for the US listing.
1 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · James Snyder▲ bull
Jefferies (Snyder) initiating Buy on a data-center power buildout thesis, 2028E EPS $6.35.
8 Jun
Demand / capexCantor · Obin (DataCloud)◆ mixed
Recap of DataCloud-Cannes: cynical industry response on why GEV cannot sign long-term framework agreements with regulated utilities — a regulated utility that locks in low turbine pricing 'looks bad' on EPS vs peers and risks prudency clawback if costs fall. Color on the gating of the 30-35 framework agreement pipeline.
12 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · James Snyder▲ bull
Jefferies (Snyder) reiterates Buy on the data-center power buildout thesis (briefing).

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

30 Apr
PRINT — earningsPWR Q1 FY26 call · Austin/Desai
Q1 FY26: revenue $7.9bn, adj. EBITDA $686m, adj. EPS $2.68 — all double-digit growth; record backlog $48.5bn (CFO Desai). FY26 guidance RAISED: revenue $34.7-35.2bn, adj. EBITDA $3.49-3.65bn, adj. EPS $13.55-14.25; confidence in the 'higher end' on FCF. '30% EPS growth with no acquisitions in the quarter' (Austin).
30 Apr
Supply / capacityPWR Q1 FY26 call · Austin▲ bull
Announcement of a multi-year $500-700m investment in high-voltage power-transformer manufacturing (345-765 kV) — to attack the '36 months on transformers' bottleneck and de-risk customers like AEP via a US supply chain. Off-site footprint ~6.7m sq ft. 'Labor builds labor' — added 5,000-6,000 organically in the year.
30 Apr
Demand / capexPWR Q1 FY26 call · Austin▲ bull
Multi-decade demand, not a single cycle: it took '70 or 100 years to build the grid…you can't double it overnight'; CCGT orders run through 2030 → execution in '2033 and beyond'. NiSource/Alphabet GenCo cited at '$5.7bn…growing every day', with announced generation work NOT yet in backlog (air permits pending; expected 'later half of the year and beyond').
11 May
Valuation / positionBofA · Fenske▲ bull
BofA/Fenske places PWR within the AI mega-bull framework as one of the 'free-rider winners' of the cycle, alongside the hyperscalers. Read-throughs: GEV, NVDA-power, BE/AAOI.
11 May
Valuation / positionJefferies · Rankie Wong– neutral
Jefferies (Rankie Wong) lists PWR in a broad US-infrastructure basket. Thematic/indirect datapoint — no PT, no rating in the file.

WMBThe Williams CompaniesmediumQ1 FY26 · May 05 → Jun 22 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
6
events
3
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

5 May
PRINT — earningsWMB Q1 FY26 call · John Porter (CFO)
Record quarter. Adj. EBITDA $2.25bn, +13% YoY; adj. EPS +22%. Transmission & Gulf led (+~$150m / ~17%), Transco +~10% YoY post-rate-case; Deepwater Gulf +60%; storage +35%; West +16%. Guidance now points to the 'upper half' of the FY26 guide; reaffirmed 10%+ EBITDA & EPS CAGR (2025-2030). Contracted base moved from ~8% to ~9%, called 'still pretty conservative'.
5 May
Demand / capexWMB Q1 FY26 call · John Porter (CFO)▲ bull
Three new majors + one upsize: NEO (5th PI project, 682MW, 12.5-year contract, ~$2.3bn, ISD H2-2028, 5x build multiple — largest PI deal to date); Atlas (Transco gas-for-backup displacing diesel at a Northeast data center, up to 164 MMcf/d, 13 years, <$50m capex); Silver Spur (Northwest Pipeline expansion, 275 MMcf/d, ~$0.5bn); Power Express upsized to 750 MMcf/d. Growth capex midpoint raised to $7.3bn.
5 May
Capital / M&AWMB Q1 FY26 call · John Porter (CFO)◆ mixed
Leverage 4.1x, modestly above the 3.5-4.0x target — a '26/27 timing issue' with historic earnings growth in 28+ resetting capacity. Financing options under review (partnership/JV around PI, capital recycling); specifics promised 'in the next couple of months'. Dividend growth explicitly maintained.
6 May
Valuation / positionJSD · Williams midstream (equity call)▲ bull
Bull: 'Williams is the ultimate manifestation' of the gas-to-power thesis. JSD argues the financially leveraged structure implies an EPS CAGR 'north of 20%' — well above the stated 10% — and that moving the floor from 8%→9% is management breadcrumbing; the desk models ~13% EBITDA CAGR. JSD survey ~80/20 bullish; consensus sees WMB beating its 10% CAGR by 200-300bps and FID'ing ~$3bn more of PI capex this year.
6 May
Valuation / positionJSD · Williams midstream (equity call)▼ bear
Pushbacks: expensive at 13-14x EBITDA, 'fundamental/energy guys' see good growth but limited upside; 'generals' rotate to smaller, higher-operating-leverage names. PI terminal-value risk: contracts 'only' 10-12.5 years, recontracting an open question — the prevailing bear thesis is that PI is lower-quality earnings. Skepticism that the 6GW backlog is being replenished. Leverage 4.1x (peaking ~4.3x in 27) needs the back-end ramp to land.
5 Jun
Valuation / positionWells Fargo · midstream Friday wrap▲ bull
WFC rates WMB Overweight; flags growing debate over the proposed PI-JV. BTM valuation work implies ~15% upside for Williams if it hits 2030 BTM power targets; WFC estimates Williams is discounting ~5 of its 8 GW BTM target. Uses 9.5x for the SOTP of the power business. DC expert: BTM now ~40% of power deals; post-grid BTM assets run at only 15-30% utilization (peak-shaving). Investor feedback on the PI-JV was 'lukewarm'.
5 Jun
Supply / capacityWells Fargo · midstream Friday wrap◆ mixed
Turbine-backlog color: more turbine reservation slots cancelled/released (~30-35 projects nationally cancelled/delayed; Kodiak picked up released slots on two occasions), with secondary-market capacity available at a premium.

AGXArgan, Inc.thinQ1 FY27 · Jun 04 → Jun 22 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
4
events
3
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

4 Jun
PRINT — earningsAGX Q1 FY27 call · Baugher/Watson
Q1 FY27 (ended Apr-30-2026): record revenue $291M, +50% y/y (vs $194M), driven by the ramp of recently awarded Power projects. Consolidated gross margin 21% (Power 23.6%); adj. EBITDA $56.4M (19.4% margin); net income $46.1M, diluted EPS $3.24 (vs $1.60 y/y). Backlog $2.8B, slightly down vs $2.9B — management stresses that backlog 'swings' between completion of one job and announcement of the next. Balance sheet: $974M cash+investments, no debt.
4 Jun
Demand / capexAGX Q1 FY27 call · Watson▲ bull
Structural demand for firm generation — electrification, onshoring, EVs and data centers pressure the grid; combined-cycle gas is 'the ideal solution' and few competitors execute gigawatt-plus fixed-price jobs. Long runway: target of $2B of revenue 'down the road', capacity for 10-12 simultaneous jobs (currently 8). Backlog includes 4 US gas plants totaling >4.1 GW.
4 Jun
Competition / shareAGX Q1 FY27 call · Watson▼ bear
Renewable demand 'softened' — solar/battery exposure less attractive (backlog 13% renewable vs 79% gas). Backlog does not grow linearly (dipped from $2.9B → $2.8B); large new jobs depend on development milestones outside Argan's control (air/water permits, gas access, turbines, financing) in a repeated window of '10-18 months'.
4 Jun
Capital / M&AAGX Q1 FY27 call · Watson▲ bull
Quarterly dividend $0.50 ($2/yr; +33% in Sep-25, 3rd consecutive increase). Buyback raised to $200M (from $150M) and extended to Jan-31-2030; $33.6M returned via repurchases in Q1 FY27.
22 Jun
Demand / capexJefferies · DC build / Vulcan-IIR▲ bull
Sector read-through (no AGX rating): ~25 GW of gas capacity starting construction in 2026 (~8 GW online '26, ~11 GW '27), then ~35 GW of starts in 2027 and ~38 GW in 2028 — a multi-year EPC tailwind. Corroborates the bear point on 'backlog swings' — most of the slippage is delays, not cancellations, gated by labor (5 craft workers retiring per 1 entrant), permitting and turbine lead times of 3-5 years.

APHAmphenol CorporationmediumQ1 FY26 · Apr 29 → Jun 22 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
7
events
5
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

29 Apr
PRINT — earningsAPH Q1-2026 call · Norwitt
Q1 FY2026: record sales $7.62B, +58% USD / +33% organic YoY; record adj. EPS $1.06, +68%. Record orders $9.435B, +78%, book-to-bill 1.24:1 with all end markets ≥1.0. Adj. op margin 27.3%, +380bp YoY. IT datacom ~41% of sales, +81% organic, +27% QoQ — 'virtually all' of the sequential organic growth from AI. Q2 guidance: sales $8.1-8.2B, adj. EPS $1.14-1.16. CommScope growing ~in line with APH's organic pace (~$4.1B sales / $0.15 accretion FY26).
29 Apr
Product / roadmapAPH Q1-2026 call · Norwitt▲ bull
Power thesis: 'We talk a lot about the data part of AI data centers… we should also talk a lot about the power part.' APH cites the 'broadest array of power interconnect from discrete connectors to very complex power cable assemblies, bus bars, liquid cooled bus bars', positioning itself as a 'core partner' as racks migrate to higher power capacities.
12 May
Competition / sharebriefing company-specific · JPM J. Meyers▼ bear
Copper-terminal-value narrative: the GLW bull case argues CPO/optics erodes copper content; APH framed as a share loser vs GLW's vertically integrated CPO; the CommScope deal seen as 'not vertically integrated like GLW'. JPM counters that APH's optical optionality is under-credited.
26 May
Competition / share650 Group · networking research▲ bull
Supports the durability of copper content: 400G-per-lane can still be copper ('no longer up for debate' per standards bodies); Broadcom is pushing a full-copper scale-up solution. In a co-packaged-copper architecture the connector business benefits — Molex, Amphenol, TE, TTM — with higher-value connectors as speeds rise. Copper 'lives longer than people anticipate'.
9 Jun
Product / roadmapJPM · Samik Chatterjee (Watts Up! 800V)▲ bull
JPM names APH (with FLEX/TEL/WOLF) best-positioned in the rewiring of the AI-DC power stack to 800V. Power solutions ~$150 of the ~$870 total interconnect content per XPU (content up 5x/chip in three years); AI power-interconnect TAM growing ~40%+ CAGR from ~$2bn (2025) to $4bn+ (2028). APH's 800V share: busbar connectors/assemblies, board-level power connectors, blind-mate power couplings and cable assemblies. CEO presenting at the BofA conference.
11 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · AI-power desk call (equity call)◆ mixed
APH described as a 'modest crowded long' on the buy-side. JPM hosted a 'super meeting with Adam Norwitt' that drew attention.
12 Jun
Competition / shareCiena optical (equity call)▲ bull
Read-through: APH is a scale-up ACC cable partner (Ciena Nitro redriver → Luxshare/Amphenol/TE).
15 Jun
Product / roadmapCiti · 800V DC power▲ bull
APH favorably positioned alongside TEL — 800V drives 'more specialized and higher value-added power distribution and connectivity', supporting datacom/data-network growth. Citi sees ±400V HVDC sidecar (CSP-driven, OCP 'Mount Diablo') adopted first (250kW-1MW racks), then NVDA 800V sidecars with Rubin Ultra in late 2H27, SST-based 800V for >1MW from ~2029.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

22 Apr
PRINT — earningsTEL FQ2 FY26 call · Curtin/Mitts
FQ2 FY26 (qtr ended 2026-03-27): sales $4.7bn, +15% reported / +7% organic y/y; adj. EPS $2.73, +24% y/y (above the guide); adj. op margin 21.7%, +130 bps. Orders $5.3bn, book-to-bill 1.12. Industrial Solutions +27% reported / +17% organic (DDN +~50% y/y; DDN orders +60% y/y building 2027 backlog). Transportation +5% (auto -4% organic, NA weak). AI revenue raised ~$150m vs 90 days earlier → approaching $2.4bn for FY26. FQ3 guide: ~$5.0bn / EPS ~$2.83.
22 Apr
Product / roadmapTEL FQ2 FY26 call · Curtin▲ bull
Copper-and-optical strategy reinforced by the RampPhotonics acquisition; 'copper will continue to be the workhorse in the rack'. Capex raised to ~6% of revenue, 'almost entirely' for the AI/DDN ramp. H1 FY26 FCF $1.3bn (record), ~100% returned to shareholders, dividend +10%.
26 May
Competition / share650 Group · networking research▲ bull
Read-through for the copper 'workhorse' thesis: 400G-per-lane can still be copper (settled by the standards bodies), with Broadcom pushing full-copper scale-up; the co-packaged-copper architecture benefits the connector vendors — Molex, Amphenol, TE, TTM — with higher-value connectors as speeds rise. Supports TE's view that copper remains the workhorse in the rack.
9 Jun
Product / roadmapJPM · Samik Chatterjee (Watts Up! 800V)▲ bull
TE Connectivity flagged best-positioned as a beneficiary of connector content in the 800V/HVDC stack (with APH/FLEX/WOLF). JPM estimates TE's power business is ~25% of total data-center-related revenue, with 800V share in busbar connectors, board-level power connectors, power couplings and cable assemblies; TE advantaged by transferable automotive-800V (EV) IP covering 'grid-to-chip'. Power interconnect = ~$150 of the ~$870 content per XPU; TAM ~$2bn→$4bn+ by 2028.
15 Jun
Product / roadmapCiti · 800V DC power▲ bull
TEL (with APH) favorably positioned. Citi cites TEL's own framing — higher power+data per rack drives 50%+ FY25-FY27 CAGR for the DDN segment, with power connectivity ~25% of segment revenue, and the transition to 800V could generate a 30%+ content increase vs today. 800V is also positive for TEL's energy business (grid hardening ~66% and DC power connections ~20%). Path: ±400V HVDC sidecar first, NVDA 800V w/ Rubin Ultra late-2H27, SST 800V from ~2029.
15 Jun
Product / roadmapbriefing · @bubbleboi channel checks▼ bear
800V opportunity timing contested: @bubbleboi channel checks argue rack-level GaN/SiC/connector content is '2-3 years before hitting earnings' without a near-term pull from DC construction (in the broader 800V basket).
18 Jun
Product / roadmapbriefing · TE VP/Head of ICT (expert call)▲ bull
TE's VP/Head of ICT framed HVDC (400V→800V) as cutting energy loss 2-5% per conversion stage, with liquid cooling mainstream by 2030.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

1 May
PRINT — earningsnVent · earnings call
Q1 2026 (record): sales US$1,242M, +53% reported / +34% organic; adjusted EPS US$1.09, +63% y/y. Systems Protection US$895M (+70%, +50% organic); Electrical Connections US$347M (+15%, -390 bps of ROS from copper inflation). Organic orders +~40% (DC-led), backlog US$2.6B. Infrastructure now 55% of sales. FY2026 guidance raised to +26%–28% reported and adjusted EPS US$4.45–4.55.
30 May
Demand / capexBofA · Obin (briefing 2026-05-30-morning.md)– neutral
BofA Datacloud Cannes expert call (6/8, 10am ET, Obin) groups NVT with VRT/ETN/CARR/JCI in the datacenter power/cooling complex — the only name-level reference in the briefings corpus.
8 Jun
Demand / capexCantor · Obin (DataCloud recap)▲ bull
DataCloud Cannes recap: CDU market (nVent's liquid cooling franchise) scaling fast — 1 MW CDUs were "really big" last year, focus now on 2.5 MW and 4–5 MW already being discussed; chillers "not going away" (rooftop heat islands 20–25°C above ambient). Content/scale tailwind for on/around-rack cooling vendors. Cites Eaton/Vertiv/Delta/Schneider as 800V/SST sidecar players (consistent with the bear nuance that nVent is not on the NVIDIA power-system list).

VRTVertiv Holdings ComediumQ1 FY26 · Apr 22 → Jun 15, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

22 Apr
PRINT — earningsVertiv · earnings call
Q1 FY26 (beat-and-raise): net sales US$2,649.5M, +30.1% y/y (+23% organic); adjusted operating margin 20.8% (+430bps); adjusted EPS US$1.17 (+83%); adjusted FCF US$653M; net leverage 0.2x. Americas +44% organic; EMEA -29% organic (guided back to growth in H2'26). FY26 guide raised: sales ~US$13.75B, adjusted EPS US$6.35 (+51%). Mgmt stopped disclosing quarterly orders/backlog in Q4'25; backlog US$15.0B at YE2025.
8 May
Valuation / positionCooperman (briefing 2026-05-08)▲ bull
Cooperman keeps VRT concentrated at ~US$800M as "the gigawatt name" — top concentrated holding treating VRT as the AI-datacenter power proxy.
14 May
Competition / shareUBS · 800V power expert call◆ mixed
800V call: expert frames UPS players as adaptable and not disrupted — "Vertiv will just adapt". Bear nuance: 800V architecture eliminates the traditional UPS ("you will not have a UPS… you will have a BES") and low-frequency transformer layers; VRT has to pivot its core profit pool toward BES/SST, with the DC safety spec (arc, breakers) not yet finalized.
3 Jun
Competition / shareAlphaSense · 800V power expert◆ mixed
Expert (ex-Amazon DC infra, now Delta): VRT/Schneider are behind ABB/Eaton on SST (solid-state transformer) technology, leading in UPS. SST at 1.4–1.6x conventional transformers (should fall with scale). 800V eliminates UPS and LF transformer (1–3% efficiency gain, >US$100M/yr opex at a 1GW campus). Choke point = DC breakers (ABB "is king"), then in-rack PSUs. 800V economics turn at ~180–200 kW/rack; "huge jump" in adoption expected in 2027.
8 Jun
Competition / shareCantor · Obin (DataCloud recap)◆ mixed
3-hour dinner with Vertiv mgmt; sat with Eaton, visited Delta/Schneider/Legrand. 800V transition "going to be gradual… many paths" — sidecar happens first, full 800V conversion later (analogous to direct-to-chip cooling taking ~2 years). Reality check: average rack density at a European EPC still ~30 kW (vs 1 MW headlines); low-voltage players have "plenty of room… slowly melting ice cube". Sidecar/SST set = Eaton/Vertiv/Delta/Schneider.
9 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · Samik Chatterjee (Watts Up! Shift to 800V)▲ bull
VRT among NVIDIA Data Center Power Systems partners (with ABB, Eaton, GE Vernova, Hitachi Energy, Mitsubishi, Schneider, Siemens). JPM builds the power-management TAM off Vertiv's own estimate (~US$1mn/MW, ~30% of US$3.0–3.5mn/MW total content) growing ~30% CAGR from US$20bn (2025) to US$45bn (2028); power-distribution TAM US$50bn → US$90bn (2028). Reinforces VRT as the cleanest read on power-management content/MW in the 800V transition.
15 Jun
Product / roadmapbriefing 2026-06-15▲ bull
Vera Rubin 800V HVDC reportedly pulled ahead to Q3 2026 (small volume, via Taiwanese media) — flagged as "a direct catalyst for $VRT and the power-semi supply chain", alongside Delta, Schneider, Eaton.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

6 May
PRINT — earningsFlex · earnings call
Q4/FY26 (beat-and-raise, headline = CPI spin-off): Q4 revenue US$7.5B, +17% y/y; adjusted GM 9.9% (record); adjusted EPS US$0.93, +27%. FY26 revenue US$27.9B (+8%), adjusted EPS US$3.30 (+25%); CPI US$6.6B (+38%, Cloud & Cooling +29% / Power +61%). CPI to be spun into a standalone public company (target Q1 CY2027, tax-free). Combined FY27 guide: revenue US$32.3–33.8B (+18%), adjusted EPS US$4.21–4.51 (+32%), CPI +65–75%.
26 May
Margin / pricingJPM · Samik (Networking conf recap)▲ bull
At JPM's conference, Flex sounded confident about power margins rising with scale and framed ~15% power margin as NOT the LT target — "room to go higher as scale improves". Demand backdrop: JPM raised its DC capex outlook to ~+80% y/y in 2026 (>US$250B step-up) and ~+50% in 2027; North American DC power capacity +8 GW in 1Q26 (largest quarterly add ever); pipeline >175 GW.
9 Jun
Product / roadmapJPM · Samik (Watts Up! Shift to 800V)▲ bull
FLEX among the best-positioned (Anord Mardix critical power + 800V/1MW rack); JPM notes that "long crowding in Flex has picked up substantially". Power-distribution TAM (Omdia) +20% CAGR from ~US$50bn (2025) to ~US$90bn (2028); power-management subset (~US$1mn/MW) +30% CAGR to US$45bn in 2028. JPM estimates Flex's power business is ~32% of CPI/SpinCo revenue today, on track to >40% in 2027; the 800V portfolio spans power shelves, PSUs, CBU, BBUs, liquid-cooled busbars, switchgear, substations and integrated power racks. NVIDIA power partner.
22 Jun
Valuation / positionS&P / briefings (2026-06-09)▲ bull
FLEX inclusion in the S&P 500 effective Jun 22, 2026 (alongside MRVL) — passive-flow tailwind.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

5 May
PRINT — earningsWolfspeed · earnings call
Q3 FY26 (quarter ended 2026-03-29): revenue US$150.2M, -19% y/y; Power US$100.1M, Materials US$50.1M. GAAP gross loss -US$40.0M (-26.6%); non-GAAP GM -20.6% (double-digit improvement vs -34% in Q2). Op loss -US$114.3M; net loss -US$119.9M; EPS -US$3.05. Mohawk Valley underutilization of ~US$46M is the main drag. Liquidity US$1,164.8M; first debt maturity in 2030. Datacenter +30% QoQ (400V→800V migration); first commercial 10kV SiC MOSFET. Q4 FY26 guide: revenue US$140–160M, GM still negative.
6 May
Capital / M&AFinTwit · @thetranscript_ (briefing 2026-05-06)▲ bull
WOLF cut its highest-cost first-lien debt by 43%; stock +9% AH.
16 May
Valuation / positionFinTwit · @insane_analyst (briefing 2026-05-16)◆ mixed
WOLF treated as the levered torque play on 800V power-semi — "+300% or -50%".
31 May
Valuation / positionDesk positioning (briefing 2026-05-31)▼ bear
Semis desk short-biased on WOLF (alongside QCOM/SWKS/NVDA/QRVO) at the end of May.
9 Jun
Product / roadmapJPM · Samik Chatterjee (Watts Up! Shift to 800V)▲ bull
WOLF among the best-positioned in the 800V theme (APH/FLEX/TEL/WOLF), though the equity rating remains UW. JPM/Navitas size the WBG market (AI DC + energy/grid) growing 30%+ CAGR from ~US$1bn (2025) to US$5bn+ by 2030 (800V pivot; PSU scaling non-linearly 2x power → ~5x SiC content; grid SSTs/BESS). LT content US$25K–35K/MW, split ~evenly SiC/GaN. WOLF's 800V participation: bare-die + packaged discrete MOSFETs and modules for grid-to-facility SST and rack power shelves; JPM estimates the power business at ~10% of total revenue.
9 Jun
Valuation / positionFinTwit (briefing 2026-06-09)◆ mixed
800V basket compiled by FinTwit ranks WOLF as the smallest at ~US$2.2B market cap (vs IFX US$116B, ON US$46B).
15 Jun
Demand / capexFinTwit · @insane_analyst / Serenity (briefing 2026-06-15)◆ mixed
@insane_analyst "sizing up WOLF (800V DC)" but skeptical on SiC vs GaN ("SiC garbage, only GaN matters"); Serenity bullish on the 800V acceleration ("very stupid to be a bear… your WOLF power-semi basket should go brrr"). NVDA Vera Rubin 800V pulled ahead to Q3 2026.
17 Jun
Competition / shareFinTwit · @trendforce (briefing 2026-06-17)▼ bear
Chinese SiC/GaN vendors challenging IDM leaders including Wolfspeed in certain segments (risk of SiC/GaN flood from China).

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

5 May
PRINT — earningsNavitas · earnings call
Q1 2026 (quarter ended 2026-03-31): revenue US$8.6M, +18% sequential, above the top of guide; high-power +35% y/y (majority of revenue), AI infrastructure +50% QoQ. Non-GAAP GM 39.0% (+30bps); non-GAAP op loss -US$11.7M; GAAP net loss -US$33.8M / -US$0.15. Cash US$221M, zero debt. Extreme concentration: Distributor A = 59% of Q1 revenue. Q2 guide: revenue US$10.0M ± US$0.5M, GM 39.25%. Management TAM: US$3.5bn by 2030 at 60%+ CAGR.
6 May
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Joseph Moore (briefing 2026-05-06)◆ mixed
MS maintains Underweight (since 2025-04-06, ref US$6.04) but raised PT to US$13.70 (from US$12.50), "tracking but proofpoints pending… Stepping up the Voltage". Deutsche Bank (Ross Seymore) also "Stepping up the Voltage".
9 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · Samik Chatterjee (Watts Up! Shift to 800V)▲ bull
Navitas is the source of JPM's WBG sizing: WBG power-device market (AI DC + energy/grid) grows 30%+ CAGR from ~US$1bn (2025) to US$5bn+ by 2030; LT content US$25K–35K/MW split ~evenly SiC/GaN (GaN mainly inside the DC — the segment most relevant to NVTS). Driven by the 800V pivot, non-linear PSU scaling (2x power → ~5x SiC content) and grid SST/BESS modernization. Corroborates NVTS's own US$3.5bn-by-2030 / 60%+ CAGR TAM; Navitas listed among the silicon suppliers highlighted by NVIDIA.
15 Jun
Competition / shareCiti · 800V DC power (briefing 2026-06-15)◆ mixed
Citi's GaN/SiC split is constructive on GaN-in-rack (where NVTS plays): GaN preferred for sub-650V conversion at the server/rack level, SiC for >1,000V infra. Earlier deployment is ±400V bipolar HVDC (each rail ≤400V → ≤650V stress) leveraging the mature 650V GaN ecosystem from EVs — favorable timing for rack GaN 2027–2028. Note: Citi's named GaN winners are TXN/ON (not NVTS) — GaN-positive theme but the share question for NVTS is open.
16 Jun
Competition / shareFinTwit · @insane_analyst (briefing 2026-06-16)▼ bear
@insane_analyst bearish on the SiC narrative — "dumb ppl falling for Navitas CEO TAM bullshit. SiC is garbage. Only GaN portfolio of value". @bubbleboi mentions NVTS as a possible re-entry into the AI-DC-power-stack (without fundamental conviction).

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

2 Jun
PRINT — earningsPalo Alto Networks · earnings call
Q3 FY26 (quarter ended Apr 30, 2026): broad beat. NGS ARR US$8.13B, +60% y/y (includes ~US$1.6B from CyberArk + Chronosphere); RPO US$18.4B; revenue US$3.00B, +31% y/y. GAAP net loss -US$177M / -US$0.22 (acquisition accounting); adjusted FCF US$910M, +57%. NGFW bookings ~+40% (best hardware quarter in a decade); SASE ARR US$1.6B +40%; XSIAM ARR >US$600M, +100% y/y. ~2,280 platformized customers, 120% net retention. FY26 guide raised: NGS ARR US$8.9–8.95B.
3 Jun
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Marshall/Weiss▲ bull
"Cleared all bogeys; customers clearly turning to PANW to secure AI adoption; single-digit cyber share = long runway". MS raised PT to US$320 (from US$253), OW; FY26E revenue +23.8%, FY27E +18.2%.
3 Jun
Competition / shareDeutsche Bank · Zelnick▲ bull
"Broad platform strength; NetSec/Prisma SASE displacement is the key differentiator vs ZS; constructive on the FY27 acceleration". DB Buy, PT US$350 — "strongest Q3 in several years" in SASE.
3 Jun
Guidance / numbersJPM · Essex/Lee▲ bull
"Solid execution with AI tailwinds and M&A synergies ahead of plan" — XSIAM, Prisma AIRS, CyberArk all beating. JPM OW.
3 Jun
Guidance / numbersUBS · PANW Q3 FY26 review + callbacks (Nikesh/Depak + IR)▼ bear
Bear's Exhibit A on quality: of the 30% product growth, 8 points came from CyberArk (inorganic); hardware revenue grew "roughly half" of the 40% bookings, implying subscription + support fell again even in the "best cyber demand environment ever". UBS models ~US$610M of organic net-new ARR in 4Q. Mythos framed as "chaos"; "cutting checks immediately is not really a thing", strategic decisions still 6+ months out. New markets: sovereign + neoclouds. At ~35x CY28 FCF, UBS sees "not a whole lot of wiggle room".
3 Jun
Guidance / numbersRedburn/Rothschild▼ bear
"Beat-and-raise, but the organic business is underwhelming" — qualitative caution on the sustainability of product revenue. Neutral.
4 Jun
Demand / capexGlasswing/Mythos cyber note▲ bull
AI-native cyber reshaping the security value chain — PANW positioned as the platform consolidator.
8 Jun
Guidance / numbersBarclays · Kalia▲ bull
IR-meeting takeaways: 4Q26 organic NNARR guide raised US$20M+ at the midpoint; FY27 could add US$1.9B+ of total NNARR; accelerating NetSec can sustain FCF margins. Barclays OW, PT US$315.
17 Jun
Guidance / numbersIR meeting (briefing 2026-06-17)◆ mixed
IR takeaways: organic subscription revenue deceleration partly explained by virtual-firewall rev-rec mechanics; NGS ARR/RPO headline flattered by M&A (~US$1.6B from CyberArk + Chronosphere).
18 Jun
Demand / capexBTG · Mythos expert call▲ bull
Expert (ex-PANW): Nikesh "referenced 800 customer meetings since April" on Mythos/AI security; "this is now the Mythos moment" (also acknowledged on the CRWD call). Unit 42 Frontier AI Defense: 800+ meetings in six weeks.
18 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Roger Boyd (cyber checks / Karl software call)▼ bear
Nikesh frames the environment as "chaos", teams focused on assessing their own risk/patching; larger strategic Mythos opportunities "will still take six months plus". UBS turns slightly more cautious on Palo (and CrowdStrike) vs peers, seeing incremental cyber dollars spreading beyond the two platforms (identity + data gaining more attention).

CRWDCrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.mediumQ1 FY27 · Apr 13 → Jun 18, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
6
events
5
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

13 Apr
Demand / capexWFC · software conf recap▲ bull
WFC reported that its meeting with CrowdStrike at the conference was "very upbeat" on Project Glasswing/Mythos; cyber was "the one bright spot where people talk more about the AI opportunity than the AI risk" — Mythos seen as a net positive for cyber budgets. On the Glasswing logo list, only CrowdStrike and Palo were cited, making it "difficult to figure out who else to own".
5 May
Demand / capexMorgan Stanley · TMT▲ bull
Morgan Stanley TMT (Outlook): 'CRWD bullish on AI demand.'
14 May
Competition / shareBernstein · Seidman▲ bull
Bernstein/Seidman: the FTNT print is a "positive for PANW/S/CRWD" read.
1 Jun
Demand / capexJefferies · Brent Thill / "Cyber Joe"▲ bull
Jefferies' CIO at the conference named CrowdStrike as his "go-to in a deserted island with one raft" (endpoint + cloud + ITDR), wanting to cut 80 cyber tools down to ~5. Structural point: even if Anthropic/OpenAI dominated the enterprise, "you would still need a third-party security auditor" — frontier-lab dominance does not displace CRWD.
3 Jun
PRINT — earningsCrowdStrike · earnings call Q1 FY27
Record Q1 print, beat-and-raise. Total revenue $1.39bn (+26% y/y); ending ARR $5.51bn (+24%, accelerating); net new ARR $256m (+32% y/y, record Q1). FCF $468.5m (34% margin, all-time high); Rule of 40 = 59. FY27 guide raised: net new ARR $1.279-1.303bn. Falcon Flex ARR $1.9bn (+99% y/y); AIDR +250%+ q/q; Kurtz repositions CRWD as "critical AI infrastructure". 4-for-1 split effective 2026-07-02.
18 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Roger Boyd▼ bear
UBS (over a dozen checks with CISOs): cyber budgets "in a better place than a few months ago" on board-level Mythos urgency, but teams remain resource-constrained and 6-12 month cycles are not compressing. Net: UBS turns slightly more positive on the non-CRWD/PANW vendors and slightly more cautious on CrowdStrike and Palo, on the view that the incremental dollars may spread out.
18 Jun
Demand / capexBTG · Mythos expert call▲ bull
BTG Mythos expert call (Outlook): escalation of AI-driven attacks = secular tailwind for AI-native cyber (Falcon / Charlotte AI / AIDR).

CRMSalesforce, Inc.mediumQ1 FY27 · May 19 → Jun 18, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
13
events
2
bull reads
6
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

16 May
Margin / pricing@theallinpod (briefing) · Benioff▼ bear
Benioff (via @theallinpod): "~$300M of Anthropic this year at Salesforce" — validates agentic spend but signals rising COGS / dependence on frontier-model vendors.
19 May
Valuation / positionBofA · reinstate▼ bear
BofA reinstated Underperform, PO $160 — FY27 revenue growth ~11%, FY28 ~9.6%, only 9x EV/FCF, "mature platform facing AI-driven structural reset".
27 May
PRINT — earningsSalesforce · earnings call Q1 FY27
Beat that "cleared the bar but didn't change the AI debate" (stock ~flat to -3%). Revenue $11.13bn (+13% y/y, +12% cc), above guide; cRPO $33.6bn (+13% cc), at the low end vs buyside hopes of 13-14%. Agentforce ARR surpassed $1bn; AI & Data ARR $3.4bn; 28.6tn tokens (+152% q/q). Non-GAAP op margin 34.8% (+250bps); $25bn ASR cut shares ~10% y/y. FY27 guide raised to ~$45.9-46.2bn; FY30 target $63bn.
27 May
Valuation / positionJPM · Murphy– neutral
JPM (Murphy) suspended rating/PT for policy reasons going into the print.
28 May
Guidance / numbersJPM · Schilsky▼ bear
JPM (Schilsky) tagged the print as "CRM = meh" — nothing changes the AI debate; cRPO came in at the low end of buyside.
28 May
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Weiss▲ bull
MS (Weiss): Overweight, PT $287.
28 May
Valuation / positionBarclays · Lenschow◆ mixed
Barclays (Lenschow): Overweight, PT cut from $252 to $236, "solid but not thesis-changing".
28 May
Valuation / positionUBS · Keirstead▼ bear
UBS (Keirstead): Neutral — "simply won't re-rate off 6-8% revs growth".
28 May
Valuation / positionRedburn Atlantic · R&Co▲ bull
R&Co Redburn: Buy — "Q1 clears the bar, H2 acceleration on track".
29 May
Competition / shareWFC · Matt Slotnick (Poio) expert call▼ bear
Ex-Google Cloud / Poio CEO argues the growth market for incumbents like CRM is "a layer above their existing system" (the agentic layer); owning the data/relationships is an advantage but does not guarantee winning that layer. The "last mile" is where value accrues and where large SW vendors have historically been weak.
1 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · Brent Thill◆ mixed
Jefferies (Brent Thill): Salesforce post-print sounded "stable, steady… not super exciting"; broader takeaway that "software is not dead", but notable magnitude of turnover in Silicon Valley sales orgs.
16 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Tech desk◆ mixed
UBS Tech (Outlook): CRM featured in the 'software pain-trade' / positioning note (alongside PLTR).
16 Jun
Capital / M&ABarclays & BofA · desks◆ mixed
Barclays & BofA desks (Outlook): CRM acquired Fin (AI); BofA Underperform PT $160 (mature-platform/AI-reset bear); Benioff cites $300M deal with Anthropic.
18 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Keirstead/McGinnis▼ bear
UBS reports a "stronger barbell" in IT budgets — enterprises pressuring apps: "minimizing seat growth, minimizing module expansion, negotiating much tougher on pricing"; the apps-spend environment is getting harder. Karpathy warned that Claude is "getting very good at absorbing workflow knowledge", a long-term threat to Salesforce's 20-year moat; down-market move (Intercom/Fin) cited.

RDDTReddit, Inc.mediumQ1 FY26 · Apr 30 → Jun 17, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
12
events
5
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

15 Apr
Guidance / numbersJPM · Anmuth◆ mixed
JPM (Internet 1Q preview): RDDT "pretty split" — revenue beats are not the debate (everyone expects ~$650M+ in Q1); what matters is US DAU, which is "on the razor's edge". Renewals of the data deals with Google + OpenAI are a mid-to-back-half event to watch.
16 Apr
Valuation / positionBofA · Fenske (spec-sales)▲ bull
BofA spec-sales pitched RDDT as one of the few non-AI-adjacent growth stocks left — ~50% growth at ~15x, down 40-50%, CPMs ~half of Pinterest's on a less mature platform, with data-deal renewals as binary upside.
30 Apr
PRINT — earningsReddit · earnings call Q1 FY26
Revenue $663.4M (+69% y/y) — 7th straight quarter >60%. Advertising $624.7M (+74%); content licensing/other $38.7M (+15%). ARPU $5.23 (+44%). Net income $204.0M vs $26.2M PY. Adj. EBITDA margin 40%; FCF margin 47%, record FCF >$300M; gross margin 91.5%. DAUq 126.8M, but growth decelerated to ~17% (from 51% a year earlier). Q2-26 guide: revenue $715-725M (+43-45%).
6 May
Demand / capexBofA · Wigg◆ mixed
BofA (Wigg): RDDT remains a top-1-or-2 cited source in AI (with Wikipedia), so OpenAI/Google renewals + a potential legal settlement with Anthropic could add high-margin dollars. The same day, RDDT -3% as Google AI Search Mode packaged Reddit content inside AI overviews.
16 May
Valuation / positionTiger Global (13F)– neutral
Tiger Global held $394M of RDDT (13F).
21 May
Demand / capexJPM (desk)▼ bear
RDDT -5% / JPM's #1 inbound on a delayed reaction to Google I/O agentic-search risk ("organic search referral traffic to RDDT shrinks as AI Mode answers in-place").
26 May
Valuation / positionRedburn Atlantic– neutral
Redburn flagged relative 3-month lows (technical).
27 May
Demand / capexBofA (advertiser survey)▲ bull
BofA advertiser survey signaled "Reddit strong 2Q trends".
28 May
Demand / capexBernstein · Seidman▲ bull
Bernstein (Seidman): the ads business is still early — ARPU +44% with ad-load headroom; active advertisers +75% y/y; SHOP integration unlocking SMB budgets.
1 Jun
Demand / capexJefferies · Brent Thill▲ bull
At the Jefferies conference, RDDT laid out a durable growth case: a long runway to add advertisers + lift existing advertisers' budgets (the biggest recent driver), reiterating the goal to double US DAU to 100M+ by converting 200M weekly users to daily.
10 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Schilsky/Anmuth◆ mixed
JPM hosting management access (OW stance); management's North Star: 100M US DAUq / 1B global DAUq. CPG advertisers' input-cost pressure also flagged.
10 Jun
Demand / capexBofA · Ben Legg◆ mixed
BofA online-advertising sector call (Outlook): 'Reddit automated ad tools' a key theme, alongside Google AI-search read-through.
17 Jun
Demand / capexUBS · Ben Legg▲ bull
UBS (Ben Legg) models RDDT ad growth Q2 73% / Q3 55% / Q4 51% (all nudged up vs April lows), calling Reddit "an amazing game of catch-up" as it cherry-picks the best features from Google/Meta.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

5 May
PRINT — earningsShopify · earnings call Q1 FY26
Above-trend print, then sold off ~19% through mid-quarter on margin/comp concerns. GMV $101B (+35% y/y, +30% cc) — 2nd straight quarter >$100B. Revenue $3.17B (+34%); Merchant Solutions 76% of revenue; Shopify Payments $67B GMV (67% of total); Shop Pay $35B GMV (+59%). FCF $476M (15% margin). Agentic signal: AI-driven traffic +8x y/y, orders from AI search ~+13x; UCP with Amazon/Meta/Microsoft/Salesforce/Stripe. Q2 FY26 guide: revenue high-20s%, only ~0.5pt of FX tailwind.
6 May
Margin / pricingJPM (desk)▼ bear
JPM TMT desk recurring "AI Token Costs Eating Internet Profits Alive" (SHOP/SPOT/META/PINS): models ~$9M hit to Subscription COGS in Q1 (~120bps of Sub GM / ~30bps of total GM), with a 2026 y/y headwind of ~160bps/~40bps to Sub/Total GM.
6 May
Valuation / positionDB · Bhavin Shah▲ bull
DB (Bhavin Shah): "strong 1Q presents a buying opportunity".
13 May
Competition / shareMorgan Stanley (PYPL note)◆ mixed
MS PYPL bear note cites "Apple/Shopify/agentic" as a competitive-intensity vector in payments — the same agentic shift cuts both ways.
20 May
Demand / capexMorgan Stanley · TMT Spec-Sales▲ bull
MS TMT Spec-Sales: agentic commerce = "big getting bigger" — GOOGL on data/protocol, AMZN on logistics, SHOP on inventory.
28 May
Guidance / numbersRothschild · Raj Chaudhury▼ bear
Rothschild (Chaudhury, Buy but cautious): Q2 ok but Q3 guide "just enough"; bears gain ammunition intra-quarter; H2 comps ~400bps harder; Q2 guide implies ~29-30% cc vs 30% in Q1; short interest rising; competitive pressure from Stripe + Meta + agentic ecommerce.
29 May
Demand / capexMorgan Stanley · AlphaWise▲ bull
MS AlphaWise: POS adoption ~2x y/y, merchants now run ~4 Shopify services (was ~3); "XYZ/SHOP/Stripe momentum".
9 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Smilek/Anmuth▲ bull
JPM (Smilek/Anmuth): Overweight, PT $150 — 3-year GMV/GP CAGR 20%+, FCF ~30%, OpInc 30%+; positive Platinum-partner channel check — "low META disruption risk", enterprise bullish, Sidekick strong, demand solid.
12 Jun
Product / roadmapJPM · Smilek/Anmuth▲ bull
JPM: Spring 2026 Editions — 150+ updates, AI Catalog ~2x conversions vs scraped data, Shop Pay expanding to non-SHOP brands (250M+ shoppers). OW PT $150 reiterated.
18 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · 'Agentic World: AMZN/WMT Deep Dive'◆ mixed
JPM (Outlook): agentic-commerce debate — AI agents as TAM-expander vs disintermediation by LLM storefronts.
19 Jun
Valuation / positionRothschild · TMT Daily– neutral
Rothschild TMT Daily (Outlook): SHOP in focus (with AMZN/META).
22 Jun
Margin / pricingUBS · Chiodo▲ bull
UBS (Chiodo, Outlook): 'Shopify: Shop Campaigns Overview & Analysis' — ~20-page deep dive into the Shop Campaigns ads product as an incremental monetization leg, with an illustrative framework to quantify the potential take-rate/attach upside; no specific dollar/GM figures disclosed.

DELLDell Technologies Inc.thinQ1 FY27 · May 28 → Jun 16, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23

events

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

28 May
PRINT — earningsDell · earnings call Q1 FY27
The largest beat in Dell's public history. Revenue $43.8B (+88% y/y); GAAP op income $3.66B vs $1.17B; op margin +330bp; EPS ~$4.86 vs ~$3.00 Street / ~$3.50 buy-side whisper. AI-optimized servers $16.1B vs $1.9B PY (+757%); traditional servers & networking $8.5B (+92%) — the surprise, driven by agentic inference demand. AI-server orders $24.4B; backlog $53.1B; 5,000+ AI-factory customers. Guide raised: FY27 revenue ~$140B→$168B; AI-server $50B→$60B; EPS $12.9→$17.9. The 2H step-down is supply, not demand; memory = constraint #1.
29 May
Demand / capexUBS · Dave Vogt (callback)◆ mixed
UBS (post-print callback): total revenue $43.8B, ~$8.5B / 24% above UBS's estimate. AI server $16.1B seen as "least controversial / sandbagged". The surprise was core/traditional server +92% y/y with UNIT growth, not just price. A large/growing share of orders is now Vera Rubin; backlog $51B exiting the quarter. CSG at 8% op margin "NOT sustainable" (normalizes to 5-6%). UBS stays Neutral on valuation — Dell trades near a market multiple / premium to NVDA/Broadcom on a business with declining gross margin.
29 May
Valuation / positionBernstein▲ bull
Bernstein: PT raised to $500 post-Q1 (from $175 at the Outperform initiation on 2025-09-15).
29 May
Valuation / positionBofA · Wamsi Mohan▲ bull
BofA (Wamsi Mohan): Buy, PT $500, FY27E EPS ~$19.
29 May
Demand / capexJPM · Samik Chatterjee▲ bull
JPM hosted a call with CFO David Kennedy + ISG pres. Arthur Lewis post-earnings: AI-server orders $24.4B, backlog $53.1B, >$100B in cumulative orders, 5,000+ AI-factory customers. Dell expects to beat a 2H PC market of ~-18% and gain share. JPM Overweight.
8 Jun
Margin / pricingBofA · Wamsi Mohan▲ bull
BofA conference wrap: Dell raising prices to defend GM amid memory inflation and *winning* on supply.
15 Jun
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Erik Woodring◆ mixed
MS (Woodring) servers note: bull = $53B AI backlog / $60B FY27 guide; MS bear = thin AI-server margins (ISG 11.7% vs 12.8%) + traditional-server pull-forward. Concedes "numbers for Dell are too low this year" but flags duration risk — if order unit growth turns neutral/negative, the market re-rates on peak-earnings fear. Prefers to play the on-prem-compute theme via distributors/VARs.

HPEHewlett Packard EnterprisemediumQ2 FY26 · May 29 → Jun 9, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
8
events
3
bull reads
4
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

29 May
Demand / capexUBS · Dave Vogt▼ bear
UBS (read-across from the Dell print, pre-HPE print): Dell's blowout may NOT be a clean read for HPE. From the mid-April meetings, HPE did not show the same level of demand strength in AI server and traditional server as Dell; it signaled agentic demand for core server only tentatively. HPE was also working on its cost structure given higher input costs. UBS "not entirely convinced" that HPE would follow Dell.
1 Jun
PRINT — earningsHPE · earnings call Q2 FY26
Blowout print; stock +25-37% on the day. Revenue $10.68B (+40% y/y, ~+9% beat); non-GAAP EPS $0.79 (+108% y/y); non-GAAP GM 36.9%; FCF $915M. Cloud & AI $7.7B (+22.9%) — Server $5.45B (+32.7%), ASP-driven by memory/SSD pass-through. Networking $2.69B (+148% reported / ~10% normalized), Juniper-driven, op margin 21.6%. AI bookings $1.8B in-quarter, cumulative $16.4B; backlog $5.9B. Guide raised hard: FY26 EPS $3.35-$3.45; FY28 targets pulled two years forward to FY26. Neri: "We have not seen any pull in. We do not see a cliff."
2 Jun
Valuation / positionGoldman Sachs · K. Murphy▲ bull
GS (K. Murphy): PT upgrade to $79 Buy (from $32), FY27/28/29E EPS +41% on average, on "server pricing inflection + Juniper synergy".
2 Jun
Valuation / positionJPM · Chatterjee▲ bull
JPM (Chatterjee): Overweight, PT $68 — "hits $3 FY28 target in FY26… multi-segment demand inflection".
2 Jun
Margin / pricingMorgan Stanley · Woodring◆ mixed
MS (Woodring): kept Equal-Weight even after raising PT to $71 — "server pricing capture > DELL; durability key debate". The beat is mostly memory/SSD ASP pass-through, not unit demand.
2 Jun
Valuation / positionBernstein · Newman▼ bear
Bernstein (Newman): Market Perform $62 (from $35), "still favors DELL over HPE".
2 Jun
Valuation / positionSIG · Hosseini▼ bear
SIG (Hosseini): only Neutral despite raising PT to $65 (from $21).
2 Jun
Valuation / positionUBS · Vogt▼ bear
UBS (Vogt): stayed Neutral post-print.
9 Jun
Demand / capexBarclays · Tim Long▲ bull
Barclays (Tim Long): DELL+HPE "compelling AI and Enterprise stories… navigated commodity costs well… still see upward bias to estimates even after large moves", with HPE better positioned in enterprise networking.

Margin / EPSEPS $0.44; revenue +18% YoYRecord GM 36%; EPS $0.62; FY25 $1.94GM 36.6%; record EPS $0.65GM 36.9% (+810bps); EPS $0.79, +108% YoY

SMCISuper Micro Computer, Inc. (Supermicro)mediumQ3 FY26 · May 05 → May 26, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
9
events
2
bull reads
4
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

5 May
PRINT — earningsSMCI (earnings) · management
Q3 FY26 (quarter ended Mar-31-2026): revenue US$10.243B, +123% y/y but -19% q/q from the record US$12.7B; missed the Street (~US$12.3-12.4B) on customer site-readiness delays + supply constraints. Non-GAAP GM 10.1% (vs the 6.4% trough in Q2); non-GAAP EPS US$0.84; NI US$483.4M.
5 May
Guidance / numbersSMCI (earnings) · management▼ bear
Q4 FY26 guidance: revenue US$11.0-12.5B, EPS US$0.65-0.79, GM guided back DOWN to 8.2-8.4% (mix / GB300 mega-rack ramp); FY26 cut to US$38.9-40.4B (below the prior 'at least US$40B').
6 May
Valuation / positionFinTwit · @ParadisLabs (via briefing)▲ bull
The contrarian long into the print worked: stock +11% AH despite the revenue miss, with the GM snap-back to 10.1% (from 6.4%) being the bull's proof that mix/expedite costs were transitory (briefing 2026-05-06).
6 May
Valuation / positionBofA · Ruplu▼ bear
BofA (Ruplu) cut the PO on 'revenue pushout, margins sequentially light' (briefing 2026-05-06).
6 May
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Chatterjee◆ mixed
Morgan Stanley (Chatterjee) maintained, with the framing 'GM Recovery Overshadowed by Corp Gov' — the margin recovery overshadowed by the governance overhang (briefing 2026-05-06).
7 May
Valuation / positionJefferies · Jeff Pu◆ mixed
Jefferies (Jeff Pu) Hold, PT US$43 (briefing 2026-05-07).
8 May
Competition / shareCNBC · Jim Cramer (via briefing)▼ bear
Cramer: 'Super Micro thorn in NVDA's side… get away from this morass', favoring DELL — the bear case in one line; value accrues upstream to NVIDIA (briefing 2026-05-08).
11 May
Capital / M&ASMCI · 10-Q Q3 FY26▼ bear
Balance sheet deteriorated: cash US$1.29B (from US$5.17B in Jun-2025); net debt ~US$7.5B (from US$787M q/q); operating cash flow ≈ -US$6.6B (≈US$10B of A/P payments); inventory US$11.1B; cash conversion cycle 106 days (from 54) (10-Q).
12 May
Competition / shareSMCI · 10-Q (via briefing)◆ mixed
Customer concentration: one large data-center operator 27% (≈US$2.8B, from 62.6%/~US$7.9B in Q2) + one enterprise at 10%. Vik Malyala named CBO (briefing 2026-05-12).
26 May
Demand / capexJPM · Networking conf recap▲ bull
At the JPM conference, Supermicro 'sounded good about demand visibility', highlighting a very large TAM, with DCBBS mix improving and giving more margin visibility. Backdrop: JPM raised its cloud-capex model to ~+80% y/y (2026)/+50% (2027) after a record 8 GW of DC power capacity added in 1Q26 (JPM 'Networking recap', 2026-05-26).

26 MayreportJPM — Networking conf recap
CRWVCoreWeave, Inc.richQ1 FY26 · May 07 → Jun 22, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
12
events
5
bull reads
5
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

7 May
PRINT — earningsCRWV (earnings) · management
Q1 FY2026: revenue US$2.1B, +112% y/y / +32% q/q; Adjusted EBITDA US$1.2B (~56% margin); Adjusted Operating Income only US$21M (~1% margin); net loss US$(740)M with interest expense of US$536M. Backlog US$99.4B (+~50% q/q, ~4x y/y), US$40B+ in new bookings, 10 customers with US$1B+. Capex guide raised to US$31-35B FY2026. FY26 revenue reaffirmed US$12-13B.
8 May
Guidance / numbersBernstein · Rezaei/Madison▼ bear
Bernstein Underperform, PT US$67 — the FY26 AOI guide of US$900M-1.1B is 'nearly unhittable per the math': it needs fit-out <8 weeks AND stabilized margins >21%; a blue-sky 6-week + 25% scenario only reaches the top of the range (briefings 2026-05-08/12/16).
16 May
Demand / capexFinTwit · @TheTranscript_ (via briefing)▲ bull
CFO: 'largely sold out of 2026 capacity with prices increasing across the board from Ampere to Hopper to Blackwell... extending into 2027'. NVDA's 13F holds 47.21M CRWV shares — a public endorsement from the ecosystem (briefing 2026-05-16).
1 Jun
Demand / capexJefferies · Brent Thill (desk)▲ bull
Desk color: 'CoreWeave absolutely crushing… they're sold out'; one of the top-3 AI companies told the desk 'we need tokens and can't find enough… we cannot find enough infrastructure' — corroborates the sold-out/demand-exceeds-supply thesis (Jefferies 'Brent Thill AI', 2026-06-01).
3 Jun
Capital / M&ARothschild (via briefing)▼ bear
Heavy fixed debt payments are a structural disadvantage vs equity-funded hyperscalers — 'Short: ORCL/CoreWeave encumbered by fixed debt payments vs GOOG's now-equity-funded flexibility' (briefing 2026-06-03).
4 Jun
Supply / capacityFinTwit · @SemiAnalysis_ (via briefing)▼ bear
NVDA Vera Rubin / MI455 racks at CoreWeave are 'still Engineering/Quality Samples — 800G OSFP cages not even populated' → near-term delivery/ramp risk (briefings 2026-06-04/05).
8 Jun
Capital / M&AWells Fargo · Fluidstack call◆ mixed
Neocloud read-through (FluidStack CEO): financing cost is the swing variable — 'if you're financing it at 6%, ship it; at high-teens, I'm less excited' (Oracle/CoreWeave brought financing in-house). The Anthropic inflection in Q1 drove the sector's supply-demand imbalance; inference economics approaching dollar-per-hour human output sustain demand durability (Wells Fargo 'Fluidstack call', 2026-06-08).
9 Jun
Product / roadmapSemiAnalysis · 'DeepSeek V4 Day 0'▲ bull
When SemiAnalysis's GB300 cluster went down at the DeepSeek V4 launch, CoreWeave contributed two spare GB300 NVL72 racks to the open-source effort — incidental confirmation that CRWV's GB300 NVL72 fleet is operational and in use (SemiAnalysis 'DeepSeek V4 Day 0→43', 2026-06-09).
11 Jun
Valuation / positionbriefing 2026-06-11▼ bear
Lockup overhang flagged: 9.2M shares registered for Jane Street's US$1B equity investment, lockup expires July 15 — potential supply overhang (briefing 2026-06-11).
13 Jun
Capital / M&AFinTwit · @The_AI_Investor (via briefing)▲ bull
NVDA made an additional US$2B strategic investment (after the US$2B in Jan-2026 at US$87.20). Added to the Nasdaq-100 in June 2026 → passive flow (briefings 2026-06-13/17).
16 Jun
Capital / M&AJPM · 'AI Capex 2.0'◆ mixed
JPM names CRWV as the proof-of-concept for public GPU financing ('with the exception of term loans and bonds for neocloud CoreWeave'). Two reads: (1) the rebounding neocloud H100 rental-price index is a 'very positive indicator' against draconian obsolescence assumptions — supportive of the ~6-year schedule; (2) but a structural duration mismatch (the market wants 10yr+ vs ~3-7yr GPU economic life) and the largest LevLoan tranche is only ~US$13bn (JPM 'AI Capex 2.0', 2026-06-16).
18 Jun
Valuation / positionCantor · 'CoreWeave call'▲ bull
Cantor models CRWV ending FY25 at ~US$17B ARR → US$30B FY26, with ~75% of the 2027 >US$30B guide already contracted; sees a large RPO beat in 2Q (consensus US$104B; Cantor ~US$125-130B) plus a likely new big deal ('going to guess Anthropic'). 2027/28 up 15-20%; run-rate EBITDA could be ~US$25-26B in '27 (vs US$18B). GPU useful life: consensus moved from 3-4yr to 7-8yr, Cantor thinks ~10yr (Cantor 'CoreWeave call', 2026-06-18).
22 Jun
Valuation / positionBernstein · 'Cloud in the Quarter'▼ bear
Bernstein reiterates Underperform, TP US$67 in its 1Q26 hyperscaler review — CRWV is 'much smaller than any of the other cloud providers', with no 'structural right-to-win' and a structural disadvantage vs hyperscalers over the long term. Framed as an 'interim capacity provider'; 1Q26 adjusted operating margin only ~0.5%. The US$8.5B DDTL 4.0 (first IG-rated, ~7%, backed solely by Meta's take-or-pay) is viewed as a bespoke 'proof of concept', not a repeatable baseline (Bernstein 'Cloud in the Quarter', 2026-06-22).

now · 2026-06-18 · Cantor "CoreWeave call"

2025-12Hedgeye: like-for-like neocloud GPU pricing fell for first time in 3 months — leading indicator of softer contract pricing · Hedgeye "Neocloud Winter" short

NBISNebius Group N.V.mediumQ1 2026 · May 11 → Jun 18, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
7
events
5
bull reads
0

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

11 May
Valuation / positionBofA▲ bull
BofA Buy, PO US$205 (PO raised, reit.; 1Q26 preview) — thesis: DC site activations supporting the 2H26 ramp, capex intensity (briefing 2026-05-11).
13 May
PRINT — earningsNBIS (earnings) · management
Q1 2026 beat-and-raise: group revenue US$399m (+684% y/y, +75% q/q); Nebius core US$390m (~98% of group, +841% y/y). ARR ~US$1.9bn exit (from ~US$1.25bn q/q). Group Adj. EBITDA US$130m (32% margin) vs US$15m in Q4'25; core margin 45% (from 24%). Net income US$621m flattered by a non-cash ClickHouse revaluation gain. Cash US$9.3bn.
13 May
Guidance / numbersNBIS (earnings) · CFO (via briefing)▲ bull
2026 guide: group revenue US$3.0-3.4bn, ARR US$7-9bn exit, adj. EBITDA margin ~40%, capex raised to US$20-25bn (from US$16-20bn in Q4) 'in response to pre-committed customer demand for 2027 capacity'. The Meta US$27bn/5yr and Microsoft (ramp H2 2026) contracts are structured to unlock ABF (briefing 2026-05-13).
14 May
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · Baer◆ mixed
Morgan Stanley (Baer) Equal-Weight, PT US$126→US$144 post-Q1 (rev US$399m, ARR US$1.92B, capex US$20-25B; briefing 2026-05-14).
14 May
Demand / capexNBIS (earnings, via briefing)▲ bull
Fully sold out — '4 or more customers competing for every GPU we bring online' (Q1 call via briefing 2026-05-14).
1 Jun
Supply / capacityFinTwit · @paradislabs (via briefing)▲ bull
NVIDIA validation: 'Nebius will take care of you' (Jensen, via @paradislabs briefing 2026-06-01). NVDA US$2bn stake + 'Exemplar Cloud' GB300 status + Vera Rubin/Vera CPU access.
8 Jun
Capital / M&AWells Fargo · Fluidstack call◆ mixed
Sector read-through for NBIS: financing cost is the swing variable (6% 'ship it' vs high-teens 'less excited'), the Anthropic inflection in Q1 drove the supply-demand imbalance, and inference economics approaching dollar-per-hour human output sustain demand durability. Supports the sold-out/contracted-ramp thesis but reinforces the dependence on capital access (Wells Fargo 'Fluidstack call', 2026-06-08).
18 Jun
Valuation / positionCantor · 'CoreWeave call'▲ bull
Cantor uses NBIS as a bull comp for CRWV — 'Nebius and CoreWeave printed the same exact quarter in 1Q', both reaffirmed 2026 sold-out and raised capex, yet NBIS is up ~80% since and overtook CoreWeave in market cap despite less deployed IT capacity. The re-rating reflects the perception that NBIS has a lot of unsold capacity amid rising GPU prices; signals that most of NBIS's revenue still comes from long-term capacity-reservation contracts (Cantor 'CoreWeave call', 2026-06-18).

Q1'263.5+ GW contracted, targeting 4+ GW YE2026; >75% owned; PA site 250–300 MW by end-2027, 1.2 GW by 2030 · Q1'26 call (2026-05-13)
↳ Owned capacity is the scarce contractable asset; PA/AL/MO sites push into 2027.

Q1'26Meta $27bn/5yr ($12bn dedicated from early-2027 + $15bn discretionary option); MSFT ramps H2'26 · Q1'26 call (2026-05-13)

group 32%; core 45%; FY26 target ~40% (nonlinear quarterly progression)

CEREBRASCerebras Systemsmediumintra-quarter (recent IPO) · May 06 → Jun 22, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
5
events
2
bull reads
0
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

6 May
Demand / capexUBS · Tim Archer (IPO-education call)▲ bull
UBS IPO-education call: OpenAI deal ~US$25B / 750MW take-or-pay across three ~US$8B tranches, initially all-cloud; UBS models cloud → 85-90% of revenue and OpenAI → 70-80% by 2028. Powers the real-time Codex-Spark model (>1,000 tokens/sec). The only vertical chip-to-cloud play in AI; >10x faster than NVIDIA. Model: revenue ~+60% in 2026, ~US$2.7B in 2027, ~US$7B in 2028; FCF-positive 2028. Framework ~11-12x EV/sales 2027 (pre-upsize).
6 May
Competition / shareBofA · Vivek Arya ('AMD recap')◆ mixed
Cerebras is 'very similar to what Groq does' — one of three fast-inference players (Cerebras/Groq/D-Matrix), worth ~10-20% incremental to the market; the SRAM dependence limits model size, optimizing for speed not throughput → a niche. AMD has an investment in Cerebras (Andrew Feldman sold SeaMicro to AMD) and Vivek would 'not be surprised to see them collaborate' (BofA 'AMD recap', 2026-05-06).
13 May
Capital / M&ACBRS (IPO pricing) · web/management– neutral
IPO priced at US$185/share on 2026-05-13 (above the raised range of US$150-160, itself up from US$115-125); first trade 2026-05-14 on the Nasdaq as CBRS; ~US$5.55B raised, ~US$56B fully-diluted at pricing; closed the first day +68% at ~US$331 (~US$95B market cap). Underwriters: Morgan Stanley, Citi, Barclays, UBS (CNBC/Yahoo Finance 2026-05-13/14).
15 Jun
Supply / capacityUBS · Arcuri ('Arcuri call')◆ mixed
Arcuri: 'it's not a demand question, it's a supply question' — Cerebras finalized its wafer order with TSMC in February counting only on OpenAI in the near term, so he is 'pretty confident they were not able to' accommodate AWS, making meaningful AWS capacity unlikely before 2027 (UBS models AWS only from 2028) (UBS 'Arcuri call', 2026-06-15).
22 Jun
Valuation / positionMorgan Stanley · 'Memory + semicap'▲ bull
Morgan Stanley initiated Overweight ahead of the first print, with a strong tailwind — high-speed token prices have risen sharply (a token 'twice as fast costs you about 10 to 12 times as much'); low-latency providers saw ~10x price growth. Cerebras is 'in the pole position' on a unique architecture. On the knock that wafer-scale 'doesn't scale for large models', MS notes that Cerebras ran the trillion-parameter Kimmy model with benchmarks; the first call is the chance to rebut that publicly (MS 'Memory + semicap', 2026-06-22).

2026-05-06UBS: profitability in 2H27, FCF-positive 2028; long-term low-60s GM / low-40s op margin · UBS IPO-education call (Tim Archer)

Token-price / market-size tailwindstructural↑ rising
