AAPLApple Inc.richQ2 FY26 · Apr 30 → Jun 23, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
10
events
4
bull reads
2
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

30 Apr
PRINT — earningsAAPL (earnings) · management
Q2 FY26 (March quarter): revenue US$111.2bn, +17% y/y (March record, above the top of the guide despite supply constraints). EPS US$2.10, +22%. iPhone US$57bn (+22%); Services record US$31bn (+16%); company GM 49.3%. Greater China +28% y/y. The board added US$100bn of buyback and dropped the net-cash-neutral target. June-qtr guidance: revenue +14% to +17%, GM 47.5-48.5%; memory is the swing factor, with growing impact beyond June.
6 May
Margin / pricingBofA · Wigg (spec-sales)▲ bull
BofA/Wigg impressed by Apple guiding margins so strongly in the worst stretch of memory cost; reads the Google 'Siri brain transplant' as better than forcing in-house models, with the fall foldable (US$2-3K) a big ASP tailwind that needs few units to move EPS toward ~US$10-12; the multiple holds on Apple's FCF advantage vs the rest of the capex-heavy Mag7 (BofA/Wigg, 2026-05-06).
12 May
Capital / M&AApple IR · Parekh (equity call)▲ bull
IR call: the board added US$100bn of buyback and dropped the net-cash-neutral target (Parekh: now evaluating cash and debt independently — the framework is 'no longer relevant'). The June constraint shifts to Mac (mini, Studio, MacBook Neo) on advanced 3nm node capacity, not memory (Apple IR, 2026-05-12).
4 Jun
Valuation / positionBarclays (desk, via briefing)▼ bear
AAPL used as a low-vol funding short vs semis by the Barclays desk (briefings 2026-05-14/06-08).
9 Jun
Product / roadmapBarclays · Tim Long (via briefing)▼ bear
Barclays (Tim Long) Underweight, PT US$253 — AI is 'another AI attempt', no killer app, questionable monetization, ~16% downside. Siri 2.0 is 'catch-up, not a breakthrough' (briefing 2026-06-09).
9 Jun
Demand / capexVital Knowledge / Morgan Stanley (via briefing)◆ mixed
AI gated by the installed base: ~850m iPhones don't run Apple Intelligence, ~1.3bn don't run advanced Siri; a more intensive Siri needs iPhone 17 Pro/Air+ (~150m of a ~1.5bn base, Woodring). Apple's capex/revenue fell ~3%→2% (Q1'20→Q1'26) vs 40-60% for peers (Vital Knowledge, 2026-06-09).
15 Jun
Demand / capexCiti/Counterpoint · WWDC & smartphone◆ mixed
Counterpoint: sell-through -5% y/y in Q1 (Apple's first negative since Q4'23) and -10% y/y for the total market in April; global FY26 shipments revised to -14%, but Apple seen ~flat. The iPhone 18 base shifts to ~Mar/2027, making 2H26 lighter. WWDC: Siri got its own app with Google/Gemini grounding knowledge. Foldable ~6M units in 2026, September launch, ASP just under US$1,000 (Citi/Counterpoint WWDC, 2026-06-15).
16 Jun
Product / roadmapMorgan Stanley · Woodring (equity call)▲ bull
Woodring (MS) frames FY27 as an 'iPhone X-like cycle saved by pricing' — ~20m foldable units, US$2,400 starting price, ~10pts of ASP growth from the foldable mix alone; MS Overweight PT US$360, FY27E EPS US$10.30 (~8% above consensus). But the bear: Siri 2.0 is '2024 right now, not 2026' and NAND going 'parabolic' (MS, 2026-06-16).
18 Jun
Margin / pricingBofA · Mohan▲ bull
BofA (Mohan) reiterates Buy, PT US$380 (37x C27 EPS) — margin holds via cost pass-through despite memory; trimming units marginally. Cook confirmed 'forced to raise pricing' in the WSJ (BofA, 2026-06-18). Last referenced price US$295.95.
23 Jun
Margin / pricingStoneX/TrendForce · 'Memory expert call (Avril)'◆ mixed
Apple has already agreed to a ~+20% increase in mobile DRAM pricing in Q3 — among the few 'special deals' above the Q3 base of ~8-13% — and is in the first LTA wave alongside US CSPs and NVIDIA (3-yr contracts 2026-28). Avril reads Apple's 'vocal' memory-cost complaints as Apple not getting all the LPDDR5 output it wants (fighting NVIDIA) and not just a pricing story (StoneX/TrendForce, 2026-06-23).
23 Jun
Margin / pricingRedburn · Conor O'Mara ('Asia Trip Takeaways')◆ mixed
Conor corroborates the Fubon read — TSMC raised Apple's wafer price only low-single-digit in 2026, then +≥10% in 2027 (the first time Apple is included in the hike) — a forward COGS headwind in the A-series/foldable cycle (Redburn 'Conor O'Mara Asia Trip Takeaways', 2026-06-23).

NFLXNetflix, Inc.thinQ1 2026 · Apr 15 → Jun 04, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
5
events
3
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

15 Apr
Guidance / numbersJPM · Anmuth (internet 1Q preview)▲ bull
JPM framed NFLX as a 'cleaner story' top-5 internet name — double-digit revenue + ~2pts/yr of margin expansion + an ads ramp, without the AI-heavy capex or the AI-disintermediation risk that weighs on most internet names. The debate is whether NFLX raises its FY26 op-margin guide (>50bps unlikely) and revenue on the print; the must-do was increasing the buyback to dispel fears of another large M&A deal post-Warner (JPM/Anmuth, 2026-04-15).
16 Apr
PRINT — earningsNFLX (earnings) · management
Q1 2026: guidance maintained, all organic. FY26 revenue growth 12-14% (~US$51B, +14% y/y), operating margin 31.5% (+~2pts y/y), ads business 'roughly doubling' to ~US$3B. Engagement: Q1 view-hours grew at a similar rate to 2H25; advertiser base +70% y/y in 2025 to >4,000; programmatic 'on its way to >50% of non-live ads'. The World Baseball Classic in Japan = the most-watched program ever in Japan (31.4M). Closed InterPositive (GenAI filmmaking).
16 Apr
Competition / shareGoldman / Sensor Tower (equity call)▲ bull
NFLX still by far the largest streaming platform with 52% of global streaming MAUs (Goldman/Sensor Tower call, 2026-04-16).
24 Apr
Valuation / positionStratechery · Eric Seufert◆ mixed
Netflix is 'the baseline, must-have, default streaming service', a source of pricing power no one else has; the ad tier matters less for its own ad revenue and more because it unlocks the high-end. Critical on ads execution: NFLX is 'not very aggressive', only launched proprietary ad tech in the US in April 2025. Cites a leaked pre-earnings review: 2030 goal of ~US$9B in ad revenue (vs ~US$2.15B in 2025, a ~4x ramp) — and notes that US$2.15B is 'less than Pinterest' given the user base (Stratechery/Seufert, 2026-04-24).
14 May
Valuation / positionJPMorgan · Anmuth (via briefing)▲ bull
JPMorgan/Anmuth reiterates Overweight, PT US$118, citing ad MAUs of 250M+ and +5 NFL games in 2026 (briefing 2026-05-14).
4 Jun
Valuation / positionBernstein (via briefing)▼ bear
Bernstein flagged that NFLX 'lacks N/T catalysts', keeping NFLX/DIS in the short camp for the summer with FIFA/NBA/F1 dominating attention (briefing 2026-06-04).

BKNGBooking Holdings, Inc.thinQ1 FY2026 · Apr 28 → Jun 18 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
5
events
3
bull reads
1
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

28 Apr
PRINT — earningsBooking Holdings · Fogel/Steenbergen (call)
Q1 FY2026 print: room nights 338M (+6% y/y, ~+8% ex-Middle East conflict, which cost ~2pts); gross bookings $53.8B (+15%); revenue $5.5B (+16%); adj. EBITDA ~$1.3B (+19%, above the top of the guide); adj. EPS $1.14 (+14%, post 25-for-1 split). The US accelerated for the 4th straight quarter to low-teens.
28 Apr
Demand / capexBooking Holdings · Fogel (call)▲ bull
Fogel argues that AI is a net positive, not a threat: incumbent scale plus proprietary intent data improve conversion, and travel discovery via LLMs expands the digital TAM ("35%... 45%... higher"). Responding to Justin Post (BofA), he stresses that frontier players are moving toward performance marketing/advertising, the Google-style auction model in which BKNG thrives.
28 Apr
Product / roadmapBooking Holdings · Fogel (call)▲ bull
Connected Trip +high-teens (~3x total transaction growth); air tickets +28%, attractions ~+25%. Penny (Priceline) is already booking accommodation/flights directly with an early conversion lift (small sample). Agoda cut its servicing cost per booking by double digits via AI automation.
28 Apr
Guidance / numbersBooking Holdings · Steenbergen (call)◆ mixed
Q2'26 guide: room nights +2-4% (conflict ~3pts headwind; underlying ~5-7%), GBV/revenue/EBITDA each +4-6%. FY26 lowered at the midpoint but the high end of GBV and adj. EPS held. Steenbergen: the assumption of impact through end-June with recovery in 2H is explicitly uncertain ("anyone can put whatever you want in your model").
28 Apr
Capital / M&ABooking Holdings · Fogel (call)▲ bull
Record $3.6B buyback in Q1 (+$343M dividend); share count down >40% since 2014 at an average price of ~$93. Cash/investments $16.5B.
28 Apr
Valuation / positionBofA · Justin Post (call Q&A)▼ bear
Justin Post (BofA) flagged that large AI players are "moving away from transactions," raising the structural risk of agentic disintermediation; BKNG's proof points are still from a very small sample.

UBERUber Technologies, Inc.richQ1 FY26 · May 6 → Jun 18, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
15
events
10
bull reads
3
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

6 May
PRINT — earningsUber · Dara Khosrowshahi (call)
Q1 FY26 print at/above the high end of guidance: gross bookings +21% y/y ("trip and audience led", audience +17%); Mobility GBs accelerated to +20% with record margins; Delivery +23% (grocery/retail); Freight returned to growth; non-GAAP EPS +44% (>2x bookings growth); record $3B buyback in the quarter.
6 May
Margin / pricingUber · mgmt (call)▲ bull
US Mobility acceleration thesis: insurance shifts from a multi-year deleverage item to a tailwind ("hundreds of millions" of savings in 2026, the first year of insurance leverage in US Mobility since COVID); price held flat -> elasticity -> trip acceleration, most visible in LA/CA.
6 May
Competition / shareBofA · Wigg (spec-sales)▲ bull
BofA (Wigg) frames the NVIDIA-UBER tie-up as a possible "sea change": the tripartite structure (NVIDIA supplies AV tech + OEM supplies the vehicle + Uber operates the fleet) makes UBER the AV winner regardless of which OEM/tech prevails; it would make sense for Waymo to license to Uber's dominant fleet rather than compete 1P.
7 May
Valuation / positionGS · Eric Sheridan (briefing)▲ bull
GS (Sheridan): Buy, PT cut from $125 to $115. Part of a cluster of constructive post-print ratings (JPM/Anmuth OW $110, Citi/Josey Buy $110, Bernstein/Devnani $110, Barclays/Sandler OW $107).
11 May
Margin / pricing@DanielTNiles (FinTwit)▼ bear
@DanielTNiles flags margin risk from AI token cost pass-through as agentic adoption scales.
12 May
Competition / shareBernstein (research)▼ bear
Bernstein: AMZN Now (30-min delivery) + Walmart/Subway delivery are incremental negatives for Eats.
18 May
Valuation / positionPershing Square / Ackman (positioning)▲ bull
Pershing/Ackman sized UBER at $2.25B (top-3 holding).
20 May
Demand / capexDB · Benjamin Black (briefing)▲ bull
DB (Black): Buy PT $108. Ads +50% y/y at a $2B+ run-rate; ~$10B of FCF; the NVDA-UBER tie-up is "one of the most important mile markers" of Uber's AV strategy, monetizing the car is the other half of the battle.
26 May
Capital / M&AJPM · Schilsky / Bernstein (briefing)▼ bear
Overhang from the Delivery Hero M&A (EUR33/$11.6B bid). JPM (Schilsky): "UBER shareholders will broadly not like this deal". Bernstein: pro-forma +24%/+10% GB/EBITDA in 2027 but ~7% dilutive to GAAP EPS, and it distracts from the AV ramp investors want.
3 Jun
Valuation / positionBofA · Fenske (SF Tech Conf)▲ bull
BofA (Fenske) at the SF Tech Conf: "short thesis is tired... a Zero Sum Thinking trade". (On the same day, @garrytan: "time to short Uber and long DoorDash".)
5 Jun
Valuation / positionJefferies · Colantuoni / JPM (briefing)◆ mixed
Jefferies (Colantuoni) names UBER as top pick for 2H26. JPM (Silver/Diebel) warns that "EC could bite" on the Delivery Hero M&A (antitrust overlap in Portugal/Spain/Sweden), preferring Talabat.
8 Jun
Demand / capexDB · Benjamin Black / BofA conference-wrap (equity call)▲ bull
DB (Black) reiterates Buy PT $108 at the BofA conference-wrap: demand-aggregation / AV-monetization thesis; stock ~$70.71-70.91 at the briefings (PTs imply ~45-55% upside).
12 Jun
Competition / shareBernstein · Seidman (briefing)▲ bull
Bernstein (Seidman): US Mobility GBs/volumes positioned to accelerate in 2026 despite Waymo's Premier subscription ("UBER One-like").
16 Jun
Demand / capexJPM · "AI Capex 2.0" (research)– neutral
JPM ("AI Capex 2.0") cites Uber (alongside Microsoft) as a company that "exhausted [its] full-year 2026 token budget within months" by putting agents into production, as evidence of the logarithmic step-up in agentic token usage.
17 Jun
Valuation / positionRothschild/Redburn · J. Goodall (briefing)▲ bull
Rothschild/Redburn (Goodall): Buy PT $112 (cut from $120) on AV TAM expansion, 35% EPS CAGR over 3 years, FY26-28 +1-3% vs consensus. Barclays (Ross) sees a higher offer likely on Delivery Hero but "many moving parts".
18 Jun
Product / roadmapBofA · Justin Post (briefing)▲ bull
BofA (Post): Maintain Buy, PO $104. Houston launch (Nuro/Lucid) + Zurich (WeRide) -> 5 notable AV launches in the US/EU in 2H26; successful launches "could change the narrative on AV supply competition".

SPOTSpotify Technology S.A.mediumQ1 FY26 · 28 Apr → 17 Jun 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
7
events
5
bull reads
2
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

28 Apr
PRINT — earningsSpotify · mgmt (prepared remarks)
Q1 FY26 print: MAU 761M (+10M q/q, +12% y/y, accelerating); Subs 293M (+3M, in line, clean churn after the January US price hike); revenue EUR4.5B (+14%); Premium ~+15% (ARPU +5.7%); Ad-Supported only ~+3%; gross margin 33.0% (+~133bp, 2nd-highest ever); OI EUR715M (but EUR49M of the beat was non-operating Social Charges); FCF EUR824M; $361M of buybacks.
8 May
Margin / pricingMS (briefing)▼ bear
MS flagged a -15%+ selloff on concerns over AI inference cost (OI guided down q/q in Q2 on reinvestment in AI inference + marketing).
21 May
Demand / capexBofA · Reif Ehrlich / JPM/Anmuth (Investor Day)▲ bull
Investor Day repositioned the growth thesis from user growth to monetization and de-risked the AI narrative. JPM/Anmuth: OW, Top Pick, PT $650 (from $600). BofA/Reif Ehrlich: Buy PT $685, Remix add-on as a "clear answer to AI disintermediation". LT targets: 35-40% GM, 20%+ op margin by 2030.
21 May
Product / roadmapDB · Black (briefing)▲ bull
DB (Black): Buy $620 - the AI remix partnership with UMG "removes the bear narrative".
22 May
Valuation / positionJPM · Schilsky (spec-sales)▲ bull
JPM spec-sales (Schilsky): "I genuinely have no idea what the Bear Case is anymore. Buy SPOT."
9 Jun
Product / roadmapBernstein · Moore (briefing)▲ bull
Bernstein (Moore): speculative-positive that a SPOT AI licensing deal is imminent; UMG superfan framework.
11 Jun
Product / roadmapBernstein · Seidman (briefing)▲ bull
Bernstein (Seidman): speculative-positive that a SPOT AI licensing deal is imminent.
17 Jun
Valuation / positionRedburn/Rothschild · Ed Vyvyan (briefing)▼ bear
Redburn/Rothschild (Vyvyan): Neutral, PT cut from $600 to $550 - the lone Neutral and the freshest datapoint. "We do not see a meaningful mass-market opportunity for AI derivative tools" and expects Remix contributing <2% of Premium revenues in 2030e; SPOT still searching for its killer differentiator, perhaps via M&A in live music. Models revenue -1%, EBIT -2-3% vs Street.

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

8 May
PRINT — earningsToyota · FY26Q4 deck (6-K)
FY26 results (12m to Mar/26): OI JPY3.766tn (-JPY1.029tn YoY, margin 7.4% from 10.0%); NI attributable JPY3.848tn (margin 7.6%); consolidated sales 9,595k vehicles (+2.5%), revenue JPY50.7tn. US tariffs were a -JPY1.38tn hit to FY26 OI, with North America swinging to an operating loss (-JPY298.6bn, margin -1.4%); company says it has "secured profits consistent with guidance."
8 May
Guidance / numbersToyota · FY26Q4 deck (6-K)▼ bear
FY27 guidance: revenue JPY51.0tn (+0.6%), OI JPY3.0tn (-JPY766bn, margin 5.9%), NI JPY3.0tn, volume 9,600k (+0.1%). The decline reflects a new Middle East impact (-JPY400bn), labor/depreciation/R&D, and the Hino deconsolidation (-JPY40bn OI). FX assumption JPY150/US$, JPY180/EUR.
8 May
Product / roadmapToyota · FY26Q4 deck (6-K)◆ mixed
HEV remains the profit pillar: FY26 electrified retail of 5,040k units (48.1% of the Toyota+Lexus mix), of which HEV 4,620k (+4.4%), BEV 243k (+68%). BEV guided to 598k retail (+146%) in FY27 — an acceleration, but off a low base and with a track record of cuts (FY26 cut from 308k to 277k).
8 May
Capital / M&AToyota · FY26Q4 deck (6-K)▲ bull
Shareholder returns: FY26 dividend JPY95 (+5), FY27 forecast JPY100 (+5); record buyback of JPY3.657tn in FY26 (largely tied to the Toyota Industries take-private via tender offer); total FY26 return JPY4.895tn. Stated 20% ROE target via the value chain (+JPY2.1tn of OI by 2030).
10 Jun
Competition / shareToyota · 20-F FY26▼ bear
The FY26 20-F frames the industry as "highly volatile" with competition intensifying via CASE; warns that competing in "electrified vehicles...will be fundamental" with no guarantees — risk of falling behind in markets with rapid BEV adoption. China equity-method profit ~flat (JPY108.2bn FY26) under intense local competition (BYD et al.).

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

1 May
PRINT — earningsCadence · 10-Q Q1 FY26
Q1 FY26 (10-Q): total revenue $1,474.2M (+18.7% y/y); GAAP income from operations $431.3M (29.3% margin); NI $335.7M; diluted EPS $1.23 (vs $1.00). Recurring/up-front mix 77%/23% (vs 82%/18% in Q1 FY25, skewed to up-front by Palladium/Protium hardware + IP timing). Backlog (RPO) $8.0B (up from $7.8B at FY25 year-end). China came back strong: $189.4M (+36% y/y, ~13% of revenue).
11 Jun
Product / roadmapfirstadopter / Tae Kim (briefing)▲ bull
CDNS signed for IP on Intel's 14A node — cited by BofA as a foundry-credibility signal in the INTC double-upgrade, and a reminder that Cadence earns collateral regardless of which foundry/customer wins the node war.

Forward commentary & how it evolved · anchored on the 2 most recent references

SNPSSynopsys, Inc.mediumQ2 FY26 · May 13 → Jun 3, 2026 · wiki synthesis 2026-06-23
6
events
2
bull reads
2
bear reads

Timeline intra-quarter — equity calls & mgmt commentaries

13 May
Valuation / positionCiti · Atif Malik (research)▲ bull
Citi (Malik): Buy, PT $610, the most bullish on the Street, on three legs - agentic AI structurally lifting the EDA growth rate, SNPS-ANSS revenue synergies ($400M first phase starting FY27), and IP monetization via royalty; sees IP +16% y/y in FY27 and the historical ~25% valuation discount vs CDNS narrowing toward the ~10% average.
20 May
Guidance / numbersMS · Lee Simpson (briefing)▼ bear
MS (Simpson): EW - FY26 is a "transition year" with muted Design IP growth (~-12% y/y in earlier reads); "2027 set-up yet to emerge," awaiting evidence of pricing leverage and acceleration in AI revenue ahead of the September Investor Day.
21 May
Competition / shareGS · Jim Schneider (briefing)▲ bull
GS (Schneider): Buy - SNPS + CDNS "uniquely insulated against AI disruption," with the proliferation of custom silicon (Vera CPU, Trainium, TPU - all of which need EDA) as a demand pull.
27 May
PRINT — earningsSynopsys · 10-Q Q2 FY26
Q2 FY26 (10-Q): GAAP revenue $2,276.0M (+42% y/y), first full quarter with Ansys in the base (Ansys contributed $652.4M); Design Automation $1,821.8M (+62%, inflated by Ansys); Design IP $454.2M (-6% y/y but +12% q/q, suggesting the bottom). Non-GAAP EPS $3.35 vs $3.14 cons (+7% beat). SNPS paid off the remaining $3.5B term loan in Q1 FY26, rapid deleveraging.
28 May
Guidance / numbersJPM · Harlan Sur / Citi / MS (briefing)◆ mixed
Post-print: FY26 guide raised - revenue ~$9.67B (from $9.61B), non-GAAP EPS midpoint $14.76, op margin +50bp to 41.0%; Q3 $2.41-2.46B (in-line). JPM (Sur) OW (guide still conservative, agentic AI structurally lifting EDA). MS raised PT to $525 (from $480) but maintains EW. FY27 EPS consensus ~$18.05.
29 May
Product / roadmap@skundojjala (FinTwit)– neutral
@skundojjala: mgmt assumes no IP upside from Intel 18A/14A tape-outs in FY26 - a watch item for the sequential recovery in Design IP.
3 Jun
Competition / shareIrrational Analysis (research)▼ bear
Irrational Analysis ("What's going on with Marvell?"): frames Synopsys as the baseline of licensable "stock IP" that internal/custom designs beat - Marvell's dense SRAM (500mV) and the CXL interface IP that Astera licenses are judged inferior to in-house IP; SNPS Design IP competes as the default merchant, not the performance leader, in the most demanding sockets.

build_timelines.py · source: wiki pages (_wiki\.md) — dated, attributed datapoints from equity calls, sell-side callbacks and management commentary. Lean (▲bull ▼bear ◆mixed –neutral) and theme are synthesis classifications; "thin" coverage = few dated datapoints on the page. Re-run after /run-inbox.
